standing-dumbbell-alternating-bicep-curl.description: Standing dumbbell alternating bicep curl targets the biceps by curling one arm at a time while standing upright.
steps: Stand tall with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand with palms facing forward and arms at your sides.
Keep elbows pinned to your sides and curl one dumbbell toward your shoulder by bending the elbow.
Lower the dumbbell slowly back to the starting position.
Repeat the curl with the opposite arm and continue alternating until the set is finished.
cues: Keep elbows tucked against your sides throughout
Move only at the elbow joint and avoid swinging
safetyNotes: Avoid using momentum or leaning back, which can strain the lower back.

bench-side-jumps.description: Bench side jumps are lateral jumps over a bench targeting lower body power and cardio endurance.
steps: Stand beside a stable bench with feet hip-width apart.
Jump sideways over the bench, swinging arms for momentum.
Land softly on the opposite side with knees bent.
Immediately jump back over to return to the start position.
Repeat side-to-side for the desired reps or duration.
cues: Keep chest up and core tight throughout.
Land on the balls of your feet to absorb impact.
safetyNotes: Use a stable bench that will not slide or tip.

band-pallof-press.description: The band pallof press is an anti-rotation core exercise that strengthens the abdominals by pressing a band away from the body while resisting twist.
steps: Anchor a band at chest height and stand sideways to it, gripping the band with both hands at your chest.
Step away from the anchor point until the band has light tension.
Brace your core and press both arms straight forward without letting your torso rotate.
Slowly return your hands to your chest and repeat for reps before switching sides.
cues: Keep ribs stacked over hips
Resist the band pulling you toward the anchor
safetyNotes: Maintain a neutral spine and avoid arching your lower back.

chest-supported-incline-row.description: Chest supported incline row builds back strength by pulling weights while lying face down on an incline bench for support.
steps: Set an incline bench to 30-45 degrees and lie face down with chest fully supported and feet on the floor.
Grab dumbbells or handles with palms facing each other and arms hanging straight down.
Pull the weights up toward your hips by driving elbows back and squeezing shoulder blades together.
Lower the weights slowly until arms are fully extended to return to the start position.
cues: Keep chest pressed firmly into the bench
Drive elbows back rather than shrugging shoulders
safetyNotes: Avoid using momentum or swinging to prevent strain on the lower back.

mb-pelvic-floor-heel-taps.description: A beginner core move that targets the abdominals and pelvic floor with slow alternating heel taps while lying on your back.
steps: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor, arms relaxed at your sides.
Gently engage your pelvic floor muscles.
Slowly slide one heel forward a few inches along the floor then return it.
Alternate the movement with the opposite heel while keeping the pelvic floor engaged.
Return both feet to the starting position once the set is complete.
cues: Keep your lower back pressed into the floor
Breathe steadily without holding your breath
safetyNotes: Stop if you feel any strain in the lower back or pelvis.

floor-dumbbell-skull-crusher.description: Lying on the floor, extend dumbbells from above the chest to target the triceps.
steps: Lie on your back on the floor with knees bent, holding a dumbbell in each hand above your chest with arms straight.
Bend elbows to lower the dumbbells toward the sides of your head with control.
Press the dumbbells back up by straightening your elbows without moving your upper arms.
Return to the start position after completing the set and set the weights down safely.
cues: Keep elbows tucked and pointed toward your feet.
Lower with control and stop before weights touch your head.
safetyNotes: Start with light weights to avoid losing control over your face.

lower-leg-lifts-with-block-feet-holds.description: Lower leg lifts with block feet holds target the lower abdominals by raising and holding the legs while feet stay secured on blocks.
steps: Lie on your back with feet resting on blocks and legs extended straight.
Engage your core and slowly lift the lower legs upward off the blocks.
Hold the top position for the set duration while keeping feet controlled.
Lower the legs slowly back down to rest on the blocks and return to start.
cues: Press lower back flat into the floor
Move legs slowly without momentum
safetyNotes: Avoid arching the lower back during the lift.

medicine-ball-dead-bugs.description: Medicine ball dead bugs build core stability by holding a ball overhead while extending opposite limbs from a lying position.
steps: Lie on your back with knees bent at 90 degrees, feet off the floor, and hold the medicine ball above your chest with both hands.
Press your lower back into the floor and slowly extend one arm overhead while straightening the opposite leg.
Return the arm and leg to the starting position with control.
Repeat the movement on the opposite side.
cues: Keep lower back pressed into the floor throughout
Move slowly and maintain control
safetyNotes: Avoid arching your lower back to reduce strain risk.

band-shuffle.description: Lateral shuffling steps with a resistance band around the ankles to activate glutes and warm up the lower body.
steps: Place a resistance band around your ankles and stand with feet hip-width apart.
Bend knees slightly and keep core engaged to stay stable.
Step sideways with one foot while keeping tension on the band, then bring the other foot to meet it.
Continue shuffling in one direction for the set reps or time, then switch directions and return to start.
cues: Keep constant tension on the band
Stay low with bent knees throughout
safetyNotes: Control each step to avoid twisting at the knees.

standing-dumbbell-alternating-hammer-cur.description: A standing dumbbell curl using neutral grip to target biceps and forearms, alternating arms each rep.
steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, arms at sides holding dumbbells with palms facing in.
Curl one dumbbell up toward your shoulder while keeping elbow close to your body.
Lower the dumbbell slowly back to the starting position.
Repeat with the opposite arm, alternating sides for all reps.
cues: Keep elbows tucked to your sides
Control the lowering phase without swinging
safetyNotes: Avoid leaning back or using momentum to lift the weights

stability-ball-knee-tucks.description: Stability ball knee tucks strengthen the core abdominals by rolling the knees toward the chest on an unstable surface.
steps: Lie on your back with arms at your sides and heels resting on the stability ball.
Brace your core and lift your hips to form a straight line from shoulders to heels.
Bend your knees to roll the ball inward until your knees tuck near your chest.
Slowly extend your legs to return to the straight-line starting position.
cues: Keep your core braced to avoid arching the lower back
Control the ball movement without letting it roll away
safetyNotes: Use a properly sized and inflated ball to maintain stability and prevent slipping.

stability-ball-dumbbell-bicep-curl.description: Seated on a stability ball, curl dumbbells to target biceps while challenging core balance and stability.
steps: Sit on the stability ball with feet flat on the floor and a dumbbell in each hand at your sides, palms forward.
Engage your core to sit tall with a neutral spine.
Curl both dumbbells toward your shoulders, keeping elbows pinned to your sides.
Lower the dumbbells slowly back to the starting position with control.
cues: Keep elbows tucked and upper arms still
Brace your core to avoid rocking on the ball
safetyNotes: Use a properly sized ball so feet are flat and knees at 90 degrees for stability

banded-standing-glute-kicks.description: Banded standing glute kicks use a resistance band to extend one leg back while standing, targeting the glutes.
steps: Loop a resistance band around both ankles and stand tall with feet hip-width apart.
Shift weight onto one leg and keep the other leg slightly bent.
Kick the free leg straight back while squeezing the glute at the top.
Slowly return the leg to the starting position without letting the band go slack.
cues: Keep hips square and level throughout.
Engage your core to avoid arching the lower back.
safetyNotes: Control the movement to maintain balance and prevent the band from slipping.

half-burpee.description: Half burpee is a bodyweight cardio exercise that builds full-body endurance by moving from standing to plank and back.
steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and arms at sides.
Bend knees, place hands on floor, and jump feet back to a high plank.
Jump feet forward to hands, then stand up to return to start.
cues: Brace your core to keep hips level in plank.
Land softly on the balls of your feet.
safetyNotes: Avoid letting hips sag or pike up in plank to protect your lower back.

medicine-ball-crunch.description: A weighted crunch using a medicine ball to strengthen the abdominal muscles.
steps: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat, holding the medicine ball to your chest.
Crunch upward by lifting shoulders off the floor while keeping the ball stable.
Pause briefly at the top.
Lower slowly back to the starting position.
cues: Keep lower back pressed into the floor
Exhale as you crunch up
safetyNotes: Avoid pulling on your neck with your hands.

bench-hops.description: Bench hops are lateral plyometric jumps over a bench to boost cardio and leg power in circuits.
steps: Stand beside a stable bench with feet hip-width apart.
Bend knees slightly and hop sideways over the bench.
Land softly on the other side with bent knees.
Immediately hop back over to the starting side.
Repeat for the target reps or duration.
cues: Swing arms for momentum and balance.
Land on the balls of your feet with soft knees.
safetyNotes: Confirm the bench is stable and won't tip.
Avoid locking knees on landing to protect joints.

floor-dumbbell-chest-press.description: Floor dumbbell chest press targets chest, shoulders, and triceps with a limited range of motion for joint safety.
steps: Lie on your back on the floor with knees bent and feet flat, holding a dumbbell in each hand at chest level.
Press the dumbbells straight up until arms are fully extended above your chest.
Lower the dumbbells slowly and with control back to the starting position at your chest.
cues: Keep lower back pressed into the floor
Tuck elbows at about 45 degrees from your body
safetyNotes: Avoid letting elbows flare outward to protect the shoulders

bench-glute-raises.description: Bench glute raises target the core abdominals by lifting the hips from a supported bench position.
steps: Lie on your back with upper back on the bench, knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Place hands across chest or on hips and brace your core.
Press through heels to lift hips toward the ceiling without arching the back.
Lower hips slowly and with control back to the starting position.
cues: Keep ribs stacked over hips
Drive through heels while bracing core
safetyNotes: Avoid hyperextending the lower back at the top to reduce strain risk.

kneeling-banded-glute-abduction.description: Kneeling exercise with a band around the thighs to target and strengthen the outer glutes.
steps: Kneel on all fours with a band around your thighs just above the knees, hands under shoulders and knees under hips.
Brace your core to keep your back flat and hips level.
Lift one knee out to the side against the band without rotating your torso.
Slowly lower the knee back to the starting position and repeat on the same side.
cues: Keep hips level and avoid twisting
Squeeze the glute at the top of the movement
safetyNotes: Stop if you feel sharp pain in the lower back or knees

stability-ball-dumbbell-hammer-preacher-.description: Hammer-grip dumbbell curls performed while leaning over a stability ball to isolate the biceps.
steps: Kneel behind a stability ball and rest your upper arms on it while holding dumbbells with palms facing each other.
Curl the dumbbells toward your shoulders by bending your elbows.
Pause briefly at the top then lower the weights slowly until your arms are extended.
Return to the starting kneeling position behind the ball.
cues: Keep upper arms glued to the ball
Control the descent without swinging
safetyNotes: Ensure the ball is stable and won't roll away during the set.

decline-knee-to-elbow.description: Decline knee-to-elbow is a core crunch on a decline bench that targets the abdominals by drawing knees toward elbows.
steps: Lie back on the decline bench and secure feet under the pads with hands lightly behind head.
Engage core and lift shoulders off bench while bringing one knee toward opposite elbow.
Hold briefly at the top then lower with control to starting position.
Repeat on the opposite side and continue alternating.
cues: Keep lower back pressed into bench
Exhale as you crunch up
safetyNotes: Avoid yanking on neck with hands; support head lightly.

weighted-stability-ball-crunch.description: A weighted crunch on a stability ball that targets the abdominal muscles.
steps: Hold a weight to your chest and sit upright on the stability ball.
Walk feet forward until your lower back is supported by the ball with knees at 90 degrees.
Crunch upward by contracting your abs to lift your shoulders off the ball.
Lower your upper body slowly back to the starting position.
cues: Exhale as you crunch up and keep your neck neutral.
Brace your core to avoid arching your lower back.
safetyNotes: Use a weight light enough for full control to protect your lower back.

landmine-row.description: The landmine row targets back muscles by pulling a barbell anchored at one end toward your torso from a hinged stance.
steps: Secure the barbell in the landmine base and add weight to the free end.
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, hinge at the hips, and grip the bar with one hand.
Pull the bar toward your hip while keeping your torso stable.
Lower the bar slowly back to the starting position and repeat.
cues: Keep your back flat and core braced.
Drive your elbow back toward your hip.
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding your lower back during the hinge.

jump-rope.description: Jump rope is a cardio exercise that targets lower body muscles while building endurance and coordination.
steps: Stand upright with feet together, holding rope handles at hip height with the rope behind you.
Swing the rope over your head in a circular motion using your wrists.
Jump lightly over the rope as it passes under your feet.
Land softly on the balls of your feet and repeat the motion rhythmically.
cues: Keep elbows close to your sides and wrists relaxed.
Jump just high enough for the rope to clear your feet.
safetyNotes: Start at a slow pace to maintain control and avoid tripping.

banded-glute-kicks.description: Banded glute kicks use a resistance band around the ankles to target and strengthen the glutes from a hands-and-knees position.
steps: Loop a resistance band around both ankles and get on all fours with hands under shoulders and knees under hips.
Keep your back flat and core engaged.
Extend one leg straight back against the band until it aligns with your torso.
Slowly return the leg to the starting position under your hip and switch sides.
cues: Squeeze your glute at the top
Keep hips level and square to the floor
safetyNotes: Avoid arching your lower back during the kick.

medicine-ball-burpees.description: A cardio exercise blending squats, planks and jumps while holding a medicine ball to build full-body endurance.
steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart holding the medicine ball at chest height.
Squat down and place the ball on the floor in front of you.
Jump your feet back into a high plank position.
Jump your feet forward to the ball then stand up to return to the start.
cues: Brace your core to keep your body stable
Land softly with knees slightly bent
safetyNotes: Keep hips from sagging in the plank to protect your lower back.

push-up-burpees.description: Push-up burpees combine a squat, plank push-up, and jump into one cardio move that builds full-body endurance.
steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and arms at your sides.
Squat down, place hands on the floor, and jump feet back to a high plank.
Lower into a push-up, press up, then jump feet forward toward your hands.
Explode into a vertical jump with arms overhead, then land softly and repeat.
cues: Keep core braced to hold a straight line from head to heels.
Land softly with knees slightly bent to absorb impact.
safetyNotes: Avoid letting hips sag or lower back arch during the plank and push-up.

dumbbell-rdl.description: Dumbbell RDL is a hip-hinge movement that targets the glutes and hamstrings while building lower-body strength.
steps: Stand with feet hip-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand in front of your thighs.
Hinge at the hips by pushing them back, keeping a soft bend in the knees and lowering the dumbbells along your legs.
Lower until you feel a stretch in the hamstrings while keeping your back flat and chest up.
Drive through your heels to extend the hips and return to standing, squeezing the glutes at the top.
cues: Keep the dumbbells close to your legs throughout the movement.
Hinge from the hips, not by bending at the waist.
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding your lower back to prevent strain on the spine.

banded-fire-hydrants.description: Banded fire hydrants target the outer glutes by lifting a banded leg sideways from a hands-and-knees position.
steps: Position yourself on all fours with a band around both thighs above the knees.
Brace your core and keep your back flat with hips square to the floor.
Lift one bent knee out to the side without twisting your torso.
Lower the leg slowly back to the starting position with control.
cues: Keep hips level throughout the movement
Control the descent to avoid using momentum
safetyNotes: Do not let your hips rotate or your lower back arch.

bench-decline-push-up.description: Decline push-up with feet elevated on a bench to target the chest, especially upper fibers, using bodyweight.
steps: Place feet on a stable bench and hands on floor slightly wider than shoulders, body straight.
Lower chest toward floor by bending elbows at 45-degree angle from body.
Keep core tight and hips aligned until chest nearly reaches floor.
Push through palms to extend arms and return to starting position.
cues: Maintain straight line from head to heels
Lower with control, elbows at 45 degrees
safetyNotes: Avoid letting hips sag to protect lower back.

bulgarian-jumps.description: Bulgarian jumps are explosive plyometric split squats with the rear foot elevated, targeting the quadriceps with added cardio demand.
steps: Place rear foot on a stable bench or step behind you and step the front foot forward into a split stance.
Lower into a lunge by bending the front knee until the thigh is roughly parallel to the floor.
Drive explosively through the front foot to jump upward as high as possible.
Land softly back into the lunge position and repeat for the desired reps before switching sides.
cues: Keep chest tall and core braced
Land with a soft knee to absorb impact
safetyNotes: Use a stable, non-slip surface for the elevated foot to avoid tipping.

incline-alternating-dumbbell-hammer-curl.description: Incline alternating dumbbell hammer curl targets the biceps with neutral-grip alternating curls on an incline bench.
steps: Sit on an incline bench set to about 45 degrees holding a dumbbell in each hand.
Let your arms hang straight down with palms facing inward in a neutral grip.
Curl one dumbbell up toward your shoulder while keeping your upper arm stationary.
Lower the weight slowly back to the start and repeat with the opposite arm.
Continue alternating until reps are complete then return to the starting position.
cues: Keep elbows pinned close to your sides.
Control the descent without swinging.
safetyNotes: Avoid using momentum or arching your back to lift the weights.

bench-low-leg-lifts-with-glute-raise.description: Bench exercise targeting core and glutes via low leg lifts combined with hip raises.
steps: Lie on your back on a bench with legs extended straight and low, hands gripping the sides for support.
Engage your core, lift legs a few inches while simultaneously raising your glutes off the bench.
Pause briefly at the top position with hips elevated.
Lower legs and glutes slowly back to the starting position with control.
cues: Keep lower back pressed into the bench
Squeeze glutes at the top of the lift
safetyNotes: Avoid overarching the lower back to prevent strain.

medicine-ball-unilateral-push-up.description: Unilateral push-up with one hand on a medicine ball to target the chest while challenging stability and core control.
steps: Set up in a high plank with one hand on the medicine ball and the other on the floor, feet together.
Lower your chest toward the floor by bending your elbows while keeping your body straight.
Push through both hands to return to the starting plank position.
Complete all reps on one side before switching hands.
cues: Keep your core braced to prevent hip rotation.
Maintain a straight line from head to heels.
safetyNotes: Ensure the medicine ball is stable and non-slip to avoid wrist or shoulder strain.

decline-dumbbell-alternating-bicep-curl.description: An alternating bicep curl on a decline bench that isolates and strengthens the biceps.
steps: Lie back on a decline bench holding a dumbbell in each hand with palms facing forward and arms extended.
Curl one dumbbell toward your shoulder while keeping the elbow pinned to your side.
Lower the weight slowly with control and switch to curl the opposite arm.
Continue alternating until reps are complete then sit up to return to start.
cues: Keep elbows tucked against your sides
Control the lowering phase on every rep
safetyNotes: Avoid swinging the weights to prevent shoulder strain.

table-top-alternating-towel-arm-slides.description: Alternating arm slides from a tabletop position using towels to build core stability and control.
steps: Start on hands and knees with a towel under each hand on a smooth floor.
Brace your core and slowly slide one arm forward while keeping hips level.
Slide the arm back under your shoulder to the start position.
Repeat with the opposite arm and continue alternating for reps.
cues: Keep hips level and stable
Maintain neutral spine without arching
safetyNotes: Stop if you feel lower back discomfort and avoid shrugging shoulders.

bench-supported-reverse-fly.description: A bench-supported reverse fly that isolates the rear shoulders and upper back using dumbbells.
steps: Lie face down on an incline bench with chest supported and feet on the floor, holding a dumbbell in each hand.
Let your arms hang straight down with a slight bend in the elbows and palms facing each other.
Raise the weights out to the sides until your arms are roughly parallel to the floor.
Slowly lower the weights back to the starting position with control.
cues: Keep a slight bend in the elbows and lead with the elbows
Squeeze the shoulder blades together at the top
safetyNotes: Avoid swinging the weights or using momentum to prevent shoulder strain

alternating-knee-to-elbow-towel-slides.description: Alternating knee to elbow towel slides are a core exercise performed in plank position using towels under the feet to slide knees toward opposite elbows.
steps: Start in a high plank with towels under both feet and hands planted under shoulders.
Slide your right knee forward toward your left elbow while keeping hips level.
Extend the right leg back to the full plank position.
Repeat by sliding the left knee toward the right elbow and returning to start.
cues: Keep core braced and hips stable
Shoulders stay stacked over wrists
safetyNotes: Avoid letting your lower back sag or arch during slides

decline-leg-hack-press.description: Machine exercise where you press a weighted platform away on a decline angle to target the quadriceps.
steps: Position your back flat against the pad with feet shoulder-width on the platform and toes slightly pointed out.
Release the safety handles while keeping your core braced.
Lower the platform by bending your knees until they reach about 90 degrees.
Drive through your feet to extend your legs and return to the starting position without locking your knees.
cues: Keep your lower back pressed into the pad
Drive through your midfoot
safetyNotes: Avoid letting knees cave inward during the press
Do not fully lock out knees at the top to protect joints

standing-single-leg-dumbbell-rdl.description: Single-leg dumbbell Romanian deadlift that targets the glutes via a hip hinge while challenging balance.
steps: Stand on one leg holding a dumbbell in each hand with arms at your sides and a slight bend in the standing knee.
Hinge at the hips to push them back while extending the free leg straight behind you and lowering the dumbbells toward the floor.
Lower until you feel a stretch in the hamstrings of the standing leg while keeping your back flat.
Drive through the heel of the standing leg to extend the hips and return to the upright starting position.
cues: Keep hips square to the floor
Maintain a neutral spine and braced core
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding the lower back; stop the set if balance is lost.

barbell-chest-press.description: The barbell chest press builds chest, shoulder, and triceps strength by pressing a barbell upward while lying on a bench.
steps: Lie on the bench with feet flat on the floor and eyes directly under the bar.
Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder width and unrack it to start position.
Lower the bar to mid-chest with elbows at about 45 degrees from your body.
Press the bar straight up until arms are fully extended, then repeat for reps.
Rack the bar after completing your set.
cues: Keep shoulder blades retracted and pressed into the bench.
Drive the bar in a straight vertical path over your chest.
safetyNotes: Use a spotter or safety pins when lifting heavy loads.
Avoid excessive elbow flare to reduce shoulder strain.

cable-low-to-high-woodchops.description: A cable exercise pulling a low handle diagonally upward across the body to target the core and obliques.
steps: Stand sideways to the low cable pulley with feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent.
Grip the handle with both hands, arms straight and extended toward the low pulley.
Rotate your torso and pull the handle up and across your body to about shoulder height.
Reverse the motion slowly to return to the starting position.
cues: Rotate from your core, not just the arms
Keep hips stable and facing forward
safetyNotes: Control the weight on the way down to avoid twisting or straining the lower back

barbell-narrow-stance-hip-thrust.description: Barbell hip thrust with narrow foot stance to target the glutes through hip extension.
steps: Sit on the floor with upper back against a bench, barbell padded across hips, and feet flat in narrow stance.
Drive hips upward by pushing through heels until body forms a straight line from knees to shoulders.
Squeeze glutes and pause briefly at the top.
Lower hips back to the floor in a controlled motion.
cues: Drive through heels
Keep chin tucked and neck neutral
safetyNotes: Avoid overarching the lower back at the top position

seated-adduction-machine.description: The seated adduction machine strengthens the inner thigh adductor muscles by squeezing the legs together against resistance.
steps: Adjust the seat and thigh pads so your knees align with the pivot and inner thighs rest against the pads.
Sit upright with back supported, feet on the platforms, and grip the handles.
Squeeze your legs together using your inner thighs until they meet in the center.
Pause briefly then slowly return your legs to the open starting position.
cues: Keep your back pressed flat against the pad
Control the return movement without letting the weight stack slam
safetyNotes: Avoid using momentum or bouncing at the end range to protect the hip joints

power-squat-facing-in.description: Barbell squat facing into the rack that builds strength in the quadriceps and glutes.
steps: Set the bar on the rack at upper-back height, step under it and position it across your upper traps with a secure grip.
Unrack by standing tall then step back to clear the rack.
Lower by hinging at hips and bending knees until thighs reach parallel or below.
Drive through heels to extend hips and knees, returning to full standing position.
cues: Brace core and keep chest tall
Knees track over toes
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding the lower back at the bottom to reduce spinal stress.

cable-rope-hammer-curls.description: Cable rope hammer curls target the biceps and forearms using a cable machine with a rope attachment and neutral grip.
steps: Attach rope to low pulley on cable machine and stand facing it feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp rope ends with palms facing each other and arms fully extended at sides.
Curl rope upward by bending elbows while keeping upper arms pinned to sides.
Slowly lower rope back to start position with control.
cues: Keep elbows tucked close to body
Squeeze biceps at the top
safetyNotes: Avoid swinging torso or using momentum to lift the weight.

dumbbell-lateral-raises-with-internal-fr.description: Shoulder isolation exercise combining dumbbell lateral raises and internal frontal raises to target deltoids.
steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart holding dumbbells at your sides, palms facing in.
Raise arms out to the sides to shoulder height with a slight elbow bend.
Lower to start then raise arms forward to shoulder height with palms rotated inward.
Control the descent back to the starting position.
cues: Lead with elbows on lateral raises
Keep core braced and avoid momentum
safetyNotes: Avoid raising above shoulder height to reduce impingement risk

kb-alternating-low-leg-raises.description: Holding a kettlebell overhead while lying down, alternate raising one leg low to target the core and abdominals.
steps: Lie on your back holding the kettlebell with both hands extended above your chest.
Extend both legs straight and hover them just above the floor.
Raise one leg upward while keeping the opposite leg low and hovering.
Lower the raised leg with control and repeat the movement with the other leg.
cues: Press lower back into the floor the entire time
Move slowly and control the descent of each leg
safetyNotes: Avoid arching your lower back by keeping the core braced.

seated-cable-lat-pull-down.description: Seated cable lat pulldown targets the back muscles, mainly the lats, by pulling a bar down to the chest from an overhead position.
steps: Sit on the machine seat with thighs secured under the pads and feet flat on the floor.
Grasp the wide bar overhead with an overhand grip, hands slightly wider than shoulders.
Pull the bar down to your upper chest by driving elbows toward your sides.
Slowly extend your arms to return the bar to the starting overhead position.
cues: Keep torso upright and ribs stacked over hips.
Squeeze shoulder blades together at the bottom.
safetyNotes: Avoid leaning back or using momentum to prevent lower back strain.
Control the return phase to avoid shoulder overextension.

cable-rope-upright-row.description: Cable rope upright row targets the shoulders by pulling a rope upward from a low cable position toward the chin.
steps: Attach rope to low pulley and stand facing the machine feet shoulder-width apart.
Grip rope ends with overhand hold arms extended straight down in front of you.
Pull rope upward to chin level driving elbows high and out to sides.
Lower rope slowly back to start position with full control.
cues: Lead with elbows high and wide
Keep core braced and torso upright
safetyNotes: Avoid raising rope above chin height to reduce shoulder impingement risk.

box-step-ups.description: Box step-ups involve stepping onto a raised platform to strengthen the quadriceps while improving balance and stability.
steps: Stand facing a stable box with feet hip-width apart.
Place one foot fully on the box and drive through the heel to lift your body up.
Step the trailing foot onto the box to stand tall on top.
Reverse the movement by stepping down one foot at a time to return to the start.
cues: Drive through the heel of the lead foot
Keep your torso upright and core braced
safetyNotes: Use a stable box and choose a height that allows controlled movement without knee strain.

hanging-decline-crunch.description: Hanging decline crunch hangs from a bar and crunches knees toward chest to target the abdominals.
steps: Hang from a pull-up bar with overhand grip and arms extended, body straight.
Engage core and lift knees toward chest while curling hips upward.
Squeeze abs at the top position for a brief pause.
Lower legs slowly back to start without swinging or using momentum.
cues: Control the motion and keep shoulders away from ears.
Exhale as you crunch upward.
safetyNotes: Avoid swinging or using momentum to lift the legs.

standing-plate-alternating-frontal-and-l.description: Standing plate alternating frontal and lateral raises strengthen the shoulders by lifting a weight plate forward then sideways while standing.
steps: Stand tall with feet shoulder-width apart and grip a weight plate with both hands at waist level.
Raise the plate straight forward to shoulder height for a frontal raise with slight elbow bend.
Lower it controlled to the start then lift it out to the sides to shoulder height for a lateral raise.
Alternate between the two movements for reps and return the plate to waist level at the end.
cues: Keep core braced and ribs stacked over hips
Lead with elbows and stop at shoulder height
safetyNotes: Avoid swinging or using momentum to protect the shoulder joints

single-leg-side-leg-press.description: Unilateral leg press variation on one leg targeting quadriceps and glutes while building balance and fixing imbalances.
steps: Sit on the leg press machine with your back flat against the pad and place one foot on the platform slightly to the side.
Release the safety handles and lower the platform until your knee bends to about 90 degrees.
Push through your heel to extend the leg and press the platform away until your knee is nearly straight.
Control the descent by bending the knee to return the platform to the starting position.
cues: Keep your knee tracking over your toes
Brace your core and avoid twisting your hips
safetyNotes: Do not lock your knee at the top to reduce joint stress
Stop if you feel knee pain or instability

banded-rainbow-kicks.description: Banded rainbow kicks target the glutes with an arcing leg motion using a resistance band from a hands-and-knees position.
steps: Loop a resistance band around both ankles and get on all fours with hands under shoulders and knees under hips.
Keep your core tight and lift one leg out to the side in a smooth arc.
Continue the arc by swinging the leg across your body midline.
Slowly return the leg to the start position without letting the band snap back.
cues: Keep hips level and square to the floor
Squeeze the glute at the top of the arc
safetyNotes: Avoid arching your lower back by bracing your core

shoulder-floor-push-up.description: A beginner floor push-up variation in pike position that targets the shoulders and upper body for strength and warm-up.
steps: Kneel then lift hips high into a pike with hands on the floor under shoulders
Bend elbows to lower the top of your head toward the floor between your hands
Press through your palms to straighten arms and return to the pike position
Repeat for the target number of reps then lower hips to rest
cues: Keep hips stacked over shoulders throughout
Lower with control and exhale on the push
safetyNotes: Stop if you feel shoulder pinching or discomfort

bent-knee-raises-with-block-between-feet.description: Bent knee raises with a block between the feet target the lower abdominals from a supine position through controlled lifts.
steps: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet off the floor, squeezing a block firmly between your feet.
Engage your core and raise your knees toward your chest while keeping the block secure.
Pause briefly at the top position with hips lifted slightly off the floor.
Lower your feet back toward the starting position with control until knees are bent again.
cues: Keep your lower back pressed into the floor
Squeeze the block throughout the movement
safetyNotes: Avoid arching your lower back or using momentum to prevent strain.

leg-press-narrow-high-stance-glute-focus.description: Narrow high stance leg press targets glutes by placing feet close together and high on the platform.
steps: Sit in the machine with back flat against the pad and feet narrow and high on the platform.
Release the safety handles and lower the weight by bending your knees with control.
Press the platform upward by extending your legs while driving through your heels.
Return to the start position without locking your knees.
cues: Drive through your heels
Keep knees tracking over toes
safetyNotes: Avoid locking knees at the top to protect joints

standing-plate-around-the-worlds.description: Standing plate around the worlds is a shoulder mobility drill circling a weight plate around the head and torso.
steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a weight plate at chest level with both hands.
Extend arms and begin circling the plate clockwise around your head.
Continue the circle, passing the plate behind your back and around to the front.
Complete the full rotation and return the plate to the starting position at chest height.
cues: Keep core braced to stay balanced
Move slowly with control, no swinging
safetyNotes: Avoid shrugging shoulders or arching the lower back during the circle.

duck-walks.description: Duck walks are a low squat walking drill that targets the quadriceps and warms up the lower body.
steps: Stand with feet hip-width apart and lower into a deep squat.
Keep chest up and core tight while staying low.
Take small forward steps without rising from the squat.
Continue for the target reps then stand to return to start.
cues: Keep knees tracking over toes
Stay low throughout the movement
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding your lower back.

bent-over-cable-bar-row.description: A bent-over row using a cable bar attachment to strengthen the back muscles while hinged at the hips.
steps: Attach a straight bar to the low pulley and stand facing the cable machine with feet shoulder-width apart.
Hinge at the hips to lean forward with a flat back until your torso is about 45 degrees to the floor.
Grip the bar with both hands and pull it toward your lower ribs by driving your elbows back.
Slowly lower the bar back down to the start position with control.
cues: Keep your back flat and core braced
Pull elbows back and squeeze shoulder blades together
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding your lower back which can cause strain.

supinated-cable-bar-pull-down.description: Cable exercise pulling a bar down with underhand grip to extend elbows and target triceps.
steps: Attach straight bar to high pulley and grip with palms up shoulder-width apart
Stand tall with elbows tucked at sides and upper arms fixed against torso
Pull bar down by straightening elbows until arms are fully extended
Slowly bend elbows to return bar to start position with control
cues: Keep elbows pinned to sides throughout
Avoid leaning or using body momentum
safetyNotes: Do not flare elbows out or round shoulders forward

dumbbell-bulgarian-rdl-into-body-weight-.description: A unilateral combo starting with dumbbell Bulgarian RDL then flowing into bodyweight pistol squat to target quads and glutes.
steps: Hold dumbbells and rest one foot on a bench behind you with the front foot planted.
Hinge at the hips to lower the dumbbells while keeping a slight bend in the front knee.
Drive through the front heel to stand tall then lift the rear foot forward into pistol position.
Squat down on the front leg with the free leg extended ahead then return to standing.
cues: Keep chest lifted and back flat on the hinge
Drive knee outward over toes throughout the squat
safetyNotes: Use a stable bench to avoid slipping during the transition

wall-sit-with.description: Wall sit with added arm curls to target quads, shoulders, and biceps in one move.
steps: Stand with back flat against a wall, feet shoulder-width apart.
Slide down until thighs are parallel to the floor, holding weights at sides.
Curl weights to shoulders by bending elbows, then lower with control.
Repeat curls for reps while holding the seated position.
Push through heels to stand and return to start.
cues: Keep lower back pressed into the wall
Tuck elbows close to sides during curls
safetyNotes: Stop if knees push past toes or you feel joint pain

incline-dumbbell-narrow-chest-press.description: Targets upper chest by pressing dumbbells upward on an incline bench with hands held close together.
steps: Sit on an incline bench and hold a dumbbell in each hand resting on your thighs.
Lie back and lift the dumbbells to start above your upper chest with palms facing forward and hands close.
Press the dumbbells straight up until arms are extended.
Lower the dumbbells slowly back to the start position above your chest.
cues: Keep elbows tucked close to your body
Drive weights in a straight line over your chest
safetyNotes: Control the descent to avoid shoulder strain

seated-dumbbell-bicep-curls.description: Seated dumbbell bicep curls isolate and strengthen the biceps by flexing the elbows while seated for stability.
steps: Sit upright on a bench with feet flat on the floor, holding a dumbbell in each hand with palms facing forward and arms at your sides.
Keep your back straight, elbows tucked close to your torso, and upper arms stationary.
Curl the dumbbells up toward your shoulders by bending your elbows, squeezing your biceps at the top.
Lower the dumbbells slowly back to the starting position with control.
cues: Keep elbows pinned to your sides
Avoid swinging or using momentum
safetyNotes: Control the lowering phase to avoid elbow strain from momentum.

banded-side-step-squats.description: Banded side step squats add resistance with a band around the thighs during lateral steps in a squat stance to target glutes and warm up lower body.
steps: Place a resistance band just above your knees and stand with feet hip-width apart.
Lower into a partial squat keeping chest lifted and weight in heels.
Step one foot sideways against band tension then bring the other foot to meet it.
Continue stepping side to side for reps or time then stand back up.
cues: Push knees outward to keep constant band tension
Keep torso upright and core braced
safetyNotes: Avoid letting knees cave inward or rounding the lower back

bent-over-cable-rope-row.description: Bent-over row using cable rope attachment to pull toward torso, targeting back muscles.
steps: Attach rope to low cable pulley and stand facing machine with feet shoulder-width apart.
Hinge at hips to lean forward to about 45 degrees while keeping back straight.
Grip rope ends and pull toward lower chest by driving elbows back and squeezing shoulder blades.
Slowly extend arms to return to starting position.
cues: Keep spine neutral and core braced throughout the movement.
Pull with elbows rather than hands to engage back fully.
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding lower back to prevent strain on the spine.

seated-cable-single-arm-row.description: Seated single-arm cable row targets the back by pulling a handle toward the hip while seated for controlled strength work.
steps: Sit on the bench with feet on the footrests and grasp the handle with one hand, arm extended.
Brace your core and keep your torso upright with a neutral spine.
Pull the handle to your lower ribs by driving the elbow back and squeezing the shoulder blade.
Slowly extend the arm forward to return to the start position without twisting.
cues: Keep torso stable and avoid rotation
Drive elbow back toward hip
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding the lower back by bracing the core throughout the movement.

tricep-rope-pulldown.description: Tricep rope pulldown is a cable exercise that isolates and strengthens the triceps through elbow extension.
steps: Attach a rope to the high pulley and stand facing the machine with feet shoulder-width apart.
Grip the rope with both hands, palms facing in, and tuck elbows close to your sides.
Pull the rope down by straightening your elbows until arms are fully extended.
Slowly bend elbows to return the rope to the starting position.
cues: Keep elbows pinned to your sides the entire time.
Squeeze triceps hard at the bottom of each rep.
safetyNotes: Avoid swinging your torso or using momentum to prevent shoulder strain.

hanging-alt-knee-raises.description: Hanging alternating knee raises target the lower abs by lifting one knee at a time while hanging from a bar.
steps: Grip a pull-up bar with hands shoulder-width apart and hang with arms straight and body stable.
Brace your core then raise one knee toward your chest while keeping the other leg straight.
Lower the raised leg slowly back to the start position.
Repeat with the opposite knee and continue alternating for the target reps.
cues: Keep shoulders down and away from ears
Control the motion without swinging
safetyNotes: Avoid arching the lower back by keeping core braced throughout the set.

kneeling-cable-overhead-tricep-extension.description: Kneeling cable overhead tricep extension isolates the triceps through arm extensions overhead while kneeling for core stability.
steps: Attach a rope to the high pulley, kneel facing away from the machine, and hold the rope behind your head with elbows bent.
Keep upper arms fixed and straighten your elbows to extend the rope overhead.
Squeeze your triceps at the top position.
Slowly bend your elbows to return the rope behind your head.
cues: Keep elbows tucked close to your head
Brace your core to avoid arching your back
safetyNotes: Control the movement and avoid using momentum to prevent shoulder strain.

smith-machine-good-mornings.description: Smith machine good mornings are a fixed-path hinge that targets the glutes and hamstrings with the bar on your upper back.
steps: Set the Smith bar at upper-back height and load it.
Step under the bar so it rests across your upper traps and unrack it.
Stand with feet hip-width apart and a slight bend in the knees.
Hinge at the hips, pushing them back while keeping your spine neutral.
Drive hips forward to return to the tall standing position.
cues: Keep your back flat and ribs stacked over hips
Push hips straight back like closing a door behind you
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding the lower back, which can strain the spine.

tricep-band-single-arm-overhead-extensio.description: Single-arm overhead tricep extension using a resistance band to isolate and strengthen the triceps.
steps: Stand with feet hip-width apart and loop the band under both feet.
Hold one end of the band in one hand and raise your arm overhead with elbow bent and pointing up.
Keep upper arm still and straighten elbow to extend arm fully overhead.
Slowly bend elbow to return hand behind head and repeat before switching sides.
cues: Keep elbow fixed and pointed at the ceiling
Brace core to avoid arching your back
safetyNotes: Control the lowering phase to avoid elbow strain

seated-dumbbell-lateral-raises.description: Seated dumbbell lateral raises isolate the side delts by raising weights outward from a seated position.
steps: Sit tall on a bench holding a dumbbell in each hand with palms facing in and arms relaxed at your sides.
With a slight bend in the elbows, raise the weights out to the sides until your arms reach shoulder height.
Pause briefly at the top position.
Lower the dumbbells slowly back to the starting position at your sides.
cues: Lead with your elbows, not your hands
Keep a slight bend in your elbows throughout
safetyNotes: Avoid swinging or using momentum to lift the weights.

dumbbell-squat.description: A lower body squat holding dumbbells at your sides, targeting the quadriceps and glutes.
steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
Brace your core, then bend your knees and hips to lower your body while keeping your chest lifted.
Descend until your thighs are roughly parallel to the floor or as low as comfortable.
Drive through your heels to stand back up to the starting position.
cues: Keep knees tracking over toes
Maintain a neutral spine
safetyNotes: Avoid letting knees cave inward to reduce knee strain risk.

sb-folding-chairs.description: SB folding chairs strengthen the core abdominals using a stability ball for a controlled folding crunch motion.
steps: Sit upright on the stability ball with feet flat on the floor and knees bent at 90 degrees.
Place hands lightly behind your head or cross arms over your chest.
Engage your core, roll hips slightly back on the ball, then crunch forward by contracting abs to fold torso toward thighs.
Slowly return to the upright starting position.
cues: Keep lower back supported by the ball
Exhale as you fold forward
safetyNotes: Avoid pulling on your neck; use abs to initiate the lift.

single-arm-renegade-row.description: Plank-based row using one arm at a time to build back strength and core stability.
steps: Start in a high plank holding a dumbbell in each hand with feet wide.
Brace your core and row one dumbbell up toward your hip.
Lower the dumbbell back to the floor with control.
Switch sides and repeat, alternating arms each repetition.
cues: Keep hips square and level throughout.
Drive elbow back close to your body.
safetyNotes: Avoid letting your hips twist or sag to protect your lower back.

bodyweight-squat-heels-elevated.description: Bodyweight squat with heels elevated on a platform to target quads and glutes while allowing deeper range.
steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and heels on a stable low platform or plates.
Brace core, then bend hips and knees to lower until thighs are at least parallel to floor.
Keep chest up and knees tracking over toes throughout the descent.
Drive through midfoot to extend hips and knees and return to standing.
cues: Keep torso upright to emphasize quads
Drive knees forward over toes
safetyNotes: Use a stable, non-slip surface under heels to prevent falling.

hanging-knee-raises-slow-eccentric.description: Hanging knee raises with slow eccentric lowering to target and strengthen the core abdominals.
steps: Grip an overhead bar with overhand grip and hang with arms extended.
Brace core and raise knees toward chest in a controlled lift.
Pause briefly at the top with knees near chest.
Slowly lower legs back to full hang over 3-5 seconds.
cues: Control the descent without swinging
Keep shoulders packed and core braced
safetyNotes: Avoid momentum to prevent lower back strain.

lower-leg-windmill.description: A core exercise where you lie on your back and rotate straight legs in a circular windmill motion to target the abdominals.
steps: Lie flat on your back with legs extended straight and arms by your sides.
Lift both legs a few inches off the ground while keeping them straight.
Slowly rotate your legs together in a large circular motion.
Complete the circle and lower legs back to the starting lifted position.
cues: Keep your lower back pressed into the floor
Move slowly with control
safetyNotes: Avoid letting your back arch off the ground during the rotation.

push-up-with-arm-row.description: A push-up variation adding a single-arm row to target the chest, back, and core.
steps: Hold dumbbells and set up in a high plank with feet wide.
Lower into a push-up until your chest nears the floor.
Press back up to the plank position.
Row one dumbbell to your hip while keeping hips square.
Lower the weight and repeat, alternating arms each rep.
cues: Keep your core braced and hips level throughout.
Pull the elbow straight back without twisting the torso.
safetyNotes: Avoid letting your lower back sag or hips rotate.

wall-sit-with-parallel-isometric-arm-hol.description: Wall sit with arms extended parallel to the floor, building quad endurance and shoulder stability.
steps: Stand with back against a wall, feet shoulder-width apart and about two feet from the wall.
Slide down until thighs are parallel to the floor with knees bent at 90 degrees.
Extend arms straight forward at shoulder height, parallel to the ground.
Hold the position for the set duration, then push through heels to stand up.
cues: Keep lower back pressed flat against the wall
Maintain neutral neck and relaxed shoulders
safetyNotes: Stop if you feel sharp knee or lower-back pain

cable-oblique-rotations.description: Cable oblique rotations strengthen the core and obliques via controlled torso twists against cable resistance.
steps: Stand sideways to the cable machine with handle at chest height and grip it with both hands.
Step away to create tension with arms extended straight in front of you.
Rotate torso away from the machine by engaging your core while keeping hips stable.
Return slowly to the starting position with control.
cues: Keep hips facing forward and stable
Rotate from the core and shoulders, not the arms
safetyNotes: Control the movement to avoid twisting the lower back or using momentum.

cable-abduction-straight-leg.description: Cable straight-leg abduction targets the outer glutes by moving the leg sideways against cable resistance.
steps: Attach ankle strap to low cable pulley and secure around working ankle.
Stand sideways to machine with feet together and light support hold.
Lift straight leg out to side while keeping hips level and torso upright.
Slowly lower leg back to start position with control.
cues: Keep torso upright and stable
Lead with heel and control the return
safetyNotes: Avoid leaning or twisting to lift heavier; use light weight for form.

bent-over-single-arm-reverse-cable-fly.description: Bent-over single-arm reverse cable fly isolates the rear shoulders by pulling a cable outward in an arc.
steps: Stand sideways to a low cable pulley and grip the handle with the hand farthest from the machine.
Hinge at the hips with a flat back and slight knee bend, letting the arm hang across your body.
Pull the handle outward and upward in an arc until the arm is parallel to the floor.
Lower the handle slowly back to the start position with control.
cues: Keep your back flat and core braced.
Lead with the elbow and squeeze the shoulder blade at the top.
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding the lower back or swinging the body to prevent strain.

decline-single-leg-press-quad-focused.description: A single-leg press on a decline machine that targets the quadriceps through controlled knee extension.
steps: Position yourself on the decline leg press machine with your back flat against the pad and one foot centered on the platform.
Unlock the safety handles and lower the platform by bending your knee until it reaches about 90 degrees.
Press the platform away by extending your leg until your knee is nearly straight.
Slowly lower the platform back to the starting position under control.
cues: Drive through the midfoot
Keep your knee aligned with your toes
safetyNotes: Avoid locking the knee at the top to reduce joint stress.

barbell-bent-over-row-sup-grip.description: Barbell bent over row with underhand grip builds upper back strength by pulling the bar toward the torso from a hinged position.
steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width, grip barbell palms-up slightly wider than shoulders, bar on floor.
Hinge at hips with slight knee bend, keep back flat, lower torso near parallel to floor.
Pull bar toward lower chest by driving elbows back and squeezing shoulder blades together.
Lower bar slowly with control back to starting position on the floor.
cues: Keep back flat and core braced throughout.
Lead with elbows, avoid using momentum.
safetyNotes: Do not round lower back; maintain neutral spine to avoid strain.

barbell-1-2-curl-full-curl-bicep-curls.description: Barbell bicep curl variation combining half-range reps to 90 degrees with full-range reps to target the biceps.
steps: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart and grip the barbell underhand at shoulder width.
Curl the bar halfway up until elbows reach 90 degrees.
Continue curling the bar fully up to your shoulders.
Lower the bar slowly back down to the starting position at your thighs.
cues: Keep elbows pinned to your sides throughout.
Control the descent without swinging.
safetyNotes: Avoid using momentum from your back or hips to lift the weight.

renegade-row.description: Plank-based dumbbell row that builds core stability and upper back strength while resisting rotation.
steps: Start in a high plank with hands gripping dumbbells and feet shoulder-width apart.
Row one dumbbell up toward your hip while keeping hips square to the floor.
Lower the dumbbell back to the floor with control.
Repeat the row on the opposite side before returning to the plank position.
cues: Brace your core to stop any hip twist
Keep your elbow tucked close to your body
safetyNotes: Avoid letting your lower back sag or hips rotate to reduce strain on the spine.

single-leg-hamstring-curl.description: Machine exercise curling one leg at a time to isolate and strengthen the hamstrings.
steps: Lie face down on the leg curl machine with the pad resting above your heel on one leg.
Grip the handles and press your hips firmly into the bench.
Bend your knee to curl the weight toward your glutes, squeezing the hamstring at the top.
Slowly lower the weight back to the starting position with control.
cues: Keep hips pressed into the bench throughout
Control the lowering phase without swinging
safetyNotes: Avoid lifting your hips or using momentum to prevent lower back strain.

barbell-narrow-stance-rdl.description: Barbell narrow stance RDL is a hip-hinge movement with feet close together that targets the hamstrings and glutes.
steps: Stand with feet narrow and parallel, holding the barbell at hip height with an overhand grip.
Hinge at the hips by pushing them back while keeping a slight knee bend and neutral spine.
Lower the bar along your legs until you feel a stretch in the hamstrings.
Drive hips forward to stand tall and return to the starting position.
cues: Keep the bar close to your legs
Hinge from the hips, not the waist
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding the lower back to protect the spine.

seated-hammer-curl.description: Seated hammer curls target the biceps and brachialis with a neutral grip for arm strength and stability.
steps: Sit upright on a bench with back supported, feet flat, holding dumbbells at sides with palms facing inward.
Keep elbows tucked to sides and curl weights toward shoulders without rotating wrists or swinging.
Squeeze biceps at the top position, then slowly lower dumbbells back to starting position with control.
Repeat for reps while maintaining upright posture and full range of motion.
cues: Keep elbows pinned to sides
Control the lowering phase
safetyNotes: Avoid swinging or using momentum to prevent lower back strain.

squatting-cable-rope-hammer-curls.description: Cable rope hammer curls performed from a squat stance to target biceps while challenging stability.
steps: Attach rope to low cable pulley and grip ends with neutral palms facing in.
Step back into a squat with chest up and torso upright.
Curl rope to shoulders by bending elbows without swinging.
Lower rope slowly and repeat while holding squat position before standing.
cues: Keep elbows pinned to sides throughout
Brace core to stay tall in squat
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding lower back; keep neutral spine to protect it.

seated-bicep-curl-with-knee-adduction.description: Seated bicep curl performed while adducting the knees to target the biceps and inner thighs.
steps: Sit upright on a bench with feet flat on the floor and a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward.
Squeeze knees together while curling the weights up toward your shoulders.
Lower the weights slowly while maintaining the knee squeeze.
Release the squeeze and return to the starting position.
cues: Keep elbows tucked close to your sides
Squeeze knees together throughout the curl
safetyNotes: Keep your back supported and core lightly braced to avoid arching.

kb-pull-over-with-glute-bridge.description: Kettlebell pullover performed from a glute bridge position to target the core and abdominals.
steps: Lie on your back with knees bent, feet flat, and hold a kettlebell with both hands above your chest.
Drive hips up into a glute bridge and hold the position steady.
Slowly lower the kettlebell behind your head while keeping hips raised.
Pull the kettlebell back over your chest to the starting position.
Lower hips to the floor to finish the rep.
cues: Brace core to keep ribs stacked over hips
Control the arc with shoulders and core
safetyNotes: Avoid overarching the lower back by maintaining a braced core throughout.

banded-kneeling-lateral-kicks.description: Kneeling on all fours with a band around the ankles, kick one leg out to the side to target glutes and hamstrings.
steps: Loop a band around both ankles and get on hands and knees with a flat back.
Brace your core and keep hips level.
Lift one knee out to the side against band tension while keeping the knee bent.
Slowly lower the leg back to the start position.
cues: Keep your back flat and hips stable
Control the return to avoid snapping the band
safetyNotes: Avoid arching your lower back during the movement.

kneeling-cable-rope-pull.description: Kneeling cable rope pull targets the back by pulling a rope attachment toward the torso from a kneeling position on a cable machine.
steps: Attach a rope to the low pulley on the cable machine and kneel facing it with knees hip-width apart.
Grab the rope ends with both hands, palms facing each other, and sit back on your heels.
Pull the rope toward your lower chest while squeezing your shoulder blades together.
Slowly extend your arms to return to the starting position with control.
cues: Keep your spine neutral and core braced
Drive elbows back close to your sides
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding your lower back or using momentum to pull the weight.

crunch-with-adduction-pelvic-floor.description: A core exercise combining a crunch with inner thigh adduction and pelvic floor engagement to strengthen the abs.
steps: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat, hands across your chest or lightly supporting your head.
Engage your pelvic floor by gently contracting as if stopping urine flow.
Squeeze inner thighs together while lifting shoulders off the ground in a controlled crunch.
Slowly lower shoulders back to the start while maintaining the squeeze and pelvic floor hold.
cues: Keep lower back pressed into the floor
Exhale on the crunch and maintain pelvic floor contraction
safetyNotes: Avoid pulling on your neck or holding your breath during the movement.

deficit-reverse-dumbbell-lunges.description: Deficit reverse dumbbell lunges involve stepping backward from an elevated platform while holding dumbbells, targeting the quadriceps with added range of motion.
steps: Stand on a low stable platform holding dumbbells at your sides with feet together.
Step one foot backward off the platform into a lunge position.
Lower until your back knee nearly touches the floor and front thigh is parallel to the ground.
Drive through the front heel to push back up to the starting position on the platform.
cues: Keep torso upright and core braced
Front knee tracks over ankle without collapsing inward
safetyNotes: Use a stable low platform to prevent slips or loss of balance during the step-back.

narrow-dumbbell-press.description: A close-grip overhead dumbbell press that targets the shoulders for strength and stability.
steps: Sit or stand tall holding dumbbells at shoulder height with palms facing forward and hands close together.
Press the weights straight overhead until your arms are fully extended.
Pause briefly at the top position.
Lower the dumbbells with control back to shoulder height.
cues: Keep elbows tucked close to your body
Brace your core to stay stable
safetyNotes: Avoid locking elbows at the top to reduce joint stress.

standing-dumbbell-windmill.description: Standing dumbbell windmill is a core and shoulder exercise where you hold weight overhead while bending sideways to the floor.
steps: Stand with feet wider than shoulders and press one dumbbell straight overhead with arm locked.
Hinge at hips and rotate torso to reach free hand toward opposite foot while keeping eyes on the dumbbell.
Pause briefly at the bottom with neutral spine and straight overhead arm.
Drive through heels and hips to return to standing start position.
cues: Keep overhead arm fully extended and stable
Hinge from hips while rotating torso
safetyNotes: Control the movement to avoid rounding the lower back or dropping the dumbbell.

smith-machine-narrow-rdl.description: Smith machine narrow RDL is a close-stance hip hinge that targets the glutes with controlled bar travel.
steps: Set the Smith bar at mid-thigh height and stand with feet narrow, gripping the bar overhand.
Unrack the bar and stand tall with a soft knee bend and flat back.
Hinge at the hips, push the glutes back, and lower the bar toward mid-shin level.
Drive the hips forward to return to standing, then rerack the bar after reps.
cues: Keep the bar close to your legs
Push hips back while keeping chest lifted
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding the lower back by bracing the core throughout.

glute-bridge-with-adduction.description: Glute bridge with adduction adds a knee squeeze to target glutes, inner thighs, and core during the hip lift.
steps: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor hip-width apart; place a soft ball or pillow between your knees.
Press knees firmly together to engage inner thighs while bracing your core.
Lift hips toward the ceiling by squeezing glutes until body forms a straight line from knees to shoulders.
Lower hips slowly back to the floor with control.
cues: Squeeze knees together throughout the movement
Drive through heels and keep ribs stacked over hips
safetyNotes: Avoid overarching the lower back by keeping the core braced and hips from rising too high.

supinated-single-arm-cable-tricep-pull-d.description: Single-arm cable tricep pulldown with supinated grip to isolate and strengthen the triceps.
steps: Attach a handle to the high pulley and stand facing the machine with feet shoulder-width apart.
Grip the handle with one hand palm-up and tuck your elbow close to your side with arm bent.
Extend your arm downward by straightening the elbow while keeping the upper arm fixed.
Slowly bend the elbow to return the handle to the start position with control.
cues: Keep elbow pinned to side throughout
Squeeze tricep at full extension
safetyNotes: Avoid swinging the torso to prevent momentum and strain

flat-lying-prone-row.description: Lying face down on a flat bench, row weights up toward your hips to target the back muscles.
steps: Lie face down on a flat bench with dumbbells in each hand and arms hanging straight down.
Pull the weights up toward your hips by driving elbows back and squeezing shoulder blades.
Pause at the top then slowly lower the weights back to the starting position.
cues: Keep chest and hips pressed into the bench
Drive elbows back toward hips, not up
safetyNotes: Avoid lifting your chest or arching your lower back during the row.

bent-over-one-arm-tricep-kickbacks.description: A bent-over single-arm movement that isolates the triceps by extending the elbow against resistance.
steps: Hold a weight in one hand, hinge at the hips with a flat back, and rest your free hand on your knee or a bench.
Tuck your upper arm against your side so the elbow is bent at about 90 degrees.
Straighten your elbow to push the weight backward until your arm is fully extended.
Slowly bend the elbow to return the weight to the starting position.
cues: Keep your elbow pinned to your side the whole time.
Move only at the elbow joint and avoid swinging.
safetyNotes: Do not round your lower back; keep a neutral spine to avoid strain.

banded-glute-bridge.description: A banded glute bridge strengthens glutes and core by lifting hips against band resistance while lying supine.
steps: Place a band around your thighs above the knees and lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat.
Press knees outward into the band and drive hips upward by squeezing glutes.
Hold briefly at the top with body aligned from knees to shoulders.
Lower hips slowly back to the starting position with control.
cues: Drive through your heels
Keep core braced and ribs down
safetyNotes: Avoid arching the lower back; keep movement controlled to protect the spine.

hanging-alternating-oblique-raises.description: Hanging from a bar, alternately raise legs to each side to target the obliques and core.
steps: Grip an overhead bar with palms facing forward and hang with arms straight.
Brace your core then lift one knee diagonally toward the same-side elbow.
Lower the leg slowly back to the start position.
Repeat the lift on the opposite side.
cues: Keep torso still and avoid swinging.
Lead with the knee and rotate through the obliques.
safetyNotes: Control the descent to avoid momentum that can strain the lower back or shoulders.

cable-single-arm-tricep-push-down.description: Single-arm cable exercise isolating the triceps through controlled elbow extension.
steps: Stand facing the cable machine with a single handle on the high pulley and feet shoulder-width apart.
Grip the handle with one hand palm down, elbow tucked at your side and forearm near parallel to the floor.
Push the handle straight down by extending your elbow until your arm is fully straight.
Slowly bend your elbow to return the handle to the starting position under control.
cues: Keep elbow pinned to your side throughout the movement.
Squeeze the tricep at full extension and control the return.
safetyNotes: Avoid locking the elbow or using momentum to reduce joint stress.

dumbbell-deficit-curtsy-lunge.description: Dumbbell deficit curtsy lunge elevates the front foot on a platform to deepen range and target quadriceps with added stability challenge.
steps: Stand on a low stable platform holding dumbbells at your sides with feet together.
Step one foot back and across behind the front leg, lowering into a curtsy lunge.
Drop until your back knee nearly reaches the floor while keeping torso upright.
Drive through the front heel to push back up and return to the starting position.
cues: Keep front knee tracking over ankle
Maintain upright torso and stacked ribs over hips
safetyNotes: Use a stable platform to prevent slipping or tipping.

cable-bar-bicep-curl.description: The cable bar bicep curl uses a low pulley straight bar to isolate and strengthen the biceps through controlled curling.
steps: Attach a straight bar to the low pulley of a cable machine and stand facing it with feet shoulder-width apart.
Grip the bar palms up, elbows tucked at your sides, and stand tall with a slight knee bend.
Curl the bar up toward your shoulders by bending your elbows while keeping upper arms still.
Slowly lower the bar back to the starting position with control.
cues: Keep elbows pinned to your sides throughout.
Squeeze your biceps at the top and control the descent.
safetyNotes: Avoid swinging your body or using momentum to lift the weight.

lying-crunch-with-adduction.description: Lying crunch with adduction targets the core abdominals by combining a standard crunch with inner-thigh squeezing.
steps: Lie on your back with knees bent, feet flat, and a small ball or cushion between your knees.
Squeeze your knees together firmly to engage the inner thighs.
Crunch upward by lifting your shoulders off the floor while keeping the squeeze.
Slowly lower your shoulders back to the starting position.
cues: Keep lower back pressed into the floor
Exhale as you crunch up and maintain the knee squeeze
safetyNotes: Avoid pulling on your neck with your hands
Stop if you feel sharp pain in your lower back

pallof-cable-press.description: The Pallof cable press builds core stability by pressing a cable away from the body while resisting rotation, targeting the abdominals.
steps: Stand sideways to a cable machine set at chest height and hold the handle with both hands at your chest.
Step away to create tension in the cable.
Press the handle straight out in front of you without rotating your torso.
Return the handle slowly to your chest to complete the rep.
cues: Brace your core to stay stable
Keep hips and shoulders square to the front
safetyNotes: Start with light weight to focus on form and avoid twisting.

barbell-b-stance-hip-thrust.description: Barbell hip thrust in a staggered B-stance that loads one glute and hamstring more to build strength and stability.
steps: Sit on the floor with upper back against a bench and place a padded barbell across your hips.
Position feet in B-stance with one foot flat on the floor and the other slightly behind on the ball of the foot.
Drive through the front heel to lift hips until your body forms a straight line from knees to shoulders.
Squeeze glutes at the top then lower hips back to the floor with control.
cues: Drive through the front heel
Keep hips level and avoid twisting
safetyNotes: Use a bar pad to protect your hips from pressure
Avoid overextending your lower back at the top

single-arm-dumbbell-bent-over-row.description: Single-arm dumbbell bent-over row builds back strength by pulling a weight toward the hip from a hinged position.
steps: Stand with feet hip-width, hold dumbbell in one hand and support opposite hand on knee or bench.
Hinge at hips to lean torso forward until nearly parallel to floor, keeping back straight.
Pull dumbbell toward hip by driving elbow back and up.
Squeeze shoulder blade at top then lower weight slowly to start position.
cues: Keep back flat and core braced
Lead with elbow without twisting torso
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding lower back to reduce strain risk.

standing-plate-reverse-flies.description: Standing plate reverse flies strengthen the rear shoulders and upper back by lifting a weight plate outward from the chest.
steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and hold a plate at chest height using both hands, palms facing in.
Slightly bend your elbows and raise the plate out to the sides until your arms reach shoulder height.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the top position.
Lower the plate slowly back to the starting position in front of your chest.
cues: Keep elbows slightly bent throughout the movement
Lead with the elbows and avoid shrugging
safetyNotes: Do not use momentum or arch your lower back; keep your core engaged for stability.

alternating-shoulder-taps.description: From a high plank, alternately tap each shoulder to build core stability and shoulder strength.
steps: Start in a high plank with hands under shoulders and body straight from head to heels.
Lift one hand to tap the opposite shoulder while keeping hips level.
Return the hand to the floor and repeat with the other side.
Continue alternating taps, maintaining a stable plank position.
cues: Keep hips still and square to the floor
Brace your core to limit rocking
safetyNotes: Drop to your knees if your lower back sags or you lose control.

barbell-box-squat.description: Barbell box squat: lower onto a box with bar on upper back to target glutes and build controlled squat depth.
steps: Position barbell on upper back, stand with feet shoulder-width in front of a sturdy box.
Brace core, push hips back and lower until you sit fully on the box.
Pause briefly on the box, then drive through heels to stand up tall.
Return to the starting standing position with bar still on your back.
cues: Sit back toward the box, not down
Keep chest up and spine neutral
safetyNotes: Use a stable box at knee height or slightly below to avoid knee stress.

machine-single-leg-extension.description: Machine single leg extension isolates the quadriceps by extending one knee against padded resistance.
steps: Sit on the machine with your back against the backrest and adjust the seat so your knee lines up with the pivot point.
Place one foot behind the padded lever while resting the other foot on the floor.
Extend your working leg forward until the knee is straight without locking it.
Lower the leg slowly back to the starting bent position with control.
cues: Keep your back pressed firmly into the pad.
Squeeze the quadriceps at the top of the movement.
safetyNotes: Avoid locking the knee at full extension to reduce joint stress.

underhand-lat-pulldown.description: The underhand lat pulldown targets the back muscles using a cable machine, pulling a bar down to the chest with palms facing inward.
steps: Sit at the lat pulldown machine, secure your thighs under the pads, and grip the bar with an underhand grip at shoulder width.
Lean back slightly with chest lifted and pull the bar down to your upper chest by driving elbows toward your sides.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the bottom for a brief pause.
Slowly straighten your arms to return the bar to the starting position overhead.
cues: Pull elbows down and back, not with your hands
Keep chest up and avoid leaning too far back
safetyNotes: Do not use momentum or swing your torso, as this can strain the lower back.

smith-machine-b-stance-rdl.description: Staggered-stance Romanian deadlift on the Smith machine that targets the glutes with emphasis on one side.
steps: Position the Smith bar at mid-thigh height and stand with feet in a B-stance, one foot slightly behind the other.
Grip the bar overhand, unrack it, and stand tall with core braced.
Hinge at the hips to push them back while lowering the bar along your front leg.
Drive through the front heel to extend hips and return to standing position.
cues: Keep back flat and ribs stacked over hips
Push hips back before bending knees
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding the lower back by maintaining core tension throughout.

seated-single-arm-dumbbell-arnold-press.description: Seated single-arm dumbbell Arnold press strengthens all shoulder heads via a rotating overhead press.
steps: Sit tall on a bench with back support, feet flat, holding one dumbbell at shoulder height with palm facing you.
Press the dumbbell overhead while rotating your wrist so the palm faces forward at full extension.
Lower the dumbbell slowly, rotating the wrist back to the starting palm-in position.
Return to the start position with control before repeating on the same side.
cues: Brace your core to keep your torso stable
Rotate smoothly without using momentum
safetyNotes: Avoid arching your lower back during the press

cable-standing-single-arm-reverse-fly.description: Standing single-arm cable reverse fly isolates the rear deltoids by pulling the arm back in a fly motion.
steps: Stand sideways to a low cable pulley and grip the handle with the hand farthest from the machine.
Step away to create tension, hinge slightly at the hips, and keep a soft bend in the knees.
With elbow slightly bent, pull the arm outward and backward until it reaches shoulder height.
Pause briefly then slowly return the arm to the start position with control.
cues: Brace your core to stop torso twisting
Lead with the elbow and squeeze the rear shoulder at the top
safetyNotes: Use light weight to keep form and avoid shoulder strain
Do not swing or use momentum on the return

seated-single-arm-press-narrow-dumbbell.description: Seated single-arm narrow-grip dumbbell press that targets the shoulders for strength and stability.
