steps: Sit tall on a bench with back support, feet flat, holding one dumbbell at shoulder height with neutral grip.
Brace your core and press the dumbbell straight overhead until your arm is fully extended.
Pause at the top, then lower the dumbbell slowly back to shoulder height.
Finish all reps on one side before switching arms.
cues: Keep ribs stacked over hips
Press in a straight line over your shoulder
safetyNotes: Avoid arching your lower back by keeping your core braced throughout.

facepull-reverse-grip.description: Reverse-grip face pull on cable targets rear shoulders and upper back for stability.
steps: Attach rope to cable at face height and grip ends with palms facing up.
Step back to tension arms straight, then pull rope to face while separating ends.
Squeeze shoulder blades at peak contraction.
Slowly return to start position with control.
cues: Keep elbows high and wide
Lead the pull with rear shoulders
safetyNotes: Avoid using momentum or arching the lower back.

barbell-reverse-lunge.description: Barbell reverse lunge targets the glutes by stepping one leg backward into a split stance while holding a barbell on the upper back.
steps: Position barbell across upper back with feet hip-width apart and core braced.
Step one foot backward and lower until both knees reach about 90 degrees.
Drive through the front heel to push back to the starting standing position.
Repeat the movement with the opposite leg.
cues: Keep torso upright and ribs stacked over hips
Front knee tracks over ankle without caving inward
safetyNotes: Avoid letting the back knee slam into the floor or rounding the lower back

cable-single-arm-tricep-pull-down.description: Single-arm cable exercise targeting the triceps by extending the elbow to pull the handle downward.
steps: Stand facing the cable machine with a handle attached high and grip it with one hand, palm facing down.
Tuck your elbow close to your side with the arm bent at about 90 degrees.
Extend your arm straight down by straightening the elbow until fully locked out.
Slowly bend the elbow to return the handle to the starting position with control.
cues: Keep elbow pinned to your side throughout.
Move only at the elbow, no shoulder or body swing.
safetyNotes: Avoid flaring the elbow outward or using momentum to prevent strain.

cable-isometric-squat-with-rope-pull.description: An isometric squat hold paired with cable rope pulls to target glutes and back.
steps: Stand facing a cable machine with a rope attached to the low pulley and feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the rope with both hands and lower into a squat until thighs are near parallel to the floor.
Hold the squat position while pulling the rope toward your chest in a controlled rowing motion.
Slowly return the rope to the start and stand up to complete the rep.
cues: Keep chest lifted and core braced.
Drive through heels and squeeze glutes at the bottom.
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding the lower back during the hold to reduce strain risk.

standing-dumbbell-curl-and-press.description: A standing compound move that combines a bicep curl with an overhead press to target the arms and shoulders.
steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding dumbbells at your sides with palms facing forward.
Curl the dumbbells to your shoulders by bending your elbows.
Press the dumbbells straight overhead until your arms are fully extended.
Lower the dumbbells back to your shoulders, then extend your arms to return to the start.
cues: Keep your core braced and ribs stacked over hips.
Avoid arching your lower back during the press.
safetyNotes: Do not lock your elbows at the top to protect your joints.

cable-abduction-behind-straight-leg-kick.description: Cable glute exercise where you kick a straight leg back and out to target hip extension and abduction.
steps: Attach ankle strap to low cable pulley and secure it to your working leg.
Stand facing the machine with support leg slightly bent and core braced.
Keep working leg straight and extend it backward while moving slightly outward.
Squeeze glutes at end range then slowly return leg to starting position.
cues: Keep hips square and avoid twisting
Lead movement with the heel
safetyNotes: Do not arch your lower back or use momentum to swing the leg.

smith-machine-stationary-lunge.description: The Smith machine stationary lunge strengthens the quadriceps in a split stance with the bar fixed for stability.
steps: Position the Smith bar at shoulder height across your upper back and step one foot forward into a lunge stance.
Unrack the bar and lower your body by bending both knees.
Descend until your front thigh is parallel to the floor while keeping your torso upright.
Drive through your front heel to return to the starting position.
cues: Keep torso tall and upright
Front knee tracks over toes
safetyNotes: Avoid letting the front knee cave inward to reduce knee stress.

decline-leg-single-leg-press-high-stance.description: Single-leg decline press with high foot stance targets glutes via hip extension on the machine.
steps: Sit on the decline leg press machine with back flat against the pad and place one foot high on the platform.
Unrack the weight by extending your working leg while keeping the safety handles released.
Lower the platform slowly by bending your knee until your thigh reaches about 90 degrees.
Drive through your heel to extend the leg and return to the starting position under control.
cues: Drive through your heel
Keep hips stable and square to the platform
safetyNotes: Control the descent to avoid lower back strain
Do not lock the knee at the top of the movement

banded-donkey-kicks.description: Banded donkey kicks strengthen the glutes by extending one leg backward against band resistance from a hands-and-knees position.
steps: Start on hands and knees with a resistance band looped around one foot.
Keep your core braced and back flat.
Extend the banded leg straight back, squeezing the glute at the top.
Slowly return the leg to the starting position without touching the knee down.
cues: Keep hips level and square to the floor
Squeeze the glute hard at the top
safetyNotes: Avoid arching your lower back during the kick

incline-dumbbell-hammer-curls.description: Incline dumbbell hammer curls build biceps strength by curling weights with a neutral grip on an inclined bench.
steps: Sit on an incline bench with a dumbbell in each hand, arms extended down and palms facing inward.
Curl the dumbbells toward your shoulders by bending your elbows while keeping upper arms still.
Pause at the top and squeeze your biceps.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with control.
cues: Keep elbows fixed and close to your sides
Lower the weights slowly without swinging
safetyNotes: Avoid using momentum to lift the weights, which reduces biceps work and risks elbow strain.

preacher-curls-single-arm.description: Single-arm preacher curl isolates one bicep by resting the upper arm on a padded bench for focused curling.
steps: Set up at the preacher bench with your upper arm fully supported on the pad and the dumbbell held with arm extended.
Curl the weight up to your shoulder by flexing the elbow while keeping the upper arm stationary.
Squeeze your bicep at the top position for a moment.
Lower the dumbbell slowly back to the starting extended position.
cues: Keep your upper arm glued to the pad
Control the lowering phase without swinging
safetyNotes: Do not hyperextend the elbow at the bottom to avoid joint stress.

seated-cable-supinated-row.description: The seated cable supinated row targets the back by pulling a cable handle toward the torso with an underhand grip while seated.
steps: Sit on the bench with feet on the platform and grasp the handle with palms facing up, arms extended.
Sit tall with a slight knee bend and keep your back straight.
Pull the handle to your lower chest by driving elbows back and squeezing shoulder blades together.
Slowly extend your arms to return to the starting position with control.
cues: Keep torso upright and avoid excessive leaning.
Lead with elbows and squeeze shoulder blades at the end.
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding your lower back or using momentum to prevent strain.

smith-machine-curtsy-lunges.description: Smith machine curtsy lunges target the glutes with a crossed-leg lunge under the fixed bar for added stability.
steps: Set the Smith bar at shoulder height and position it across your upper back.
Unrack the bar and step one foot back, crossing it behind the other leg.
Lower until your back knee is near the floor while keeping your torso upright.
Drive through the front heel to return to the starting position.
cues: Keep your chest lifted and hips square.
Drive through the front heel to stand.
safetyNotes: Avoid letting your front knee cave inward during the lunge.

smith-machine-shoulder-press.description: The Smith machine shoulder press targets the shoulders by pressing a fixed bar overhead from a seated or standing position.
steps: Position yourself under the bar at shoulder height with a grip slightly wider than shoulders and feet planted firmly.
Unrack the bar and press it straight overhead until arms are fully extended.
Lower the bar with control back to shoulder level.
Rerack the bar safely after your set.
cues: Brace your core to keep ribs stacked over hips
Press in a straight vertical line without flaring elbows
safetyNotes: Avoid overarching your lower back to reduce strain risk.

machine-seated-abduction.description: Seated machine abduction targets the outer glutes by pushing thighs apart against padded levers.
steps: Sit with back against the pad and feet on the footrests.
Adjust thigh pads to rest against the outer sides of your legs.
Push knees outward against the pads until thighs are apart.
Slowly bring legs back together to the starting position.
cues: Keep torso upright and back pressed into the seat
Control the return without letting legs snap inward
safetyNotes: Avoid using momentum or swinging to prevent knee or hip strain.

bench-supported-w.description: Lying face-down on a bench, raise dumbbells to form a W with your arms to target the rear shoulders and rotator cuff.
steps: Lie face-down on a flat bench holding a dumbbell in each hand with arms hanging straight down toward the floor.
Bend elbows to about 90 degrees and lift upper arms out to the sides until parallel to the floor.
Externally rotate forearms upward so hands point toward the ceiling forming a W shape.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with control.
cues: Keep thumbs pointing up at the top position
Squeeze shoulder blades together without shrugging
safetyNotes: Use light weights to avoid straining the shoulders or rotator cuff.

ball-single-leg-curl.description: A stability ball exercise targeting the hamstrings with one leg while lying on your back.
steps: Lie on your back with one heel on the ball and the opposite knee bent.
Lift your hips to form a straight line from shoulders to the working knee.
Bend the working knee to roll the ball toward your glutes.
Extend the leg to roll the ball out and lower your hips to the floor.
cues: Keep hips high and stable throughout
Control the movement with your heel
safetyNotes: Do not let hips sag to avoid lower back strain.

decline-sumo-leg-press.description: A machine leg press with wide sumo foot placement on a decline platform that targets the quadriceps.
steps: Sit on the machine and place feet wide on the platform with toes turned out about 45 degrees.
Release the safety handles and lower the platform by bending the knees.
Push through the heels to extend the legs and return to the start position.
Re-engage the safety handles after the set is complete.
cues: Drive through your heels
Keep knees tracking over toes
safetyNotes: Keep your lower back pressed into the pad throughout the movement.

alternating-dumbbell-bulgarian-split-squ.description: Alternates dumbbell Bulgarian split squats with Romanian deadlifts to target the glutes via squat and hinge patterns.
steps: Hold dumbbells at your sides and place one foot on a bench behind you for the split squat stance.
Lower until your front thigh is roughly parallel to the floor, keeping torso upright.
Drive through the front heel to stand, then hinge at the hips with a flat back for the RDL.
Return to standing and switch legs or repeat the sequence as needed to complete the set.
cues: Keep front knee tracking over toes
Maintain neutral spine on both movements
safetyNotes: Use a stable bench to prevent slipping or tipping.

dumbbell-skull-crusher.description: Dumbbell skull crusher is a lying triceps extension that isolates the back of the upper arms.
steps: Lie on a flat bench holding dumbbells with arms extended above your chest, palms facing each other.
Keep upper arms still and bend elbows to lower dumbbells toward your forehead.
Pause briefly then straighten elbows to press weights back to the start position.
Control the descent and return fully to the starting position after each rep.
cues: Keep elbows tucked and pointed toward the ceiling
Lower slowly without letting weights touch your head
safetyNotes: Avoid flaring elbows outward to protect shoulders

hanging-leg-raises.description: Hanging leg raises build core strength by lifting straight legs while hanging from an overhead bar.
steps: Hang from a pull-up bar with overhand grip and arms fully extended.
Brace your core and keep legs straight with feet together.
Lift legs upward until they reach at least hip height.
Lower legs slowly and controlled back to the hanging start position.
cues: Keep lower back from arching
Control the descent without swinging
safetyNotes: Avoid using momentum or kipping to lift the legs

bent-knee-raise-with-yoga-block-adductio.description: A core exercise lying on your back, squeezing a yoga block between bent knees while raising them to target the abdominals.
steps: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat, placing a yoga block between your knees.
Squeeze the block firmly with your inner thighs.
Raise your bent knees toward your chest in a controlled motion.
Lower your knees back to the start without letting the block slip.
cues: Press your lower back into the floor throughout.
Exhale as you raise and squeeze the block.
safetyNotes: Avoid arching your lower back or using momentum.

prone-bent-leg-hamstring-machine.description: Lying face-down on a leg curl machine, bend knees to pull heels toward glutes, targeting the hamstrings.
steps: Lie prone on the machine with hips at the edge of the pad and knees aligned with the pivot.
Position ankles under the padded lever with legs fully extended.
Curl legs by bending knees to bring heels toward glutes.
Slowly extend legs back to the starting position with control.
cues: Keep hips pressed firmly into the pad
Control the lowering phase without momentum
safetyNotes: Avoid lifting hips or using momentum to prevent lower back strain

machine-tricep-push-down.description: Machine tricep pushdown involves pushing a bar down to extend the arms, primarily targeting the triceps.
steps: Stand facing the machine, feet shoulder-width, and grip the bar with palms down.
Tuck elbows at your sides with upper arms fixed against your body.
Push the bar straight down until arms are fully extended.
Slowly bend elbows to return the bar to the starting position.
cues: Keep elbows pinned to your sides.
Squeeze triceps at the bottom.
safetyNotes: Avoid locking elbows forcefully to protect joints.
Do not swing or use momentum.

dumbbell-single-leg-hip-thrust.description: Single-leg dumbbell hip thrust targeting the glutes for strength and stability on one side.
steps: Sit on the floor with upper back against a bench, one foot flat on the floor and the other leg extended.
Place a dumbbell across your hips and hold it steady with both hands.
Drive through the heel of your planted foot to lift your hips until your body forms a straight line from knee to shoulder.
Squeeze your glutes at the top, then lower your hips back to the floor in a controlled motion.
cues: Keep hips level and square to the floor
Drive through your heel without arching your lower back
safetyNotes: Avoid letting the knee of your working leg cave inward to protect the joint.

bulgarian-split-squat-glute-focused.description: A single-leg squat with the rear foot elevated on a bench that targets the glutes through hip extension.
steps: Stand with your back to a bench and rest the top of one foot on it.
Step the other foot forward into a split stance with your torso upright.
Lower until your front thigh is roughly parallel to the floor.
Drive through the front heel to return to the starting position.
cues: Keep your front knee tracking over your toes
Drive through the front heel and squeeze the glute at the top
safetyNotes: Avoid letting the front knee cave inward to protect the knee joint.

dumbbell-narrow-chest-press.description: The dumbbell narrow chest press targets the chest and triceps by pressing two dumbbells with a close grip from a lying position on a bench.
steps: Lie on a flat bench holding a dumbbell in each hand with palms facing each other and weights touching above your chest.
Lower the dumbbells slowly toward the center of your chest by bending your elbows.
Press the dumbbells upward until your arms are fully extended.
Return the dumbbells to the starting position above your chest.
cues: Keep elbows tucked close to your sides
Squeeze your chest at the top of each rep
safetyNotes: Avoid flaring elbows outward to reduce shoulder strain

barbell-walking-lunges.description: Barbell walking lunges are a dynamic leg exercise where you hold a barbell on your upper back and step forward into alternating lunges, primarily targeting the quadriceps.
steps: Position the barbell across your upper back, gripping it securely with both hands.
Step forward with one leg into a lunge, lowering until both knees bend to about 90 degrees.
Drive through the front heel to push off and bring the back leg forward into the next lunge.
Continue alternating legs while walking forward for the desired reps, then return to standing.
cues: Keep your torso upright and core engaged for balance.
Step far enough so your front knee stays aligned over your ankle.
safetyNotes: Avoid letting your front knee cave inward to reduce joint stress.

cable-single-arm-frontal-raises.description: Single-arm cable exercise that isolates the front shoulder by raising the arm forward from a low pulley.
steps: Stand facing away from a cable machine with the pulley at the lowest setting, feet shoulder-width apart and one hand gripping the handle.
Keep a slight bend in the elbow and raise the arm forward to shoulder height with palm facing down.
Pause briefly at the top then slowly lower the arm back to the starting position.
Complete reps on one side before switching arms.
cues: Brace your core to avoid leaning back
Lead with the back of your hand and keep the wrist straight
safetyNotes: Avoid using momentum or swinging the weight to prevent shoulder strain
Do not raise above shoulder height to reduce impingement risk

hanging-knee-raises.description: Hanging knee raises target the lower abdominals by lifting bent knees toward the chest while hanging from a bar.
steps: Hang from a pull-up bar with an overhand grip and arms fully extended.
Brace your core and raise your knees toward your chest in a controlled motion.
Pause briefly at the top position.
Lower your legs slowly back to the starting hanging position.
cues: Keep shoulders packed down and away from ears
Avoid swinging or using momentum
safetyNotes: Do not let your lower back arch excessively to protect the spine.

cable-kickback.description: Cable kickback is a glute isolation move on a cable machine that extends the leg backward to strengthen the buttocks.
steps: Attach an ankle strap to the cable machine's low pulley and secure it around one ankle.
Face the machine, hold the frame for support, and stand with a slight bend in your standing knee.
Keep your leg straight and kick it backward while squeezing your glute at the top.
Slowly return your leg forward to the start without letting the cable yank you.
cues: Keep hips square and torso stable.
Squeeze glute at peak without arching your back.
safetyNotes: Avoid momentum or lower-back hyperextension by controlling every rep.

sb-stomach-vaccum.description: The stability ball stomach vacuum targets deep core abdominals by drawing the belly inward while seated on the ball.
steps: Sit upright on the stability ball with feet flat on the floor and hands resting on thighs.
Exhale fully to empty your lungs.
Draw your navel toward your spine and hold the contraction.
Breathe shallowly while maintaining the hold, then release and return to normal breathing.
cues: Pull belly button toward spine
Keep chest lifted and shoulders relaxed
safetyNotes: Avoid holding your breath to prevent dizziness.

duel-cable-lat-pulls.description: Dual cable lat pulldown targets the lats and upper back by pulling two overhead cables downward while seated.
steps: Sit at the cable machine with thighs secured under pads and grasp both handles with an overhand grip.
Sit tall with arms extended overhead and a slight lean back from the hips.
Pull the handles down toward your upper chest while squeezing shoulder blades together.
Slowly extend arms back to the starting position with control.
cues: Lead with elbows pulling down and back
Keep chest lifted and ribs stacked over hips
safetyNotes: Avoid swinging or using momentum to prevent lower back strain.

bench-supported-y.description: Bench-supported Y raises target the shoulders by lying face-down on a bench and lifting arms into a Y shape.
steps: Lie face-down on an incline bench with chest supported and arms hanging straight down holding weights.
With thumbs up, raise arms diagonally forward and outward into a Y position.
Squeeze shoulder blades together at the top position.
Slowly lower arms back to the starting position.
cues: Keep neck neutral and gaze down
Lead the movement with your elbows
safetyNotes: Avoid shrugging shoulders or arching the lower back during the lift.

dumbbell-opposite-lateral-and-frontal-ra.description: This shoulder exercise alternates a lateral raise on one side with a frontal raise on the other using dumbbells to hit all deltoid heads.
steps: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with palms facing in.
Raise one arm out to the side to shoulder height while raising the opposite arm forward to shoulder height.
Pause briefly at the top then lower both arms with control back to the starting position.
Switch which arm does the lateral raise on the next rep and continue alternating.
cues: Keep a slight bend in the elbows throughout.
Move slowly and avoid using momentum or swinging.
safetyNotes: Do not raise arms above shoulder height to protect the joints.

cable-donkey-kickback.description: Cable donkey kickback isolates the glutes by extending one leg backward against resistance from a cable machine.
steps: Attach ankle strap to low pulley and secure around working ankle.
Face machine, hold frame for balance, and stand with slight forward lean.
Brace core then extend leg straight back, squeezing glute at top.
Slowly return leg to start without letting weight stack touch down.
cues: Keep hips square and avoid twisting torso.
Squeeze glute hard at peak and control the return.
safetyNotes: Do not arch lower back; maintain neutral spine to avoid strain.

machine-single-arm-chest-press.description: A machine exercise where you press a handle forward with one arm to target the chest muscles.
steps: Sit on the machine and adjust the seat so the handle is level with your mid-chest.
Grip the handle with one hand and keep your elbow bent at about 90 degrees.
Press the handle forward until your arm is straight.
Slowly return the handle to the starting position with control.
cues: Keep your back pressed flat against the pad
Exhale as you push the handle forward
safetyNotes: Avoid locking your elbow at the end of the press

leg-curl-machine-glute-emphasis.description: The leg curl machine with glute emphasis is a machine-based movement that targets the hamstrings through knee flexion while positioning for added glute activation.
steps: Lie face down on the machine with your knees aligned to the pivot point and ankles under the padded lever.
Grip the handles and keep your hips pressed firmly into the bench.
Bend your knees to curl the lever toward your glutes, focusing on the contraction.
Slowly lower the weight back to the start position without locking your knees.
cues: Keep hips glued to the pad
Squeeze glutes at the top
safetyNotes: Avoid using momentum or arching your lower back.

prone-hamstring-machine.description: Lying face down on the prone hamstring machine, this exercise isolates the hamstrings through controlled knee flexion.
steps: Lie face down on the bench with knees at the edge and ankles under the padded lever.
Grip the handles and press hips firmly into the pad.
Bend knees to curl heels toward glutes in a smooth motion.
Slowly extend legs to return the lever to the start position.
cues: Keep hips pressed down throughout
Control the lowering phase without locking knees
safetyNotes: Avoid lifting hips off the pad or using momentum to prevent lower back strain.

chest-fly-machine.description: The chest fly machine isolates the chest muscles by bringing padded handles together in a controlled hugging motion.
steps: Sit with back flat against the pad and adjust seat so handles align with mid-chest.
Grasp handles with neutral grip and elbows bent at about 90 degrees.
Bring handles together in front of chest in a smooth arc until they nearly touch.
Slowly return handles to starting position with control, keeping tension on the chest.
cues: Keep a slight bend in elbows throughout the movement.
Squeeze chest at the point of peak contraction.
safetyNotes: Avoid letting handles travel too far back to prevent shoulder strain.

preacher-hammer-curl-single-arm.description: Single-arm preacher curl with neutral hammer grip to isolate the biceps on a bench pad.
steps: Sit at the preacher bench and rest your upper arm fully on the pad while holding a dumbbell in a neutral grip.
Curl the dumbbell toward your shoulder by bending only at the elbow.
Squeeze the biceps briefly at the top then lower the weight slowly to full extension.
cues: Keep upper arm pinned to the pad
Control the lowering phase
safetyNotes: Do not lock the elbow at the bottom to avoid joint strain

single-arm-kneeling-cable-row.description: A unilateral cable row done from a kneeling position to target the back muscles on one side.
steps: Kneel on one knee with the opposite foot forward, facing the low cable pulley.
Grab the handle with the hand on the same side as your forward foot.
Pull the handle toward your hip by driving the elbow straight back.
Slowly straighten the arm to return to the starting position with control.
cues: Keep torso stable and avoid twisting
Squeeze shoulder blade at the top of the pull
safetyNotes: Do not round your lower back or use momentum to prevent strain.

cable-single-arm-upright-row.description: Single-arm cable pull from low to high that targets the shoulders for strength and stability.
steps: Stand beside the cable machine with the pulley set low and attach a handle.
Grip the handle with one hand, palm facing in, and extend your arm fully downward.
Pull the handle upward toward your chin while leading with a high elbow.
Lower the handle slowly back to the starting position with control.
cues: Lead with your elbow high and out to the side
Brace your core to stay tall and stable
safetyNotes: Use light weight to avoid momentum or shoulder strain

sb-plank.description: A core exercise holding a plank position with forearms on a stability ball to target the abdominals.
steps: Place a stability ball on the floor and kneel in front of it.
Position forearms on the ball with elbows under shoulders.
Extend legs back to form a straight line from head to heels.
Hold for the desired duration then lower knees to return to start.
cues: Keep body straight from head to heels
Brace core to prevent hips from sagging
safetyNotes: Avoid letting the ball roll by keeping weight centered
Start on a stable surface to reduce fall risk

leg-press-hack-stance.description: Leg press hack stance is a machine exercise that targets the quadriceps with feet placed low and close on the platform.
steps: Sit with your back flat against the pad and place feet low and narrow on the platform.
Release the safety handles while keeping knees slightly bent.
Lower the platform by bending knees until they reach about 90 degrees.
Drive through your feet to extend legs and return to start position.
cues: Keep knees tracking over toes
Press lower back into the seat
safetyNotes: Avoid locking knees at the top to reduce joint stress
Stop if lower back lifts off the pad

single-arm-cable-over-head-lat-pulldown.description: Single-arm cable overhead lat pulldown isolates one side of the back, targeting the lats through a downward pull from above.
steps: Attach a single handle to the cable pulley at its highest position.
Stand or kneel facing the machine and grasp the handle with one hand, arm extended overhead.
Pull the handle down and back toward your side by driving the elbow down.
Slowly extend the arm to return the handle to the overhead starting position.
cues: Keep torso stable and avoid twisting
Lead with the elbow and squeeze the lat at the bottom
safetyNotes: Control the return to avoid shoulder strain from sudden upward pull

plank-with-kettlebell-unilateral-slide.description: Plank variation with unilateral kettlebell slides to build core stability and anti-rotation strength.
steps: Set up in a high plank with one kettlebell placed beside your body within reach.
Slide the kettlebell forward or laterally with one hand while maintaining plank alignment.
Return the kettlebell to the start position under control without shifting your hips.
Complete reps on one side then switch arms to finish the set.
cues: Brace your core and keep hips square to the floor.
Move slowly to prevent torso rotation.
safetyNotes: Avoid letting your lower back sag or shoulders shrug during slides.

glute-bridge-w-adduction-slow-eccentric.description: Supine hip bridge with inner-thigh squeeze and slow lowering phase to target glutes, adductors, and core stability.
steps: Lie on your back, knees bent, feet flat, with a soft ball or cushion between your knees.
Brace your core and drive hips upward into a bridge while firmly squeezing knees together.
Pause briefly at the top, then lower hips slowly to the floor over 3-5 seconds.
Return to the starting position with control.
cues: Squeeze knees together throughout the movement
Lower with control to keep tension on the muscles
safetyNotes: Avoid arching your lower back at the top position

single-arm-overhead-dumbbell-tricep-exte.description: Single-arm overhead dumbbell extension that isolates and strengthens the triceps.
steps: Stand or sit tall holding a dumbbell in one hand and press the arm straight overhead with elbow pointing up.
Bend the elbow to lower the dumbbell slowly behind the head.
Straighten the arm to press the dumbbell back overhead.
Lower the arm to the side after completing reps and switch sides.
cues: Keep upper arm still and elbow pointed toward ceiling
Move only through the elbow joint
safetyNotes: Avoid flaring the elbow outward or arching the lower back.

smith-machine-sumo-squat.description: A wide-stance squat on the Smith machine that targets the quadriceps with guided bar support for stability.
steps: Stand with feet wide apart and toes turned out under the Smith bar.
Unrack the bar across your upper back by rotating the handles.
Bend knees and hips to lower until thighs are parallel to the floor.
Drive through heels to extend legs and return to standing start position.
cues: Keep chest lifted and torso upright
Push knees outward over toes
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding lower back by keeping core engaged throughout

barbell-overhead-tricep-extensions.description: Barbell overhead tricep extension targets the triceps by lowering and pressing a barbell behind the head from an overhead start position.
steps: Grip the barbell with both hands shoulder-width apart and press it overhead until arms are fully extended.
Keep upper arms fixed and bend elbows to lower the barbell behind your head in a controlled motion.
Extend elbows to press the barbell back to the overhead starting position.
Repeat for reps then lower the barbell safely to your shoulders or rack.
cues: Keep elbows tucked close to your head throughout.
Move only at the elbow joints, not the shoulders.
safetyNotes: Avoid flaring elbows outward or using momentum to prevent shoulder strain.

barbell-sumo-hip-thrust.description: Barbell sumo hip thrust targets the glutes with a wide stance and hip extension from a seated position against a bench.
steps: Sit on the floor with upper back against a stable bench, barbell padded across hips, and feet wide in sumo stance.
Drive through heels to thrust hips upward until body forms a straight line from knees to shoulders.
Squeeze glutes hard at the top then lower hips back to the floor in control.
Reset fully before the next rep.
cues: Drive through heels
Keep ribs stacked over hips
safetyNotes: Use padding on the bar to avoid hip bruising.

leg-press-sumo-stance.description: Leg press sumo stance is a machine exercise with feet wide and toes out that targets the quadriceps.
steps: Sit in the machine with your back flat against the pad and place feet wide on the platform with toes turned out.
Release the safety handles and slowly bend your knees to lower the platform in a controlled motion.
Drive through your heels to extend your legs and push the platform back to the starting position.
Re-engage the safety locks once your set is complete before exiting the machine.
cues: Keep knees tracking over toes
Maintain neutral spine and braced core
safetyNotes: Control the descent to avoid knee strain or hyperextension at the bottom.

dumbbell-squat-with-curl-and-press.description: A compound move that pairs a squat with a bicep curl and overhead press to hit legs, arms, and shoulders.
steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding dumbbells at your sides with palms facing forward.
Lower into a squat by bending knees and hips while keeping chest lifted.
Drive through heels to stand up, curling the dumbbells to your shoulders.
Press the dumbbells straight overhead, then lower them back to your sides.
cues: Keep chest up and core braced
Drive knees outward as you squat
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding your lower back during the squat.

kettlebell-swings.description: Kettlebell swings use explosive hip thrusts to target the glutes and lower body while providing a cardio boost.
steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and kettlebell on the floor between your feet.
Hinge at the hips to grip the handle with both hands while keeping your back flat.
Swing the kettlebell back between your legs then drive your hips forward to swing it to chest height.
Let the kettlebell swing back down between your legs and repeat the motion.
cues: Drive hips forward explosively
Keep arms relaxed and spine neutral
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding your lower back during the hinge to reduce strain risk.

ball-straight-leg-bridge.description: A stability ball move where you bridge hips upward with straight legs to target hamstrings and glutes.
steps: Lie on your back with heels on the ball and legs straight.
Press through heels to lift hips into a straight bridge.
Hold briefly at the top with glutes squeezed.
Lower hips slowly back to the floor.
cues: Keep legs fully straight
Drive through heels
safetyNotes: Avoid excessive lower back arch by keeping core braced.

barbell-bulgarian-split-squat-quad-focus.description: The barbell Bulgarian split squat is a unilateral exercise that targets the quadriceps and glutes with a barbell on the upper back.
steps: Position the barbell on your upper back and rest your rear foot on a bench behind you.
Step your front foot forward so the knee aligns over the ankle.
Lower your body by bending the front knee until the thigh is parallel to the floor.
Drive through the front heel to return to the starting position.
cues: Keep torso upright to emphasize quads.
Drive through the front heel.
safetyNotes: Avoid letting the front knee cave inward.

seated-dumbbell-shoulder-press-with-addu.description: Seated dumbbell overhead press that builds shoulder strength while engaging the core through arm adduction at the top.
steps: Sit tall on a bench with back support, feet flat, and dumbbells held at shoulder height palms facing forward.
Brace your core then press the dumbbells straight overhead until arms are fully extended.
Bring the dumbbells together overhead to add adduction while keeping shoulders down.
Lower the weights with control back to the starting position at shoulder height.
cues: Keep ribs stacked over hips and avoid arching the lower back
Press in a straight line and squeeze at the top
safetyNotes: Maintain a neutral spine throughout to reduce lower back stress

table-top-knee-taps-with-block-adduction.description: Core exercise in tabletop position using a block between the knees for adduction while performing controlled knee taps.
steps: Lie on your back with knees bent and lift legs into tabletop so shins are parallel to the floor.
Place a block between your knees and press them gently into it.
Brace your core and tap one foot lightly toward the floor then return to tabletop.
Alternate sides while maintaining steady pressure on the block and a flat lower back.
cues: Keep lower back pressed into the floor
Squeeze the block steadily without letting hips rock
safetyNotes: Stop if you feel strain in your lower back or neck

sl-glute-bridge-w-leg-raise.description: Single-leg glute bridge with leg raise strengthens core abdominals and glutes via hip extension and stability.
steps: Lie on your back with one knee bent and foot flat, opposite leg extended straight.
Raise the straight leg vertically toward the ceiling while keeping hips level.
Drive through the grounded heel to lift hips off the floor into a bridge.
Lower hips slowly to the ground, then return the raised leg to the start position.
cues: Brace core to keep pelvis stable
Drive through heel without arching back
safetyNotes: Avoid twisting hips or overarching the lower back during the lift.

seated-single-arm-dumbbell-curl-with-blo.description: Seated single-arm dumbbell curl while squeezing a block between the knees to target biceps and core.
steps: Sit tall on a bench with feet flat and place a block between your knees.
Hold a dumbbell in one hand with palm forward and arm extended at your side.
Curl the dumbbell to your shoulder while squeezing the block firmly with your knees.
Lower the dumbbell slowly to the start position and release the squeeze.
cues: Keep elbow pinned to your side throughout the curl.
Sit upright and avoid leaning or arching your back.
safetyNotes: Control the weight on the way down to avoid swinging or momentum.

towel-pike.description: Towel Pike is a sliding core exercise from plank position that lifts hips into a pike to target the abdominals.
steps: Place towels under feet and set up in a high plank with hands under shoulders.
Brace core and slide feet forward toward hands while lifting hips upward.
Continue sliding until body reaches a pike with hips high and legs straight.
Slowly extend feet back to the starting plank position under control.
cues: Keep shoulders stacked over wrists throughout
Initiate movement from the core, not momentum
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding the lower back as hips rise

power-hack-squat.description: Machine-based squat that targets the quadriceps with back support from the pad.
steps: Position feet shoulder-width on the platform with shoulders under the pads and back flat against the support.
Release the safety handles and lower by bending knees and hips until thighs are parallel to the platform.
Drive through your heels to extend legs and return to the starting position.
Re-engage the safety locks after completing the set.
cues: Keep lower back pressed into the pad
Drive through heels without locking knees
safetyNotes: Avoid letting knees cave inward or tracking too far past toes.

banded-glute-kickbacks.description: Banded glute kickbacks use a resistance band to extend one leg back, isolating and strengthening the glutes.
steps: Loop a resistance band around one ankle and anchor the other end low behind you.
Stand tall holding support for balance, feet together and core braced.
Kick the banded leg straight back while squeezing the glute at full extension.
Slowly return the leg to the start position under control.
cues: Keep hips level and square to avoid twisting.
Squeeze the glute hard at the top without arching the back.
safetyNotes: Control the movement to prevent lower back strain from momentum or overextension.

cable-abduction-bent-knee.description: Cable bent-knee abduction strengthens the glutes by lifting the leg sideways against cable resistance.
steps: Secure the ankle strap to the low pulley and attach it to your outer ankle.
Stand with your side to the machine, working leg away from it, and bend that knee.
Abduct the bent leg outward to about 45 degrees while keeping hips stable.
Return the leg slowly to the starting position under control.
cues: Keep your core engaged to avoid tilting your pelvis.
Drive the movement from your glute, not your hip flexor.
safetyNotes: Move slowly and avoid using momentum to swing the leg.

tricep-pushdown-bar-grip-change.description: Cable tricep pushdown using a straight bar where grip changes target the triceps through elbow extension.
steps: Attach straight bar to high cable pulley and stand facing the machine.
Grip bar with palms down or up, elbows tucked at sides.
Push bar down until arms fully extend while keeping upper arms still.
Return bar slowly to start and switch grip for the next set.
cues: Keep elbows pinned to sides
Control the return without swinging
safetyNotes: Avoid flaring elbows or leaning back to protect shoulders and lower back.

kettlebell-rdl.description: Kettlebell Romanian deadlift that strengthens the hamstrings and glutes via a controlled hip hinge.
steps: Stand with feet hip-width apart holding the kettlebell in front of your thighs with both hands and a slight knee bend.
Hinge at the hips by pushing them back while keeping your back flat and chest lifted.
Lower the kettlebell along your legs until you feel a stretch in the hamstrings.
Drive hips forward to stand tall, squeezing glutes at the top.
cues: Keep spine neutral and kettlebell close to legs
Hinge from hips, not waist
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding the lower back during the hinge.

banded-clams-with-hip-raise.description: Side-lying banded exercise that combines a clamshell leg abduction with a hip raise to target the glutes.
steps: Place a band above your knees and lie on your side with knees bent and hips stacked.
Lift your hips off the ground into a bridge while keeping feet together.
Push your top knee upward against the band, opening your legs like a clamshell.
Lower the knee then the hips back to the starting position.
cues: Keep hips stacked and avoid rolling backward
Squeeze glutes to drive the hip raise
safetyNotes: Do not let your lower back arch during the hip lift.

smith-machine-rdl.description: The smith machine RDL is a guided hip hinge exercise that targets the glutes and hamstrings.
steps: Stand with feet hip-width apart under the bar set at hip height and grip it overhand.
Hinge at the hips by pushing them back with a soft knee bend while lowering the bar along your legs.
Lower until you feel a hamstring stretch with a flat back then pause briefly.
Drive hips forward to stand tall and return to the starting position.
cues: Keep your back flat and chest lifted
Push hips back while keeping the bar close to your legs
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding your lower back to prevent strain

standing-dumbbell-wide-bicep-curl.description: A standing dumbbell curl with wide grip that targets the biceps brachii muscles.
steps: Stand tall with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with palms facing forward and hands wider than shoulders.
Keep upper arms still and curl both dumbbells upward toward your shoulders.
Pause briefly at the top and squeeze your biceps.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position with control.
cues: Keep elbows pinned to your sides
Avoid swinging or using momentum
safetyNotes: Do not let elbows flare outward or use body momentum to lift.

kettlebell-sumo-rdl.description: A wide-stance Romanian deadlift with a kettlebell that targets the glutes through hip hinging.
steps: Stand with feet wider than shoulder width, toes turned out, and kettlebell on the floor between your feet.
Hinge at the hips, push your butt back, and grasp the kettlebell with both hands while keeping your back flat.
Drive your hips forward to stand tall and squeeze your glutes at the top.
Hinge at the hips again to lower the kettlebell with control back to the floor between your feet.
cues: Push hips back and keep chest up
Keep shins vertical and weight in heels
safetyNotes: Maintain a neutral spine throughout to protect your lower back.

supported-hip-dead-bug.description: A core exercise where you lie on your back with hips supported and move opposite arm and leg to build abdominal stability.
steps: Lie on your back with hips supported on a pad or bench, knees bent at 90 degrees, and arms reaching toward the ceiling.
Press your lower back gently into the support and brace your core.
Slowly extend one arm overhead while straightening the opposite leg toward the floor.
Return to the starting position with control and repeat on the other side.
cues: Keep lower back pressed down throughout
Move slowly and avoid momentum
safetyNotes: Stop if you feel lower back arching or discomfort

smith-machine-narrow-squat.description: Narrow-stance squat on the Smith machine that primarily targets the quadriceps.
steps: Set the bar at upper back height and stand with feet in a narrow stance under it.
Unrack the bar by rotating it forward and standing tall with core engaged.
Lower by bending knees and hips until thighs are parallel to the floor or deeper.
Drive through heels to extend legs and return to the standing start position.
cues: Keep knees tracking over toes throughout the movement
Maintain an upright torso and neutral spine
safetyNotes: Set safety stops at a height that supports you if you cannot complete a rep.

low-leg-bent-knee-windmill.description: A beginner core exercise lying on your back that rotates bent knees side to side to target the abdominals.
steps: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor, arms extended to the sides.
Brace your core and slowly lower both knees toward one side while keeping shoulders on the ground.
Return knees to the center in a controlled motion.
Repeat the rotation to the opposite side and continue alternating for the desired reps.
cues: Keep lower back pressed into the floor
Move slowly without using momentum
safetyNotes: Avoid letting shoulders lift off the ground during the rotation

resistance-band-bicep-curls.description: Resistance band bicep curls strengthen the biceps by flexing the elbows against band tension.
steps: Stand on the band with feet shoulder-width apart, gripping one end in each hand with palms forward.
Keep elbows tucked at your sides and shoulders relaxed.
Curl hands toward shoulders by bending elbows.
Slowly lower hands back to the starting position.
cues: Keep elbows pinned to your sides throughout.
Control the lowering phase without swinging.
safetyNotes: Avoid arching your back or using momentum to lift.

seated-dumbbell-reverse-fly.description: Seated dumbbell reverse fly targets rear shoulders by raising weights out to sides while leaning forward from the hips.
steps: Sit on the edge of a bench holding a dumbbell in each hand with palms facing in.
Lean torso forward with a flat back and let the weights hang below your chest.
Raise the dumbbells out to the sides with elbows slightly bent until arms are parallel to the floor.
Lower the weights slowly back to the starting position.
cues: Lead with your elbows and squeeze shoulder blades together at the top.
Keep a slight bend in elbows and avoid swinging the weights.
safetyNotes: Do not round your lower back; maintain a neutral spine to avoid strain.

cable-overhead-tricep-extensions.description: Cable overhead tricep extension isolates the triceps by extending the arms overhead against cable resistance.
steps: Attach a rope to the low pulley and stand facing away from the cable machine.
Grasp the rope with both hands and raise your arms so elbows point up behind your head.
Extend your arms fully overhead by straightening the elbows.
Slowly bend the elbows to return the rope behind your head.
cues: Keep elbows tucked close to your ears
Control the lowering phase without swinging
safetyNotes: Avoid arching the lower back by bracing your core throughout.

lower-leg-bent-knee-windmill-with-block.description: A core exercise where you bend one knee and rotate to reach a block, targeting the abdominals.
steps: Stand with feet wide apart, place a block outside one foot, and bend that knee slightly.
Extend one arm upward and rotate your torso to reach the opposite hand toward the block.
Pause briefly at the bottom while keeping your chest open.
Reverse the motion to return to standing with control.
cues: Brace your core to control the twist
Keep the bent knee tracking over your toes
safetyNotes: Move slowly to avoid twisting strain on the lower back.

barbell-hip-thrusts.description: Barbell hip thrusts build glute strength by driving the hips upward from a seated floor position against a weighted bar.
steps: Sit on the floor with upper back against a bench and padded barbell across hips.
Position feet flat on floor shoulder-width apart with knees bent at 90 degrees.
Drive hips upward by pushing through heels until body forms a straight line from knees to shoulders.
Squeeze glutes at top then lower hips controlled back to starting position.
cues: Drive through heels
Keep ribs stacked over hips
safetyNotes: Use a pad on the bar to protect hip bones from pressure.

standing-dumbbell-frontal-raises.description: Standing dumbbell frontal raises target the front shoulders by lifting weights forward from the hips to shoulder height.
steps: Stand with feet hip-width apart, holding dumbbells in front of your thighs with palms facing your body.
Keep arms nearly straight and raise the dumbbells forward to shoulder height.
Pause briefly at the top then slowly lower the weights back to the starting position.
cues: Keep core tight and avoid leaning back
Lift with control using shoulders, not momentum
safetyNotes: Do not swing the weights or raise above shoulder height to reduce impingement risk

isometric-glute-bridge-with-adduction.description: Hold a glute bridge while squeezing knees together to target core abdominals through isometric effort.
steps: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor hip-width apart.
Lift hips to form a straight line from knees to shoulders.
Squeeze knees firmly together and hold for the target duration.
Lower hips slowly back to the floor to finish.
cues: Keep ribs stacked over hips
Squeeze thighs inward without letting hips sag
safetyNotes: Avoid overarching the lower back by maintaining core tension.

cable-single-arm-lateral-raises.description: Cable single-arm lateral raise isolates the side deltoid by lifting a cable handle outward to shoulder height.
steps: Stand sideways to a low cable pulley and hold the handle with your outer hand, arm at your side.
Keep a slight elbow bend and raise your arm out to the side until it reaches shoulder height.
Pause at the top then slowly lower the handle back to the starting position at your side.
Keep your torso upright and core braced throughout the movement.
cues: Lead with the elbow and keep wrist neutral.
Lift slowly without swinging or using momentum.
safetyNotes: Avoid raising above shoulder height to reduce shoulder impingement risk.

smith-machine-sumo-rdl.description: Wide-stance Romanian deadlift on the Smith machine targeting hamstrings and glutes.
steps: Position feet wide with toes turned out and grip the Smith bar at hip level.
Unlock the bar and stand tall with a neutral spine and slight knee bend.
Hinge at the hips to push them back while lowering the bar along your legs.
Drive hips forward to return to the tall standing position.
cues: Keep chest lifted and back flat
Push hips back then squeeze glutes to stand
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding the lower back at any point in the movement.

standing-plate-lateral-raises.description: Standing plate lateral raises target the side shoulders by lifting a weight plate out to the sides while standing.
steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a plate with both hands in front of your thighs.
Keep elbows slightly bent and raise the plate out to the sides until arms reach shoulder height.
Pause briefly at the top, then slowly lower the plate back to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired reps while maintaining control.
cues: Lead with your elbows
Keep shoulders down and away from ears
safetyNotes: Avoid swinging or using momentum to prevent shoulder strain.

single-leg-dumbbell-rdl.description: Single-leg hinge with dumbbells targeting hamstrings and glutes while improving balance and stability.
steps: Stand on one leg holding a dumbbell in each hand with a slight knee bend.
Hinge at the hips and push the free leg back as you lower the weights.
Lower until a stretch is felt in the standing hamstring while keeping the back flat.
Drive through the heel to return to standing, squeezing the glute at the top.
cues: Keep hips square and level to the ground.
Brace your core to maintain a neutral spine.
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding the lower back to reduce strain risk.

seated-dumbbell-arnold-press.description: The seated dumbbell Arnold press builds all three shoulder heads via a rotating overhead press from a supported seat.
steps: Sit tall on a bench with back support, dumbbells at shoulder height and palms facing you.
Press weights overhead while rotating wrists so palms face forward at the top.
Fully extend arms with a slight elbow bend, then reverse the motion.
Lower dumbbells to shoulders while rotating palms back to face your body.
cues: Brace core to keep lower back pressed into the bench.
Rotate wrists smoothly without momentum or jerking.
safetyNotes: Avoid locking elbows at the top to protect shoulder joints.

machine-seated-abduction-forward-lean.description: Seated machine exercise with forward lean to abduct thighs and target the glutes.
steps: Sit on the machine, lean torso forward from hips, and position outer thighs against pads.
Place feet on rests with knees bent near 90 degrees.
Push legs outward against resistance while holding the forward lean.
Slowly return legs to start position under control.
cues: Keep forward lean to emphasize glutes over outer thighs.
Move slowly and avoid using momentum on the return.
safetyNotes: Do not round lower back; maintain neutral spine to reduce strain risk.

decline-leg-press.description: The decline leg press targets quads and glutes by pressing a weighted platform away while lying back on an angled machine.
steps: Lie back in the machine with your back flat against the pad and feet shoulder-width on the platform.
Release the safety locks and lower the platform by bending your knees to a comfortable depth.
Press through your heels to extend your legs and return the platform to the start position.
Re-engage the safety locks after completing your reps.
cues: Keep lower back pressed into the pad
Drive through your heels
safetyNotes: Stop if you feel lower back strain
Avoid letting knees cave inward

squat-jumps.description: Squat jumps are explosive plyometric moves that build power in the quadriceps and glutes by jumping from a squat position.
steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and toes slightly turned out.
Lower into a squat by bending knees and hips while keeping chest lifted.
Drive through feet to explode upward into a jump, swinging arms for momentum.
Land softly with bent knees and return to the squat position.
cues: Keep chest up and core tight
Land softly with knees bent to absorb impact
safetyNotes: Avoid locking knees on landing to reduce joint stress.

table-top-isometric-hold-w-adduction.description: Isometric core hold in tabletop leg position with thigh adduction to target abdominals.
steps: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor.
Lift feet until knees are at 90 degrees and shins parallel to the floor.
Press knees together firmly and hold the position isometrically.
Maintain the hold for the target duration while breathing steadily.
Lower feet back to the floor to return to the start position.
cues: Keep lower back pressed into the floor
Squeeze thighs together without rocking the pelvis
safetyNotes: Stop if you feel lower back strain or discomfort

alternating-rocking-lat-pulldown.description: Alternating rocking lat pulldown builds back strength with single-arm pulls and a controlled forward-back torso rock on the cable machine.
steps: Sit at the lat pulldown station, secure thighs under pads, and grip the bar overhand with both hands slightly wider than shoulders.
Lean back slightly, pull the bar to your upper chest with one arm while rocking your torso backward from the hips.
Control the return to start as you rock forward and immediately switch to pulling with the opposite arm.
Continue alternating arms on each rep while keeping the motion smooth and controlled.
cues: Rock from the hips, not the lower back
Drive elbow down and back toward hip
safetyNotes: Avoid momentum or excessive swing that strains shoulders or spine

machine-seated-hovering-abduction.description: Seated machine exercise that isolates the glutes by driving the thighs outward against resistance pads.
steps: Sit upright with back against the pad and position outer thighs against the abduction pads.
Adjust seat so knees form a 90-degree angle and grip the handles for stability.
Push legs outward in a controlled arc, hovering briefly at full abduction while squeezing glutes.
Slowly return legs to the start position without letting pads rest or touch.
cues: Brace core to keep torso stable
Drive with outer glutes, not momentum
safetyNotes: Avoid locking knees or arching lower back during the push

dumbbell-pullover.description: The dumbbell pullover is performed lying on a bench, moving a dumbbell overhead to behind the head to target the chest.
steps: Lie on your back on a flat bench with feet planted and hold a dumbbell with both hands above your chest, arms extended.
With elbows slightly bent, lower the dumbbell in a controlled arc behind your head until you feel a chest stretch.
Engage your chest to pull the dumbbell back up to the starting position above your chest.
Return to the initial position and repeat for reps.
cues: Keep elbows slightly bent throughout the movement
Press your lower back into the bench
safetyNotes: Control the weight on the way down to maintain grip and avoid shoulder overstretch

table-top-w-adduction-alt-foot-raise.description: Beginner core exercise in tabletop position with alternating foot raises and leg adduction to target the abdominals.
steps: Lie on your back and lift your knees to form a tabletop with shins parallel to the floor.
Squeeze your inner thighs together to adduct the legs while bracing your core.
Slowly lower one foot toward the floor without arching your back, then return it to start.
Alternate sides for the set and finish by returning both feet to the tabletop position.
cues: Keep your lower back pressed into the floor
Move slowly and control the leg motion
safetyNotes: Avoid arching your lower back off the ground during foot raises.

cable-rdl.description: Cable RDL is a hip-hinge exercise that targets the glutes with cable resistance.
steps: Stand facing a low cable pulley with feet hip-width apart and grasp the bar attachment with both hands.
Hinge at the hips by pushing your glutes back while keeping a slight knee bend and flat back.
Lower the cable until you feel a hamstring stretch, then pause briefly.
Drive through your heels to extend your hips and return to standing while squeezing your glutes.
cues: Keep your back flat and chest up
Push hips back before bending knees
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding your lower back to reduce strain risk.

barbell-incline-chest-press.description: The barbell incline chest press builds upper chest strength by pressing a barbell upward from an inclined bench.
steps: Set bench to 30-45 degree incline and lie back with feet flat on floor.
Grip bar slightly wider than shoulders and unrack it above upper chest.
Lower bar to upper chest with elbows at 45 degrees.
Press bar upward to full extension then rack it safely.
cues: Keep shoulder blades retracted and chest up
Drive feet into floor for stability
safetyNotes: Use a spotter for heavy sets to avoid getting pinned under the bar.

barbell-hipthrust.description: Barbell hip thrust targets the glutes by driving the hips upward with a barbell across the hips from a seated floor position.
steps: Sit on the floor with upper back against a bench and barbell padded across the hips, feet flat and knees bent.
Brace core, then drive through heels to lift hips until body forms a straight line from knees to shoulders.
Squeeze glutes at the top position.
Lower hips back to the floor in a controlled motion.
cues: Drive through your heels
Keep ribs stacked over hips
safetyNotes: Use padding on the bar to protect hips from pressure.

ball-leg-curl.description: Lying leg curl using a stability ball to strengthen the hamstrings.
steps: Lie on your back with legs extended and heels resting on top of the stability ball.
Raise your hips to create a straight line from shoulders to heels.
Bend your knees to roll the ball toward your glutes while keeping hips elevated.
Slowly extend your legs to roll the ball back to the starting position.
cues: Drive through your heels to initiate the curl
Keep your core tight and hips high
safetyNotes: Control the movement to avoid rolling off the ball or straining your back.

incline-dumbbell-bicep-curls.description: Incline dumbbell bicep curls isolate the biceps by curling weights while seated on an angled bench.
steps: Sit on an incline bench set to 45-60 degrees holding a dumbbell in each hand with arms hanging straight down.
Curl the dumbbells up toward your shoulders by bending your elbows.
Squeeze your biceps at the top then slowly lower the weights back to the start position.
Return to the fully extended arm position with control.
cues: Keep elbows pinned at your sides
Control the lowering phase without swinging
safetyNotes: Keep your back pressed into the bench to avoid lower back strain

barbell-bicep-curl.description: The barbell bicep curl builds arm strength by lifting a barbell from hip level to shoulder height using elbow flexion.
steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, grip the barbell underhand with hands slightly wider than shoulders
Keep elbows close to your torso and curl the barbell up toward your shoulders
Squeeze your biceps at the top of the movement
Slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position at your hips
cues: Keep elbows pinned to your sides
Control the descent without swinging
safetyNotes: Avoid using momentum or arching your back, which can strain the lower back

banded-side-step-squat.description: Banded side step squat targets glutes with lateral steps while holding a low squat position.
steps: Place a resistance band just above your knees and stand with feet hip-width apart.
Lower into a squat by bending knees and pushing hips back, keeping chest up.
Step one foot sideways to stretch the band while staying low in the squat.
Bring the other foot over to meet it then stand up to return to start.
cues: Push knees outward to maintain band tension
Stay low throughout the side steps
safetyNotes: Avoid letting knees cave inward to prevent joint strain.

crab-walk.description: Crab walk is a bodyweight exercise in a reverse tabletop position that targets the core, shoulders, and glutes.
steps: Sit on the floor with knees bent, feet flat, and hands behind hips palms down.
Lift hips off the ground to form a straight line from knees to shoulders.
Walk forward or sideways by moving opposite hand and foot together.
Continue for the target reps or time then lower hips back to the floor.
cues: Keep hips lifted and level
Move opposite limbs at the same time
safetyNotes: Avoid letting hips sag to prevent lower back strain.

barbell-bent-over-row.description: The barbell bent over row builds back strength by hinging at the hips and pulling the bar to your torso.
steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, bend at the hips, and grip the barbell with an overhand grip slightly wider than your shoulders.
Hinge forward until your torso is nearly parallel to the floor with a flat back and slight knee bend.
Pull the barbell toward your lower chest by driving elbows back and squeezing shoulder blades together.
Lower the barbell with control back to the starting position while maintaining your hinged posture.
cues: Keep your back flat and core braced
Lead with your elbows and squeeze at the top
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding your lower back to reduce strain risk.

barbell-shoulder-press.description: The barbell shoulder press is an overhead lift that targets the shoulders by pressing a barbell from shoulder height to full arm extension.
steps: Set the barbell on a rack at shoulder height and stand with feet shoulder-width apart.
Grip the bar with hands slightly wider than shoulders, palms forward, and unrack it to rest on your upper chest.
Press the bar straight overhead until your arms are fully extended.
Lower the bar with control back to shoulder height.
cues: Brace your core to keep your torso stable
Press the bar in a straight line over your head
safetyNotes: Avoid excessive arching in your lower back

barbell-deadlift.description: The barbell deadlift builds hamstring and posterior chain strength by lifting a loaded barbell from the floor to hip height.
steps: Stand with feet hip-width apart, bar over mid-foot, and grip just outside the legs.
Hinge at the hips with slight knee bend, flat back, and chest up to grasp the bar.
Drive through the heels to extend hips and knees while keeping the bar close to the body.
Lower the bar by hinging hips back and bending knees until it returns to the floor.
cues: Keep the bar close to your shins and thighs
Brace core and maintain neutral spine
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding the lower back to reduce strain risk.

barbell-shrugs.description: Barbell shrugs strengthen the trapezius by lifting the shoulders upward with a barbell held in front of the body.
steps: Stand with feet hip-width apart and grip the barbell overhand, hands just outside your thighs.
Lift the bar to hang at arm's length in front of your thighs with a slight knee bend.
Shrug your shoulders straight up toward your ears, keeping arms fully extended.
Pause at the top, then slowly lower the bar back to the starting position.
cues: Keep your arms straight and avoid bending elbows
Shrug vertically without rolling shoulders
safetyNotes: Use controlled movements to prevent straining the neck or lower back

barbell-squat.description: The barbell squat is a compound lift that primarily targets the quadriceps through knee and hip extension.
steps: Set the barbell on a rack at shoulder height, step under it, and rest it across your upper back.
Grip the bar, unrack it, and stand with feet about shoulder-width apart and toes slightly turned out.
Brace your core, then bend at the hips and knees to lower until thighs are at least parallel to the floor.
Drive through your heels to extend your hips and knees and return to standing.
cues: Keep chest up and core braced
Push knees out in line with toes
safetyNotes: Use safety bars or a spotter for heavy loads to prevent being pinned if form breaks.

chest-press-machine.description: The chest press machine targets the chest by pushing handles forward from a seated position.
steps: Sit with back flat against the pad, feet on the floor, and handles at mid-chest height.
Grip handles with palms forward and elbows bent near 90 degrees.
Push handles forward until arms are extended without locking elbows.
Slowly return handles to the starting position.
cues: Keep back pressed into the pad
Squeeze chest at full extension
safetyNotes: Avoid locking elbows to prevent joint strain.

cable-overhead-tricep-extension.description: Cable overhead tricep extension isolates the triceps by extending the arms overhead against cable resistance.
steps: Attach rope to low pulley and stand with back to cable machine.
Grip rope overhead with elbows bent and pointing forward.
Extend arms fully by straightening elbows while keeping upper arms still.
Slowly bend elbows to return to the overhead starting position.
cues: Keep elbows tucked close to head
Brace core to avoid arching lower back
safetyNotes: Use controlled weight to prevent shoulder strain from poor form.

chin-up.description: Chin-ups are a bodyweight exercise where you hang from a bar with an underhand grip and pull up until your chin clears the bar, targeting the back.
steps: Grip the bar with palms facing you and hands shoulder-width apart, then hang with arms fully extended.
Engage your core and pull your body up by driving elbows down until chin passes the bar.
Pause briefly at the top position.
Lower yourself with control back to the full hang starting position.
cues: Keep core tight to avoid swinging
Pull elbows down and back, not just with arms
safetyNotes: Avoid momentum or kipping swings to reduce shoulder strain risk.

bodyweight-squat.description: The bodyweight squat targets the quadriceps by lowering the hips back and down then standing up using only body weight.
steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Push hips back and bend knees to lower until thighs are parallel to the floor or as low as comfortable.
Keep chest up and weight in heels throughout the descent.
Drive through heels to extend legs and return to standing.
cues: Sit back as if into a chair
Knees track over toes
safetyNotes: Avoid letting knees cave inward to reduce joint stress.

dumbbell-incline-flys.description: Dumbbell incline flys target the upper chest with a controlled fly motion on an inclined bench.
steps: Sit on an incline bench set to 30-45 degrees and hold a dumbbell in each hand with palms facing inward.
Lie back and press the dumbbells up above your chest with a slight bend in the elbows.
Lower the weights slowly in a wide arc out to the sides until you feel a stretch in your chest.
Squeeze your chest to bring the dumbbells back together above your chest and return to the start position.
cues: Keep a slight bend in your elbows the whole time.
Control the descent and focus on chest stretch and squeeze.
safetyNotes: Avoid locking elbows or using momentum to swing the weights up.

dumbbell-lateral-raise.description: Dumbbell lateral raise targets the side shoulder muscles by lifting weights out to the sides from a standing position.
steps: Stand with feet hip-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides with palms facing in.
Slightly bend your elbows and raise the weights out to the sides until arms are parallel to the floor.
Pause briefly at the top then lower the dumbbells slowly back to the starting position.
cues: Lead with elbows not hands
Keep a slight bend in elbows
safetyNotes: Avoid swinging or using momentum to lift the weights.

dumbbell-fly.description: Dumbbell fly isolates the chest by lying on a bench and moving dumbbells outward in a wide arc.
steps: Lie on your back on a flat bench holding a dumbbell in each hand above your chest with elbows slightly bent.
Lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a controlled arc until you feel a stretch in your chest.
Squeeze your chest to bring the dumbbells back together above your chest.
Return to the starting position and repeat for reps.
cues: Keep elbows slightly bent and fixed
Focus on chest squeeze at the top
safetyNotes: Do not lower elbows below bench level to avoid shoulder strain.

dumbbell-overhead-tricep-extensions.description: Dumbbell overhead tricep extension isolates and strengthens the triceps by extending the elbows while holding weight overhead.
steps: Stand or sit tall and hold one dumbbell with both hands, arms fully extended overhead.
Bend elbows to lower the dumbbell behind your head, keeping upper arms close to your ears.
Straighten elbows to press the dumbbell back up to the starting position.
Repeat for reps then lower the dumbbell safely to your chest.
cues: Keep elbows tucked and pointing forward
Move only at the elbows, not the shoulders
safetyNotes: Brace your core to avoid arching your lower back.

dumbbell-static-lunges.description: Dumbbell static lunges target the quadriceps by holding a split stance and lowering the back knee toward the floor.
steps: Hold a dumbbell in each hand and stand tall with feet together.
Step one foot backward into a split stance with feet hip-width apart.
Bend both knees to lower until the back knee nearly touches the floor.
Drive through the front heel to return to the starting split stance.
cues: Keep torso upright with chest lifted
Front knee tracks over the ankle
safetyNotes: Avoid letting the front knee cave inward.

floor-back-extensions.description: Prone bodyweight move lying face down that lifts the chest and legs to target the lower back muscles.
steps: Lie face down on the floor with arms extended forward or by your sides and forehead resting on the ground.
Engage your core and squeeze your glutes to lift your chest and thighs a few inches off the floor.
Hold briefly at the top while keeping your neck neutral and gaze down.
Lower your chest and legs back to the floor in a slow, controlled motion.
cues: Squeeze your lower back and glutes at the top
Keep ribs and hips stacked without overarching
safetyNotes: Avoid lifting too high or jerking to prevent lower back strain

goblet-squat-heels-elevated.description: A quad-focused squat variation with heels elevated on a platform while holding a weight at chest height.
steps: Hold a dumbbell or kettlebell at chest level with both hands and stand with heels raised on a stable platform.
Brace your core, then bend at the hips and knees to lower until thighs are near parallel to the floor.
Drive through your feet to stand back up to the starting position.
Reset your stance and repeat the movement while keeping control.
cues: Keep chest tall and elbows tucked in
Drive knees outward over toes
safetyNotes: Use a stable platform under the heels to prevent slipping or imbalance.

leg-curl-machine.description: The leg curl machine isolates and strengthens the hamstrings by bending the knees against resistance.
steps: Lie face down on the machine with legs extended and ankles under the padded lever.
Adjust the pad to rest on your lower calves just above the heels.
Bend your knees to curl the pad toward your glutes in a controlled motion.
Slowly lower the weight back to the starting position with legs extended.
cues: Keep hips pressed firmly into the pad
Squeeze hamstrings at the top before lowering
safetyNotes: Avoid lifting hips off the bench to prevent lower back strain.

hyperextensions.description: Hyperextensions are performed on a bench to strengthen the lower back by extending the torso upward from a hinged position.
steps: Position yourself face down on the hyperextension bench with hips supported and feet secured under the pads.
Cross arms over chest and keep body aligned from head to heels.
Hinge at the hips to lower torso toward the floor until a stretch is felt in the lower back.
Raise torso back to start by contracting the lower back muscles until body is straight.
cues: Keep neck neutral and gaze down throughout the movement.
Control the descent without using momentum.
safetyNotes: Avoid hyperextending the spine at the top to reduce lower back strain.

preacher-curls-machine.description: The preacher curl machine isolates the biceps by fixing your upper arms on a padded support.
steps: Adjust the seat so your upper arms rest flat on the pad with elbows aligned.
Grip the handles palms up, sit tall, and press chest into the support.
Curl the handles toward your shoulders by bending only at the elbows.
Lower the handles slowly back to the start position with full control.
cues: Keep upper arms glued to the pad the entire time.
Control the lowering phase without locking elbows.
safetyNotes: Avoid swinging or using momentum to lift the weight.

facepull.description: Face pulls are a cable exercise targeting the rear shoulders and upper back to improve posture and shoulder stability.
steps: Attach a rope to a cable pulley at face height and grip it with both hands, palms facing in.
Step back until arms are extended with tension on the cable and feet shoulder-width apart.
Pull the rope toward your face by driving elbows back and out while squeezing shoulder blades.
Slowly extend arms forward to return to the starting position with control.
cues: Lead with elbows outward
Squeeze upper back at the end
safetyNotes: Use light weight and avoid momentum to prevent shoulder strain from poor form.

narrow-grip-seated-cable-row.description: The narrow grip seated cable row targets the mid-back and lats by pulling a close-grip handle toward the torso while seated.
steps: Sit on the bench with feet on the platform, knees slightly bent, and grab the narrow handle with arms extended.
Keep torso upright and pull the handle to your lower chest while driving elbows back.
Squeeze shoulder blades together at the end position.
Slowly extend arms to return the handle to the start with control.
cues: Keep your back straight and core braced
Pull elbows back instead of yanking with the hands
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding the lower back by staying tall throughout the row.

rack-pull.description: A rack pull is a partial deadlift from knee-height rack pins that builds strength in the glutes and back.
steps: Set the bar on rack pins at knee height and stand with feet hip-width apart.
Grip the bar overhand just outside your legs and brace your core.
Drive through your heels to extend hips and knees until standing tall.
Lower the bar back to the pins in a controlled motion.
cues: Keep spine neutral and chest lifted.
Squeeze glutes at full lockout.
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding the lower back to reduce strain risk.

reverse-deltoid-fly-machine.description: Seated machine move that isolates the rear shoulders by pulling handles backward in a reverse fly pattern.
steps: Sit facing the machine and adjust the seat so handles are at shoulder height with chest supported.
Grab handles with neutral grip and elbows slightly bent.
Pull handles back by squeezing shoulder blades together without shrugging.
Slowly return handles to start position with control.
cues: Lead with elbows and squeeze rear shoulders at the back.
Keep chest tall and avoid rounding your upper back.
safetyNotes: Avoid momentum or swinging to protect shoulder joints.

push-up.description: Bodyweight push-up that builds chest, shoulder, and tricep strength by lowering and pressing from a plank position.
steps: Place hands shoulder-width apart on the floor and extend legs back into a straight plank.
