Bend elbows to lower your chest toward the floor while keeping your body straight.
Press through your palms to straighten your arms and return to the plank position.
Repeat for the target number of reps.
cues: Keep body straight from head to heels
Lower with control and drive up explosively
safetyNotes: Avoid letting hips sag to prevent lower back strain.

seated-calf-raise-machine.description: The seated calf raise machine targets the calves by raising and lowering a weighted pad with your toes while seated.
steps: Sit on the machine with balls of feet on the platform edge and thighs under the knee pads.
Grip handles and keep back flat against the support.
Push through the balls of feet to raise heels as high as possible.
Slowly lower heels back below platform level to return to start.
cues: Rise fully onto toes without locking knees
Control the descent to avoid bouncing
safetyNotes: Avoid dropping heels too low if it strains the Achilles tendon.

barbell-skullcrushers.description: Barbell skullcrushers isolate the triceps by lying on a bench and lowering then pressing a barbell toward the forehead.
steps: Lie on your back on a flat bench and grip the barbell with hands shoulder-width apart above your chest.
Bend your elbows to lower the barbell toward your forehead with control.
Extend your elbows to press the barbell back up to the starting position.
Repeat for reps then rack the bar safely.
cues: Keep elbows tucked and stationary throughout the movement.
Lower with control and avoid locking out at the top.
safetyNotes: Do not let elbows flare outward to reduce shoulder stress.

shoulder-press-machine.description: A machine exercise that strengthens the shoulders by pressing handles overhead from a seated position.
steps: Adjust the seat so the handles are level with your shoulders and sit with your back against the pad.
Grip the handles with palms facing forward and elbows bent.
Press the handles upward until your arms are fully extended.
Lower the handles slowly back to the starting position.
cues: Keep your back flat against the pad and core braced.
Exhale as you press up and avoid locking your elbows.
safetyNotes: Do not arch your lower back; keep it supported by the seat.

leg-press.description: The leg press is a seated machine exercise where you push a weighted platform away with your legs, primarily targeting the quadriceps.
steps: Sit on the machine with your back flat against the pad and feet placed shoulder-width apart on the platform.
Release the safety handles and lower the platform by bending your knees to about 90 degrees.
Drive through your heels to extend your legs and push the platform back to the start position.
Re-engage the safety handles once the set is complete.
cues: Drive through your heels
Keep knees aligned with toes
safetyNotes: Avoid locking your knees at the top to reduce joint stress.

smith-machine-squats.description: Smith Machine Squats use a guided bar on a fixed track to target the quadriceps with added stability for beginners.
steps: Set the bar at shoulder height on the Smith machine and stand with feet shoulder-width apart beneath it.
Position the bar across your upper back and unlock it by rotating the handles.
Bend at the knees and hips to lower until thighs are parallel to the floor.
Drive through your heels to stand back up to the starting position.
cues: Keep chest up and core tight
Drive through heels with knees tracking over toes
safetyNotes: Set safety stops at a height that catches the bar if you lose control.
Avoid letting knees cave inward or rounding the lower back.

seated-dumbbell-shoulder-press.description: The seated dumbbell shoulder press strengthens the shoulders by pressing weights overhead from a supported seated position.
steps: Sit upright on a bench with back support, feet flat on the floor, holding a dumbbell in each hand at shoulder height with palms facing forward.
Press the dumbbells straight upward until your arms are fully extended overhead.
Lower the dumbbells slowly back to shoulder height with control.
Repeat for the desired reps then return the weights to your sides.
cues: Keep your back pressed into the bench and core tight
Press straight up without locking your elbows
safetyNotes: Avoid excessive arching in your lower back during the press.

standing-calf-raise-machine.description: The standing calf raise machine strengthens the calves by raising your heels against resistance while standing.
steps: Adjust shoulder pads to your height and stand with balls of feet on the platform edge.
Position shoulders under pads, grip handles, and unlock the machine.
Push through the balls of feet to raise heels as high as possible.
Lower heels slowly below platform level in a controlled motion.
cues: Keep knees slightly bent throughout
Pause briefly at the top for full contraction
safetyNotes: Avoid bouncing at the bottom to prevent tendon strain

single-leg-leg-press-machine.description: Single-leg leg press machine isolates and strengthens the quadriceps of one leg through controlled pushing.
steps: Sit with your back flat against the pad and place one foot centered on the platform.
Release the safety handles and lower the platform until your knee bends to about 90 degrees.
Push the platform away by extending your leg until it is nearly straight.
Slowly bend your knee to return the platform to the starting position under control.
cues: Drive through your heel
Keep your lower back pressed into the seat
safetyNotes: Avoid locking your knee at the top to prevent joint strain.

step-up.description: The step-up targets the quadriceps by stepping onto an elevated platform and driving up to stand.
steps: Stand facing a stable step or platform with feet hip-width apart, holding weights if desired.
Place one foot fully onto the step.
Drive through the heel of the lead foot to lift your body up until both feet are on the step.
Step back down starting with the trailing foot, then the lead foot, returning to the start position.
cues: Drive through the heel of the lead foot
Keep chest up and core braced
safetyNotes: Use a stable platform to avoid slipping or tipping.

static-barbell-lunge.description: The static barbell lunge builds quad strength by holding a barbell on your back while lunging in a fixed stance.
steps: Position the barbell on your upper back and shoulders, gripping it firmly with both hands.
Step one foot forward into a split stance with feet hip-width apart.
Lower your body until both knees are bent at about 90 degrees.
Drive through your front heel to return to the starting position.
cues: Keep your torso upright throughout the movement
Ensure your front knee stays aligned over your ankle
safetyNotes: Do not let your front knee collapse inward to avoid knee strain.

straight-arm-pulldown.description: Straight arm pulldown is a cable exercise that targets the lats and upper back with fully extended arms.
steps: Stand facing a high cable pulley with a straight bar attached, feet shoulder-width apart.
Grasp the bar with an overhand grip and step back so arms are fully extended overhead.
Pull the bar down to your thighs by contracting your lats while keeping elbows locked straight.
Slowly raise the bar back to the starting position above your head with control.
cues: Keep arms completely straight throughout the movement
Lead with your elbows pointing down and back
safetyNotes: Avoid leaning backward or using momentum to prevent lower back strain

standing-single-leg-calf-raise.description: A standing exercise where you raise your heel on one leg to target and strengthen the calf muscles.
steps: Position yourself standing on one leg with the ball of your foot on the edge of a step or raised platform.
Lower your heel down slowly below the platform to stretch the calf.
Drive through the ball of your foot to lift your heel as high as you can.
Lower your heel back to the starting position in a controlled manner.
cues: Keep your standing leg knee straight
Focus on full range of motion without bouncing
safetyNotes: Use a wall or support for balance if you feel unstable.

sumo-deadlift.description: The sumo deadlift uses a wide stance to lift a barbell from the floor, primarily targeting the hamstrings.
steps: Stand with feet wider than shoulder-width, toes pointed out 30-45 degrees, and bar over mid-foot.
Hinge at hips and bend knees to grip the bar inside your legs with straight arms and flat back.
Brace core, lift chest, and drive through heels to stand tall while pushing knees outward.
Hinge hips back and bend knees to lower the bar controlled to the floor.
cues: Push knees out over toes
Keep back flat and chest up
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding the lower back to reduce spine strain risk.

superman.description: The Superman strengthens your back by lying face down and lifting arms and legs off the ground.
steps: Lie face down on the floor with arms extended forward and legs straight.
Engage your core and lift arms, chest, and legs a few inches off the ground.
Hold the lifted position for the desired duration while breathing steadily.
Lower arms and legs slowly back to the starting position.
cues: Look down to keep your neck neutral.
Squeeze glutes and lower back as you lift.
safetyNotes: Avoid lifting too high to prevent lower back strain.

tricep-dips-machine.description: Seated machine tricep dips build triceps strength by pressing handles downward through elbow extension.
steps: Adjust the seat so handles are at upper-chest level and sit with back flat against the pad.
Grip the handles with palms facing down and elbows tucked close to your sides.
Press the handles down by straightening your arms until they are fully extended.
Slowly bend your elbows to return the handles to the starting position with control.
cues: Keep elbows pinned to your sides
Control the lowering phase without momentum
safetyNotes: Avoid locking elbows at full extension to reduce joint stress.

tricep-dips-bench.description: Bench tricep dips build arm strength by lowering and raising your body using a stable edge like a chair.
steps: Sit on the edge of a sturdy bench or chair with hands gripping the sides beside your hips.
Slide hips off the edge and extend legs forward with heels on the floor.
Bend elbows to lower your body until upper arms are about parallel to the floor.
Straighten arms to push back up to the starting position.
cues: Keep elbows pointing straight back
Lower with control and avoid shrugging shoulders
safetyNotes: Use only a stable non-slip surface to prevent falling

up-and-down-plank.description: Dynamic plank that moves between high and forearm positions to build core stability and upper body strength.
steps: Start in high plank with hands under shoulders and body straight from head to heels.
Lower one forearm to the floor then the other to reach forearm plank position.
Push up one hand at a time to return to high plank.
Repeat the sequence alternating which arm leads each rep.
cues: Keep hips level and core braced the whole time.
Move slowly with control and avoid rocking side to side.
safetyNotes: Do not let your lower back sag or hips twist; maintain a rigid body line.

tricep-pushdown-bar.description: Tricep pushdown with a bar on a cable machine isolates and strengthens the triceps through elbow extension.
steps: Attach a straight bar to the high pulley, grip overhand with hands shoulder-width, and stand facing the machine with elbows tucked at sides.
Keep upper arms stationary and push the bar down by straightening your elbows until arms are fully extended.
Squeeze the triceps at the bottom position for a brief pause.
Slowly bend your elbows to return the bar to the starting position with control.
cues: Keep elbows pinned to your sides the entire time.
Control the return without letting the weight pull you forward.
safetyNotes: Avoid swinging your torso or using momentum to move the bar.

v-bar-pulldown.description: The V-bar pulldown is a cable machine exercise that targets the back muscles by pulling a V-shaped bar down to the chest from a seated position.
steps: Adjust the thigh pads so your legs are secure with feet flat on the floor.
Grasp the V-bar with a neutral grip and sit tall with chest up.
Pull the bar down to your upper chest by driving elbows down and back.
Slowly extend your arms to return the bar to the starting position.
cues: Keep torso upright and avoid leaning back.
Squeeze shoulder blades together at the bottom.
safetyNotes: Control the weight on the return to avoid using momentum.

tricep-push-up.description: A narrow-grip push-up variation that targets the triceps more than standard push-ups.
steps: Start in a high plank with hands close together under your chest, forming a diamond with thumbs and index fingers.
Bend elbows to lower your chest toward the floor while keeping elbows tucked close to your sides.
Press through your palms to straighten your arms and return to the starting plank position.
Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels throughout each rep.
cues: Keep elbows tucked close to your sides
Engage your core to maintain a straight body line
safetyNotes: Drop to your knees if wrists or shoulders feel strained.

wall-sit.description: An isometric hold with back flat against a wall and thighs parallel to floor, targeting the quadriceps and lower body.
steps: Stand with back against a wall and feet shoulder-width apart.
Slide down until thighs are parallel to the floor and knees bent at 90 degrees.
Hold the position for the desired duration while breathing steadily.
Push through heels to straighten legs and return to standing.
cues: Keep lower back pressed flat against the wall
Align knees over ankles without letting them cave inward
safetyNotes: Stop immediately if you feel sharp knee pain or joint discomfort.

plank-289.description: The plank is an isometric hold that targets the core abdominals by maintaining a straight body line on forearms and toes.
steps: Start on all fours then lower onto your forearms with elbows under shoulders.
Extend your legs back and balance on your toes to form a straight line from head to heels.
Engage your core and hold the position for the target duration.
Lower your knees to the floor to return to the starting position.
cues: Keep hips level without sagging or piking up
Breathe steadily and look down to maintain a neutral neck
safetyNotes: Avoid letting your lower back sag which can strain the spine.

wide-grip-seated-cable-row.description: Wide grip seated cable row pulls a wide bar toward the torso while seated, targeting the upper back, lats, and rhomboids.
steps: Sit on the bench with feet flat on the platform and grab the wide bar with an overhand grip wider than shoulder width.
Lean slightly forward at the hips with a straight back and arms fully extended.
Pull the bar to your lower chest by driving elbows back and squeezing shoulder blades together.
Slowly extend your arms to return to the starting position with control.
cues: Keep chest lifted and back straight throughout
Pull elbows back rather than yanking with hands
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding the lower back or using momentum to swing the weight.

wide-grip-lat-pulldown.description: Wide grip lat pulldown uses a cable machine to pull a wide bar down to your upper chest, targeting the back and lat muscles.
steps: Sit at the machine and adjust the thigh pads so your legs are secured with feet flat on the floor.
Reach up and grab the bar with a wide overhand grip, hands wider than shoulder width.
Lean back slightly and pull the bar down to your upper chest by driving elbows down and back.
Slowly extend your arms to return the bar to the starting position with control.
cues: Keep chest lifted and pull through your elbows, not your hands.
Squeeze shoulder blades together at the bottom of the movement.
safetyNotes: Avoid swinging your torso or using momentum to prevent lower back strain.
Do not pull the bar behind your neck to reduce shoulder stress.

hip-taps-293.description: Hip taps are a plank-based core move that targets the abdominals by alternating hip dips for stability.
steps: Start in forearm plank with elbows under shoulders and body straight from head to heels.
Brace core and slowly dip one hip toward the floor.
Return to plank position with control.
Repeat the dip on the opposite side.
cues: Keep hips level and avoid excessive rocking.
Brace core to maintain a neutral spine.
safetyNotes: Avoid letting lower back sag to prevent strain.

leg-lowers-296.description: Leg lowers target the abdominals by lying on your back and slowly lowering extended legs toward the floor while keeping the lower back flat.
steps: Lie on your back with legs extended straight up toward the ceiling and arms at your sides.
Engage your core to press your lower back firmly into the floor.
Slowly lower both legs toward the floor as far as possible without arching your back.
Raise your legs back to the starting position and repeat for reps.
cues: Keep lower back pressed into the floor the entire time.
Control the descent using your abs only.
safetyNotes: Stop immediately if your lower back arches or you feel back pain.

basic-crunch-290.description: The basic crunch targets the abdominal muscles by lifting the upper torso off the floor while lying on your back.
steps: Lie on your back with knees bent, feet flat on the floor, and hands lightly behind your head.
Engage your core and lift your shoulders off the ground by curling your upper body forward.
Pause briefly at the top with shoulder blades raised, then slowly lower back down.
Return to the starting position with control and repeat for reps.
cues: Keep your lower back pressed into the floor
Lift using your abs without pulling on your neck
Exhale as you curl up
safetyNotes: Avoid jerking or using momentum to prevent neck and lower back strain.

bicycle-crunches-294.description: Bicycle crunches target the abdominals with alternating leg extensions and torso twists.
steps: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat, hands lightly behind your head.
Lift shoulders off the ground and draw one knee toward your chest.
Twist to bring the opposite elbow toward the raised knee while extending the other leg.
Alternate sides in a smooth pedaling motion and return to the start position.
cues: Press lower back into the floor throughout
Move slowly without yanking your neck
safetyNotes: Avoid pulling on your head with your hands to prevent neck strain.

russian-twist-sb-295.description: Seated on a stability ball, this core move targets the abdominals with controlled side-to-side torso twists.
steps: Sit on the stability ball with feet flat on the floor and knees bent.
Lean back slightly, keeping your spine neutral and core braced.
Clasp hands at chest level and rotate torso to one side.
Return to center then rotate to the opposite side before sitting upright.
cues: Keep core tight to stay balanced on the ball.
Twist from the torso, not the arms or shoulders.
safetyNotes: Move slowly to maintain control and avoid losing balance.

weighted-crunches-298.description: Weighted crunches add resistance to standard crunches to strengthen the abdominal muscles.
steps: Lie on your back with knees bent, feet flat, and hold a weight on your chest.
Engage your core and lift your shoulders off the floor by curling upward.
Pause briefly at the top then lower slowly back to the start position.
Repeat for the desired number of reps.
cues: Keep lower back pressed into the floor
Exhale on the way up and control the descent
safetyNotes: Avoid pulling on your neck or using momentum to prevent strain.

alternating-bench-lower-leg-lifts-with-g.description: Lie on a bench and alternate lifting each leg while raising hips to target the core.
steps: Lie on your back on a bench with legs extended and heels off the edge.
Engage core then lift one leg while raising hips off the bench.
Lower leg and hips with control then switch to the opposite leg.
Continue alternating for the set number of reps.
cues: Keep lower back pressed into the bench
Squeeze glutes at the top of each raise
safetyNotes: Avoid arching the back to reduce strain risk.

mountain-climbers-301.description: Mountain climbers are a dynamic plank-based move that targets the core abdominals with added cardio.
steps: Start in a high plank with hands under shoulders and body straight from head to heels.
Drive one knee toward your chest while keeping hips low.
Quickly switch legs, extending the first leg back as the other knee comes forward.
Continue alternating at a steady pace for the target reps.
Return to the high plank position to finish.
cues: Brace your core to keep hips stable
Keep shoulders over wrists
safetyNotes: Avoid letting your lower back sag by maintaining core tension.

alternating-toe-touch-300.description: Alternating toe touch is a core exercise targeting the abdominals by reaching opposite hand to foot from a lying position.
steps: Lie on your back with legs extended straight and arms reaching overhead.
Lift one leg toward the ceiling and reach the opposite hand toward its toes.
Lower the leg and arm back to the starting position with control.
Repeat on the opposite side, alternating for the desired number of reps.
cues: Keep lower back pressed into the floor
Reach with control and avoid momentum
safetyNotes: Move slowly to protect your lower back and avoid straining your neck.

plank-jack-302.description: Plank jacks are a dynamic move where you jump your feet in and out from a plank to target the core abdominals.
steps: Start in a high plank with hands under shoulders and body straight from head to heels.
Brace your core and jump both feet out wide to the sides.
Quickly jump your feet back together to the starting position.
Continue jumping in and out for the full duration while keeping hips level.
cues: Keep hips stable and avoid rocking side to side.
Brace your abs tight throughout the movement.
safetyNotes: Do not let your lower back sag or hips rise too high during jumps.

plank-push-303.description: Plank Push builds core strength by holding a forearm plank while pushing into the floor to create tension.
steps: Start on forearms with elbows under shoulders and body in a straight line from head to heels.
Engage your core and press forearms firmly into the floor.
Hold the position while maintaining even tension for the target duration.
Release the push and lower to rest.
cues: Keep hips level and avoid sagging
Push the floor away to stay rigid
safetyNotes: Do not let lower back arch or hips drop to prevent strain.

table-top-knee-taps-305.description: Tabletop knee taps is a core exercise from a lying position with legs at 90 degrees that targets the abdominals through alternating controlled foot taps.
steps: Lie on your back with arms at your sides and knees bent.
Lift both legs into tabletop position with knees at 90 degrees and shins parallel to the floor.
Slowly lower one foot to tap the floor while keeping the other leg stable.
Return the foot to tabletop and repeat with the opposite leg.
cues: Press lower back into the floor throughout
Move slowly and keep core braced
safetyNotes: Avoid arching the lower back; stop if you feel strain there.

deep-core-sl-knee-to-elbow-306.description: Single-leg core exercise drawing the knee to the elbow while bracing deep abdominals from a supine position.
steps: Lie on your back with legs extended and arms overhead or at sides.
Brace your deep core and draw one knee toward the opposite elbow.
Pause briefly at the top position with control.
Slowly return the leg to the starting position.
cues: Keep lower back pressed into the floor
Move slowly without momentum
safetyNotes: Avoid arching the lower back during the movement.

elbows-to-knees-sit-up-291.description: A core exercise where you lie on your back and lift your torso to bring elbows toward bent knees, targeting the abdominals.
steps: Lie on your back with knees bent, feet flat on the floor, and hands lightly behind your head.
Engage your core to lift your shoulders off the ground.
Bring your elbows toward your knees in a smooth motion.
Lower your upper body back down to the starting position with control.
cues: Keep lower back pressed into the floor.
Exhale as you lift and avoid pulling on your neck.
safetyNotes: Do not yank your head forward with your hands to reduce neck strain risk.

deep-core-rainbow-side-taps-308.description: A core exercise lying on your back where legs move in a controlled rainbow arc side to side tapping the floor to target deep abdominals.
steps: Lie on your back with knees bent and shins parallel to the floor in tabletop position.
Press your lower back into the floor and engage your deep core.
Slowly arc both legs together to one side until feet tap the floor.
Return through center to the starting position and repeat on the opposite side.
cues: Keep lower back pressed down throughout
Move slowly with control and no momentum
safetyNotes: Stop if you feel any lower back strain or arching

pilates-lower-leg-lifts-309.description: Pilates lower leg lifts target the lower abdominals by lifting and lowering straight legs while lying on your back.
steps: Lie on your back with legs extended straight, arms by your sides, and lower back pressed into the floor.
Engage your core and slowly lift both legs a few inches off the ground.
Hold briefly at the top, then lower the legs with control without letting them touch the floor.
Repeat the lift and lower for the desired number of reps.
cues: Keep lower back pressed firmly into the floor
Move slowly and control the descent
safetyNotes: Stop if your lower back arches or you feel strain in your back or hips.

reverse-crunch-304.description: The reverse crunch targets the lower abs by lifting the hips toward the chest while lying on your back.
steps: Lie on your back with knees bent at 90 degrees and feet lifted off the floor, hands by your sides.
Engage your core and press your lower back into the floor.
Lift your hips off the ground by bringing your knees toward your chest.
Slowly lower your hips back to the starting position with control.
cues: Press lower back into the floor throughout
Lift with your abs, not momentum or legs
safetyNotes: Keep movements slow to avoid straining the lower back or using momentum.

lower-leg-sb-twists-311.description: Lower leg SB twists use a stability ball between the legs to rotate the hips and strengthen the core abdominals.
steps: Lie on your back with a stability ball held between your lower legs and knees bent at 90 degrees.
Extend your arms out to the sides with palms facing up for support.
Twist your hips to slowly lower the ball toward one side while keeping shoulders on the floor.
Return to the center and repeat the twist to the opposite side.
cues: Brace your core to control the rotation
Keep lower back pressed into the floor
safetyNotes: Stop if you feel any lower back discomfort

sb-alt-dead-bug-312.description: Stability ball alternating dead bug that builds core abdominal strength and stability through controlled limb movements.
steps: Lie on your back with a stability ball supporting your upper back and shoulders, knees bent at 90 degrees.
Hold your arms straight up toward the ceiling.
Slowly extend one arm overhead while straightening the opposite leg, keeping your core braced.
Return to the start position and repeat with the opposite arm and leg.
cues: Keep ribs stacked over hips
Move slowly without arching your back
safetyNotes: Stop if you feel lower back strain and reduce range of motion

elevated-hip-bent-knee-raises-310.description: Lying on your back with feet elevated and knees bent, this move lifts the hips to target the lower abs.
steps: Lie on your back with feet on an elevated surface and knees bent to 90 degrees, arms at sides.
Brace your core and tilt your pelvis to lift hips off the ground.
Drive knees toward chest until hips are fully raised.
Lower hips slowly back to the starting position with control.
cues: Keep lower back pressed into the floor
Exhale as you lift hips
safetyNotes: Avoid using momentum or arching the back to reduce strain risk.

alternating-ankle-touch-307.description: A beginner core exercise done lying on your back where you crunch up and alternately reach to touch each ankle, targeting the abdominals.
steps: Lie on your back with knees bent, feet flat on the floor, and arms at your sides.
Engage your core and lift your head and shoulders off the ground.
Reach your right hand toward your right ankle, then switch sides to reach the left hand to the left ankle.
Alternate reaches while keeping your core tight, then lower your upper body back to the start position.
cues: Keep lower back pressed into the floor
Exhale as you reach toward each ankle
safetyNotes: Do not pull on your neck or use momentum to lift

table-top-alternatin-towel-arm-slides-31.description: Core stability move in tabletop position using towels to alternate arm slides and target the abdominals.
steps: Kneel on all fours in tabletop position with towels under your hands and back flat.
Brace your core and slowly slide one arm forward along the floor.
Return the arm to the starting position under your shoulder.
Repeat the slide with the opposite arm and continue alternating.
cues: Keep your back flat and hips level
Brace your core to prevent any rocking
safetyNotes: Stop if you feel lower-back strain or cannot keep your spine neutral.

oblique-side-crunch-313.description: The oblique side crunch targets the side abdominal muscles through controlled lateral bending while lying down.
steps: Lie on your side with knees bent and one hand behind your head.
Crunch your torso upward by contracting the obliques to lift your shoulders.
Pause briefly at the top of the movement.
Lower back down with control to return to the start position.
cues: Keep your neck neutral and avoid yanking your head.
Squeeze the obliques at the top without twisting the torso.
safetyNotes: Do not pull on your neck with your hand.

toe-touches-299.description: Toe touches are a bodyweight core exercise performed lying on your back to target the abdominal muscles.
steps: Lie flat on your back with legs extended straight up and arms reaching toward the ceiling.
Engage your core to lift your shoulders off the floor while reaching hands toward your toes.
Lower your upper body back to the starting position in a controlled manner.
cues: Keep lower back pressed into the floor
Exhale as you reach upward
safetyNotes: Avoid pulling on your neck or using momentum to prevent strain.

elevated-hip-alt-bent-knee-raises-w-kb-h.description: Holding a kettlebell while performing alternating bent-knee raises from an elevated hip position targets the core abdominals.
steps: Lie on your back with hips elevated on a stable surface and hold a kettlebell at chest level.
Bend both knees and raise one toward your chest while keeping hips lifted and stable.
Lower the leg with control and switch to raise the opposite knee.
Continue alternating legs for the set duration before returning hips to the floor.
cues: Brace core to keep hips level
Move slowly without momentum
safetyNotes: Use a stable elevated surface to avoid tipping or falling.

sb-knee-tucks-319.description: SB knee tucks use a stability ball to strengthen the core abdominals by drawing the knees toward the chest.
steps: Lie face up with the stability ball under your lower back and feet flat on the floor.
Place hands behind your head or across your chest for support.
Engage your abs to tuck knees toward your chest while rolling slightly on the ball.
Extend legs back to the starting position with control.
cues: Brace your core to keep the movement stable
Move slowly without using momentum
safetyNotes: Avoid excessive lower back arching to prevent strain.

stability-bird-dog-318.description: The stability bird dog is a core exercise on all fours that strengthens the abdominals and builds balance.
steps: Start on all fours with hands under shoulders and knees under hips.
Extend one arm forward and the opposite leg straight back while keeping your back flat.
Hold briefly, then return to the starting position.
Repeat on the other side.
cues: Keep your core braced to avoid arching your lower back.
Move slowly and keep hips level throughout.
safetyNotes: Do not let your lower back sag or twist your torso as you extend.

elevated-hip-sl-raises-w-kb-hold-317.description: Single-leg hip raise from an elevated position while holding a kettlebell to target core abdominals.
steps: Lie with upper back supported on a bench, hips lifted, and hold a kettlebell at chest level.
Extend one leg straight while keeping hips elevated and stable.
Lower the leg slowly back toward the floor without dropping the hips.
Return both feet to the starting position after the hold.
cues: Brace core to prevent hip rotation
Keep ribs stacked over hips
safetyNotes: Maintain neutral spine to avoid lower back strain

smith-machine-body-weight-prone-grip-row.description: A bodyweight row on the Smith machine using an overhand grip to target the back muscles.
steps: Set the Smith machine bar to about waist height and lie on your back directly underneath it.
Grip the bar with an overhand prone grip, hands slightly wider than shoulder width.
Keep your body straight like a plank and pull your chest toward the bar.
Lower yourself back to the starting position with control.
cues: Keep core tight and body straight
Drive elbows back and squeeze shoulder blades
safetyNotes: Avoid letting hips sag to prevent lower back strain.

smith-machine-body-weight-supinated-grip.description: Bodyweight row on Smith machine with underhand grip pulls torso to bar, targeting back muscles for beginners.
steps: Set Smith bar to waist height and lie on back underneath it.
Grip bar with palms up shoulder-width apart, arms straight.
Lift hips to form straight line from head to heels.
Pull chest to bar by driving elbows back and squeezing shoulder blades.
Lower body slowly with control to starting position.
cues: Keep body rigid like a plank throughout
Drive elbows toward hips, not upward
safetyNotes: Avoid letting hips sag to prevent lower back strain

sb-crunch-320.description: The SB crunch uses a stability ball to perform crunches that target the core abdominals.
steps: Sit on the stability ball and roll back until your lower back is supported with feet flat on the floor.
Cross arms over chest or rest hands lightly behind head without pulling.
Brace core and curl shoulders upward toward the ceiling in a controlled crunch.
Lower upper body slowly back to the starting position on the ball.
cues: Keep chin tucked to protect the neck
Exhale as you lift and squeeze abs at the top
safetyNotes: Avoid yanking on the head or neck with the hands

single-arm-weighted-row-324.description: A single-arm row that strengthens the back by pulling a weight upward from a bent-over stance.
steps: Hinge at the hips with a flat back, feet shoulder-width apart, and hold the weight in one hand while supporting with the other on your knee.
Pull the weight toward your hip by driving the elbow back and keeping it close to your body.
Squeeze the shoulder blade at the top, then lower the weight slowly to the start position.
Complete reps on one side before switching arms.
cues: Keep your back flat and core braced
Drive the elbow back rather than yanking with the hand
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding the lower back during the hinge to reduce spinal stress.

narrow-grip-lat-pulldown-326.description: Seated cable exercise using a narrow grip to pull the bar to your chest, targeting the lats and upper back.
steps: Sit and adjust thigh pads to lock your legs in place with feet flat.
Grasp the bar with a narrow overhand grip, hands close together.
Pull the bar down to your upper chest while driving elbows toward your sides.
Slowly extend your arms to return the bar to the starting position overhead.
cues: Keep chest lifted and shoulders pulled down and back.
Focus on pulling with your elbows rather than your hands.
safetyNotes: Avoid leaning back excessively or using momentum to prevent lower back strain.

dumbbell-incline-row-325.description: Dumbbell incline row builds back strength by rowing weights while lying chest-down on an incline bench.
steps: Lie face down on an incline bench with a dumbbell in each hand and arms hanging straight down.
Pull the dumbbells up toward your hips by driving your elbows back and up.
Squeeze your shoulder blades together at the top position.
Lower the dumbbells slowly back to the starting position with control.
cues: Keep your chest pressed firmly into the bench.
Lead the pull with your elbows rather than your hands.
safetyNotes: Avoid swinging or using momentum to lift the weights.

table-top-knee-taps-314.description: Tabletop knee taps strengthen the core abdominals from a stable supine position with knees bent at 90 degrees.
steps: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat, then lift feet to bring knees into tabletop position.
Place hands by your sides or on hips and press lower back into the floor.
Slowly lower one knee to tap toward the floor while keeping the opposite leg stable.
Return the knee to tabletop and alternate sides in a controlled manner.
cues: Keep lower back pressed into the floor
Move slowly without momentum
safetyNotes: Stop if you feel lower back strain or arching

flat-lying-supinated-row-329.description: Lying face-down row with palms up that pulls the elbows back to strengthen the upper back.
steps: Lie face down on the floor with legs straight and arms extended forward, palms facing up.
Keep your core tight and pull your elbows back toward your hips while squeezing your shoulder blades.
Pause at the top for a second with elbows close to your body.
Slowly lower your arms back to the starting position with control.
cues: Drive elbows back, not hands
Keep neck neutral and chest slightly lifted
safetyNotes: Avoid arching your lower back or lifting your hips off the floor.

seated-cable-rows-narrow-grip-328.description: Seated narrow-grip cable rows build mid-back strength by pulling the handle toward your torso while seated.
steps: Sit on the bench with feet on the footrests and grasp the narrow attachment with both hands.
Sit tall with a slight knee bend, arms extended, and neutral spine.
Pull the handle to your lower chest by driving elbows back and squeezing shoulder blades.
Slowly extend arms to return to the starting position.
cues: Keep torso upright and avoid leaning back.
Pull with elbows, not hands, and squeeze at the top.
safetyNotes: Do not round your lower back or swing the torso to complete reps.

single-arm-dumbbell-row-323.description: A back exercise where you pull a dumbbell toward your hip with one arm while braced on a bench.
steps: Place one knee and same-side hand on a bench, opposite foot on floor, and hold a dumbbell with arm extended.
Keep your back flat and core tight as you start with the dumbbell hanging straight down.
Pull the dumbbell up toward your hip by driving your elbow back and squeezing your shoulder blade.
Lower the dumbbell slowly back to the starting position with control.
cues: Keep your torso stable and avoid twisting.
Pull with your back, not just your arm.
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding your lower back; maintain a neutral spine to reduce strain risk.

dumbbell-lat-pull-over-330.description: The dumbbell lat pullover is done lying on a bench to pull a weight overhead and back, targeting the back muscles.
steps: Lie on your back on a flat bench with feet on the floor, gripping one dumbbell with both hands above your chest.
Keep elbows slightly bent and slowly lower the dumbbell in an arc behind your head.
Pull the dumbbell back over your chest using your back muscles to return to the start position.
Repeat the movement for the desired number of reps.
cues: Keep your lower back pressed into the bench
Focus on pulling with your back, not your arms
safetyNotes: Control the weight at all times and avoid using momentum
Stop if you feel pain in your shoulders

barbell-bicep-curls-332.description: Barbell bicep curls build arm strength by lifting a barbell with an underhand grip to target the biceps.
steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, grip the barbell underhand with hands slightly wider than shoulders, and let it rest at thigh level.
Keep elbows tucked at your sides and curl the barbell upward toward your shoulders.
Pause briefly at the top and squeeze your biceps.
Slowly lower the barbell back to the starting position at your thighs.
cues: Keep elbows pinned to your sides
Control the descent without swinging
safetyNotes: Avoid using body momentum to swing the weight up

cable-bar-bent-over-row-331.description: A cable machine bent-over row using a bar attachment to target the back by pulling weight toward the torso while hinged at the hips.
steps: Attach a straight bar to the low pulley and grip it overhand with hands shoulder-width apart.
Hinge at the hips with a flat back and slight knee bend until your torso is about 45 degrees to the floor.
Pull the bar to your lower chest by driving elbows back and squeezing shoulder blades together.
Lower the bar slowly back to the starting position with control.
cues: Keep your back flat and core braced throughout the movement.
Pull with your elbows and focus on squeezing the back muscles.
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding the lower back, which can strain the spine.

barbell-21-s-bicep-curls-334.description: Barbell 21's bicep curls combine partial and full reps to target the biceps for strength and endurance.
steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and grip the barbell underhand at shoulder width, arms extended.
Perform 7 curls from the bottom to the halfway point, keeping elbows fixed at your sides.
Complete 7 curls from the halfway point to the top position.
Finish with 7 full-range curls from bottom to top, then lower the barbell to the start.
cues: Keep elbows pinned to your sides.
Control the lowering phase without swinging.
safetyNotes: Avoid locking elbows at the bottom to reduce joint stress.

dumbbell-hammer-curls-339.description: Dumbbell hammer curls target the biceps using a neutral grip to build arm strength.
steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand palms facing inward
Keep elbows close to your torso and curl the weights toward your shoulders
Squeeze your biceps at the top of the movement
Lower the dumbbells slowly back to the starting position with control
cues: Keep upper arms stationary against your sides
Avoid swinging the weights
safetyNotes: Do not use momentum to swing the dumbbells up

seated-cable-rows-wide-grip-327.description: Seated cable row with wide grip pulls a bar to the torso while seated to target upper back muscles.
steps: Sit on the bench with feet on the platform and knees slightly bent, grasping the wide bar overhand with hands wider than shoulders.
Sit tall with chest lifted and pull the bar toward your lower chest by driving elbows back.
Squeeze shoulder blades together at the end position.
Slowly extend arms to return to the starting position with a straight back.
cues: Keep torso upright and avoid leaning back
Drive elbows toward hips to engage back
safetyNotes: Do not round your lower back or use momentum to swing the weight

stability-ball-dumbbell-preacher-curl-33.description: Kneeling behind a stability ball to perform preacher-style curls that isolate the biceps using dumbbells.
steps: Kneel behind the stability ball with your chest supported against it.
Place your upper arms on the ball and grip a dumbbell in each hand with palms facing forward.
Curl the weights up towards your shoulders by bending your elbows.
Lower the dumbbells slowly back down to full arm extension.
cues: Keep elbows fixed on the ball throughout the movement.
Control the descent to avoid momentum.
safetyNotes: Stabilize the ball firmly before starting to prevent slipping.

cable-bar-alternating-1-2-curl-full-curl.description: Cable bar exercise alternating half and full biceps curls to target the biceps with controlled range of motion.
steps: Attach a straight bar to the low pulley and stand facing the machine with feet hip-width apart.
Grip the bar underhand at shoulder width and keep elbows tucked at your sides.
Curl halfway up then lower to start for the half rep, then immediately do a full curl to the top and lower fully.
Alternate between half and full curls for all reps while maintaining upright posture.
cues: Keep elbows pinned to your sides
Control the descent on every rep
safetyNotes: Avoid swinging your torso to lift the bar

alternating-incline-bicep-curls-333.description: Alternating incline bicep curls use dumbbells on an incline bench to target the biceps with added stretch.
steps: Sit on an incline bench set to 45-60 degrees holding a dumbbell in each hand with palms facing forward and arms extended.
Keep your back pressed into the bench and upper arms stationary at your sides.
Curl one dumbbell toward your shoulder while keeping the opposite arm extended, then lower with control.
Alternate to the other arm and repeat for the target reps before returning to the starting position.
cues: Keep elbows pinned to your sides
Control the lowering phase without swinging
safetyNotes: Avoid locking elbows at the bottom to reduce joint strain

stability-ball-cable-rope-hammer-preache.description: Sit on a stability ball and curl a cable rope with hammer grip, using the ball to support upper arms like a preacher bench.
steps: Position cable pulley low and sit on stability ball facing the machine with feet planted.
Grip rope ends with neutral hammer grip and rest upper arms on the ball.
Curl rope up toward shoulders by bending elbows while keeping upper arms fixed.
Lower rope slowly back to start position with control.
cues: Keep elbows pinned against the ball
Squeeze biceps at the top without swinging
safetyNotes: Ensure the ball stays stable under you to avoid rolling or tipping.

alternating-dumbbell-bicep-curls-340.description: Alternating dumbbell bicep curls target the biceps by curling one arm at a time with dumbbells.
steps: Stand with feet hip-width apart holding a dumbbell in each hand, palms facing forward.
Curl one dumbbell toward your shoulder by bending the elbow while keeping the upper arm still.
Lower the dumbbell slowly back to the starting position.
Repeat with the opposite arm, alternating sides for the desired reps.
cues: Keep elbows tucked close to your sides
Control the lowering phase without swinging
safetyNotes: Avoid using momentum by swinging your torso.

standing-dumbbell-calf-raise-342.description: The standing dumbbell calf raise strengthens the calf muscles by lifting the heels while holding dumbbells at your sides.
steps: Stand with feet hip-width apart and hold a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
Rise onto the balls of your feet by pushing through your ankles.
Pause briefly at the top, then lower your heels slowly back to the floor.
Return to the starting position and repeat.
cues: Keep knees straight but not locked
Control the lowering phase
safetyNotes: Avoid bouncing at the bottom to prevent strain on the Achilles tendon.

stability-ball-cable-bar-preacher-curl-3.description: Biceps isolation move using a cable bar with upper arms supported on a stability ball in preacher position.
steps: Attach a straight bar to the low pulley and place a stability ball in front of the cable machine.
Sit on the ball, lean forward, and rest your upper arms on the ball with palms facing up.
Grip the bar and curl it toward your shoulders while keeping elbows fixed on the ball.
Lower the bar slowly back to the start position with control.
cues: Keep upper arms glued to the ball the entire time.
Squeeze biceps at the top without swinging.
safetyNotes: Keep the ball stable under you to avoid rolling or losing balance.

dumbbell-bench-press-343.description: The dumbbell bench press builds chest strength by pressing weights upward while lying on a bench.
steps: Lie flat on a bench with a dumbbell in each hand at chest height, feet on the floor.
Press the dumbbells upward until arms are straight above your chest.
Lower the dumbbells slowly back down to chest level with control.
Repeat for reps then return dumbbells to thighs before sitting up.
cues: Keep elbows at about 45 degrees from your body.
Press straight up over your chest while keeping shoulders stable.
safetyNotes: Avoid locking elbows at the top or flaring arms wide to reduce shoulder strain.

dumbbell-chest-fly-345.description: The dumbbell chest fly is an isolation move that targets the chest by arcing dumbbells from wide to together while lying down.
steps: Lie on your back on a flat bench holding a dumbbell in each hand with arms extended above your chest.
Lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a wide arc with a slight bend in the elbows.
Bring the dumbbells back together above your chest by squeezing the chest muscles.
Return to the starting position and repeat for the desired reps.
cues: Keep a slight bend in your elbows throughout the movement.
Squeeze your chest at the top position.
safetyNotes: Avoid lowering the weights too far below chest level to protect the shoulders.

incline-dumbbell-chest-fly-346.description: The incline dumbbell chest fly targets the upper chest by arcing dumbbells together while lying on an inclined bench.
steps: Lie back on an incline bench holding a dumbbell in each hand above your chest with a slight bend in the elbows.
Lower the dumbbells out to the sides in a controlled arc until you feel a stretch in your chest.
Raise the dumbbells back up in the same arc motion, squeezing your chest muscles at the top.
Repeat the movement for the desired reps and carefully return the weights to the starting position.
cues: Keep elbows slightly bent and fixed throughout the movement
Control the descent to avoid swinging the weights
safetyNotes: Do not lower the dumbbells past the point of comfort to protect your shoulders.

single-arm-dumbbell-chest-press-344.description: A single-arm dumbbell press performed lying on a bench that targets the chest muscles unilaterally.
steps: Lie on your back on a flat bench with feet planted on the floor and a dumbbell in one hand.
Hold the dumbbell at chest level with elbow bent and palm facing forward.
Press the dumbbell straight up until your arm is fully extended above your shoulder.
Lower the dumbbell slowly back to the starting position at your chest.
cues: Keep your core braced to avoid arching your lower back.
Drive through your chest without letting your shoulder shrug.
safetyNotes: Control the weight on the way down to protect your shoulder joint.

incline-push-up-348.description: An incline push-up targets the chest using an elevated surface like a bench to reduce difficulty for beginners.
steps: Place hands shoulder-width on a stable elevated surface with feet on the floor behind you.
Extend legs so your body forms a straight line from head to heels.
Bend elbows to lower your chest toward the surface with control.
Push through your hands to return to the starting position.
cues: Keep your body straight from head to heels.
Lower until your chest nearly touches the surface.
safetyNotes: Ensure the elevated surface is stable and secure before starting.

decline-push-up-347.description: A push-up with feet elevated on a stable surface to increase demand on the chest and shoulders.
steps: Place feet on a sturdy chair or couch and hands on floor slightly wider than shoulders.
Set up in a straight plank position from head to heels.
Bend elbows to lower chest toward the floor with control.
Push through hands to return to full arm extension.
cues: Keep core tight so hips stay level.
Lower with control and exhale on the push.
safetyNotes: Confirm the elevated surface is stable and non-slip before starting.

machine-chest-press-349.description: The machine chest press is a seated exercise using a guided machine to target the chest muscles through a forward pressing motion.
steps: Sit on the machine with your back flat against the pad and adjust the seat height so handles are level with your mid-chest.
Grip the handles with palms facing forward and place your feet flat on the floor.
Press the handles forward until your arms are extended without locking the elbows.
Slowly bend your elbows to return the handles to the starting position.
cues: Keep your back pressed firmly against the pad
Exhale as you press the handles forward
safetyNotes: Avoid arching your lower back or lifting your hips during the movement.

star-jump-351.description: Star jumps are a full-body cardio move that raises heart rate through explosive jumping.
steps: Stand with feet together and arms at your sides.
Jump up while spreading legs wide and raising arms overhead.
Land softly with knees slightly bent.
Jump back to the starting position with feet together and arms down.
cues: Keep core engaged and chest lifted.
Land on the balls of your feet to cushion impact.
safetyNotes: Clear enough space around you to avoid collisions during jumps.

butt-kicks-355.description: Butt kicks are a cardio exercise that targets the hamstrings and glutes while raising heart rate through jogging in place.
steps: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart and arms relaxed at your sides.
Jog in place while kicking your heels up toward your glutes.
Swing your arms naturally in opposition to your legs.
Continue alternating kicks for the target number of reps.
Slow to a stop and return to a standing position.
cues: Keep your core tight and posture upright
Land softly on the balls of your feet
safetyNotes: Avoid over-kicking or arching your lower back

air-squats-356.description: Air squats are a bodyweight exercise targeting the quads, glutes, and hamstrings for lower-body strength.
steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Bend at the hips and knees to lower your body as if sitting back into a chair.
Lower until your thighs are at least parallel to the floor while keeping your chest up.
Drive through your heels to push back up to the starting standing position.
cues: Keep knees tracking over toes
Maintain neutral spine with chest lifted
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding your lower back by keeping your core engaged throughout.

skaters-353.description: Skaters are side-to-side lateral jumps that raise heart rate while strengthening legs and improving balance.
steps: Stand with feet hip-width apart and knees softly bent.
Push off one foot to jump sideways, landing on the opposite foot.
Swing the trailing leg behind the landing leg for balance.
Immediately repeat to the other side, alternating in a smooth rhythm.
cues: Land softly with knee over toes
Keep chest lifted and core braced
safetyNotes: Avoid locking the knee on landing to protect joints.

180-degree-squat-354.description: A cardio squat jump with 180-degree mid-air rotation to build leg power and endurance.
steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, toes forward.
Lower into a squat by bending knees and sitting hips back.
Explode up into a jump while rotating 180 degrees in the air.
Land softly in a squat facing the opposite direction then repeat to return to start.
cues: Keep chest lifted and core braced
Land with knees tracking over toes
safetyNotes: Rotate the whole body together to avoid twisting at the knees

burpees-352.description: Burpees combine a squat, push-up, and jump into one explosive movement targeting full-body cardio and strength.
steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and squat down to place hands on the floor.
Jump feet back into a high plank position.
Lower chest for a push-up then press back up.
Jump feet forward to hands and explode upward into a jump, landing softly.
cues: Keep core tight to protect your back.
Land softly with bent knees to absorb impact.
safetyNotes: Step feet back instead of jumping if needed to reduce joint stress.

walking-lunges-359.description: Walking lunges build lower-body strength by stepping forward into alternating lunges that target the quads, glutes, and hamstrings.
steps: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart and core braced.
Step one foot forward and lower until both knees reach about 90 degrees.
Drive through the front heel to bring the back leg forward into the next lunge.
Continue alternating legs while moving forward for the target reps.
Turn around and walk back to the starting position.
cues: Keep torso upright and ribs stacked over hips
Front knee tracks over ankle, not past toes
safetyNotes: Ensure clear space to walk without tripping

cable-glute-medius-kickback-362.description: Standing cable exercise where you kick one leg back to target and strengthen the glute medius for hip stability.
steps: Attach ankle strap to low cable pulley and secure around working ankle.
Stand sideways to machine, hold frame for balance with inside hand, feet together.
Keep leg straight and kick it backward and slightly outward against resistance.
Slowly return leg to starting position with control, then switch sides.
cues: Keep hips square and avoid twisting torso
Squeeze glute at the top without arching lower back
safetyNotes: Use light weight to maintain control and avoid swinging or momentum

side-to-side-squats-357.description: Side to side squats target the lower body by stepping laterally into a squat, working quads, glutes and inner thighs.
steps: Stand with feet hip-width apart and toes forward.
Step one foot out to the side while bending both knees to lower into a squat.
Push through the heel of the stepping foot to return to center.
Repeat the movement stepping to the opposite side.
cues: Keep chest lifted and back straight.
Push knees out over toes as you squat.
safetyNotes: Avoid letting knees cave inward during the movement.

dumbbell-walking-lunges-glute-focused-36.description: Holding dumbbells at your sides, this glute-focused exercise builds lower-body strength through alternating forward walking lunges.
steps: Stand tall with a dumbbell in each hand and feet hip-width apart.
Step one foot forward and lower into a lunge with both knees near 90 degrees.
Drive through the front heel to push forward into the next lunge with the opposite leg.
Alternate legs while walking forward for the target reps then return to the start position.
cues: Keep chest up and core braced
Squeeze the glute to stand tall on each step
safetyNotes: Avoid letting the front knee cave inward or extend past the toes

barbell-sumo-deadlift-361.description: The barbell sumo deadlift uses a wide stance to target the glutes and build lower body strength.
steps: Position feet wider than shoulder-width with toes out and bar over midfoot.
Grip bar inside legs with overhand hold and lower hips while bracing core.
Drive through heels and push knees out to stand tall with hips extended.
Hinge at hips and bend knees to lower bar controlled back to the floor.
cues: Push knees outward over toes
Keep spine neutral and chest lifted
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding the lower back to reduce strain risk.

lateral-lunge-358.description: The lateral lunge steps sideways to strengthen inner thighs, glutes, and quads while building lower-body stability.
steps: Stand with feet together and hands on hips or at your sides.
Step one foot out to the side and bend that knee while keeping the other leg straight.
Lower your hips back until your thigh is about parallel to the floor.
Push through the bent leg to return to the starting position.
cues: Keep your chest lifted and torso upright.
Track your bent knee over your toes.
safetyNotes: Avoid letting the knee cave inward; start with a smaller step if needed.

cable-bar-rdl-363.description: The cable bar RDL is a cable-machine hip hinge that targets the glutes through controlled backward push of the hips.
steps: Attach a straight bar to the low pulley and stand facing the machine with feet hip-width apart.
Grip the bar overhand and step back to create light tension in the cable.
Hinge at the hips by pushing your butt straight back while keeping your back flat.
Drive hips forward to stand tall and squeeze glutes, then return to the hinged position.
cues: Keep spine neutral with ribs stacked over hips
Push hips back as if closing a door behind you
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding the lower back during the hinge to reduce strain risk.

stationary-lunge-glute-focused-365.description: A stationary lunge that targets the glutes by stepping one foot back and lowering into a split stance.
steps: Stand with feet hip-width apart.
Step one foot back into a split stance.
Lower until both knees reach about 90 degrees.
Drive through the front heel to return to standing.
cues: Keep torso upright with ribs stacked over hips.
Drive through the front heel to engage glutes.
safetyNotes: Avoid letting the front knee cave inward or pass the toes.

barbell-goodmorning-367.description: The barbell good morning is a hip hinge exercise that targets the glutes and hamstrings with a barbell across the upper back.
steps: Set the barbell on a rack at shoulder height and position it across your upper back with a secure grip.
Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and keep a slight bend in the knees.
Hinge at the hips to lower your torso forward while maintaining a flat back.
Drive hips forward to return to the upright starting position.
cues: Keep your back flat and hinge from the hips.
Keep ribs stacked over hips throughout the movement.
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding the lower back to reduce strain risk.

broad-jump-jog-back-350.description: Explosive broad jumps forward followed by jogging back to start for cardio and lower-body power.
steps: Stand with feet hip-width apart and arms at sides.
Swing arms back while bending knees, then jump forward as far as possible.
Land softly and turn to jog back to the starting point.
Reset your stance and repeat for the target reps.
cues: Swing arms forward to drive momentum
Land with soft knees to absorb impact
safetyNotes: Clear enough space to avoid tripping or hitting objects

single-leg-reverse-lunges-366.description: Single leg reverse lunges target the glutes by stepping one leg backward into a lunge position from standing.
steps: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart and core engaged.
Step one foot backward and lower until both knees reach about 90 degrees.
Drive through the front heel to push back to the starting position.
Repeat on the opposite leg.
cues: Keep torso upright and ribs stacked over hips
Front knee tracks over ankle without caving inward
safetyNotes: Focus on a fixed point ahead to maintain balance and avoid rushing the movement.

landmine-squat-369.description: A landmine squat uses an anchored barbell held at chest level to target the glutes in a controlled squat movement.
steps: Stand facing the landmine with feet shoulder-width apart and grip the bar end at chest height.
Bend knees and hips to lower into a squat while keeping chest lifted.
Drive through heels to return to the starting standing position.
cues: Keep chest up and core braced
Knees track over toes
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding your lower back during the squat.

smith-machine-goodmornings-364.description: A Smith machine good morning is a hip-hinge exercise that targets the glutes and hamstrings while strengthening the posterior chain.
steps: Position the Smith bar across your upper back with feet shoulder-width apart and a slight knee bend.
Unlock the bar and hinge at the hips by pushing them back while keeping your spine neutral.
Lower your torso until it is nearly parallel to the floor.
Drive hips forward to return to the standing start position.
cues: Keep your back flat and chest lifted
Hinge from the hips rather than bending at the waist
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding the lower back to reduce strain risk.

dumbbell-box-step-up-glute-focused-368.description: The dumbbell box step-up targets the glutes by stepping onto a raised platform while holding weights.
steps: Stand facing a stable box while holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
Place one foot fully on the box and drive through the heel to step up.
Bring the trailing foot onto the box to stand tall with both feet.
Step down first with the trailing foot then the lead foot to return to the start.
cues: Drive through the heel to engage the glutes
Keep chest up and torso upright
safetyNotes: Use a stable box that will not slip or tip over

landmine-squat-and-press-370.description: A compound move pairing a squat with an overhead press using a landmine bar to target glutes and shoulders.
steps: Secure the bar in the landmine, grip the free end at chest height, and stand with feet shoulder-width apart.
Lower into a squat by bending knees and hips while keeping chest lifted and back neutral.
Drive through heels to stand, pressing the bar straight overhead until arms are extended.
Lower the bar back to chest level and reset for the next rep.
cues: Brace core to keep spine stable
Press straight up, not forward
safetyNotes: Avoid letting knees cave inward during the squat

landmine-curtsy-lunge-372.description: Landmine curtsy lunge targets glutes by holding the bar at chest height and stepping one leg back behind the other into a crossed lunge.
steps: Stand facing the landmine with feet hip-width apart and grip the bar end with both hands at chest height.
Step one foot back and across behind the opposite leg into a curtsy position.
Lower until both knees bend deeply with your back knee near the floor.
Drive through the front heel to push back up to the starting stance.
cues: Keep chest lifted and torso upright
Drive through front heel to stand
safetyNotes: Avoid letting the front knee cave inward to protect the joint.

landmine-rdl-371.description: Landmine RDL is a hip-hinge movement using a landmine bar to target the glutes and hamstrings.
steps: Stand with feet hip-width apart, gripping the end of the landmine bar with both hands.
Hinge at the hips by pushing your glutes back while keeping your back flat.
Lower the bar until you feel a stretch in the hamstrings.
Drive hips forward to return to standing, squeezing the glutes at the top.
cues: Keep spine neutral and ribs stacked over hips
Hinge from the hips, not the lower back
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding the lower back to reduce strain risk

landmine-alternating-squat-and-rdl-373.description: Landmine movement alternating squats and Romanian deadlifts to strengthen the glutes.
steps: Stand over the landmine bar and grip the end with both hands at chest height, feet shoulder-width.
Lower into a squat by bending at the knees and hips while keeping your chest lifted.
Drive up to stand, then hinge at the hips for an RDL with a slight knee bend.
Return to the start position and alternate between the squat and RDL for each rep.
cues: Brace your core to stay stable
Hinge from the hips without rounding your back
safetyNotes: Keep a neutral spine throughout to reduce lower back strain

leg-press-high-stance-374.description: Leg press high stance targets the glutes by placing feet higher on the platform during the machine press.
steps: Sit in the leg press machine with back flat against the pad and feet placed high and shoulder-width on the platform.
Release the safety handles while keeping knees slightly bent.
Lower the platform by bending knees to about 90 degrees.
Drive through heels to extend legs back to start position without locking knees.
Re-engage safety handles after finishing reps.
cues: Push through heels to target glutes
Keep lower back pressed into the seat
safetyNotes: Avoid locking knees at the top to reduce joint stress
Control the descent and never let the platform drop quickly

power-squat-sumo-facing-in-375.description: Wide-stance sumo squat facing into the rack that targets the glutes with deep hip drive.
steps: Stand facing into the rack with feet wider than shoulders and toes turned out 30-45 degrees.
Position the barbell on your upper back and unrack it.
Lower by bending hips and knees until thighs are near parallel to the floor.
Drive through heels to stand tall and return to start.
cues: Push knees outward over toes
Keep chest lifted and ribs stacked over hips
safetyNotes: Avoid knees caving inward to reduce joint stress.

dumbbell-squat-glute-focus-376.description: Holding dumbbells at your sides, this squat targets the glutes by driving hips back and through the heels.
steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
Push hips back and bend knees to lower until thighs are roughly parallel to the floor.
Drive through heels to stand up, squeezing glutes at the top.
Return to the starting position with control.
cues: Push hips back to engage glutes
Drive through heels while keeping chest up
safetyNotes: Keep knees tracking over toes to prevent inward collapse
Maintain a neutral back by avoiding rounding at the bottom

barbell-rdl-379.description: The barbell Romanian deadlift is a hip-hinge movement that targets the glutes and hamstrings.
steps: Stand with feet hip-width apart and grip the barbell overhand just outside your legs.
Hinge at the hips by pushing your glutes back while keeping your back flat.
Lower the bar along your thighs until you feel a stretch in your hamstrings.
Drive your hips forward to stand tall and return to the starting position.
cues: Keep the bar close to your legs throughout the movement
Hinge from the hips while maintaining a neutral spine
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding your lower back; stop if form breaks down.

power-squat-goodmornings-378.description: Barbell hip hinge in wide squat stance targeting glutes and posterior chain.
steps: Set barbell on upper back with feet wider than shoulder width and toes slightly out.
Brace core and hinge at hips by pushing buttocks back while keeping chest lifted.
Lower torso until nearly parallel to floor or to comfortable depth with flat back.
Drive hips forward through heels to return to tall standing position.
cues: Keep spine neutral and ribs stacked over hips
Push hips back before bending knees
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding lower back to reduce strain risk

dumbbell-deadlift-383.description: The dumbbell deadlift is a hip-hinge movement that builds hamstring strength by lifting weights from the floor to standing.
steps: Stand with feet hip-width apart and place dumbbells on the floor just outside your feet.
Hinge at the hips with a slight knee bend to grip the dumbbells, keeping your back flat.
Drive through your heels to stand tall while squeezing your glutes at the top.
Hinge at the hips to lower the dumbbells back to the floor with control.
cues: Keep your back flat and chest lifted
Push hips back as you lower
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding your lower back during the lift to reduce strain risk.

decline-leg-press-narrow-stance-377.description: Decline leg press narrow stance uses a machine to target glutes by pushing a platform with feet close together.
steps: Sit in the machine with back flat against the pad and feet narrow on the platform shoulder-width apart.
Release the safety handles and lower the platform by bending your knees to a comfortable depth.
Push the platform away by driving through your heels until legs are nearly straight.
Control the descent back to the start position and re-engage the safety handles.
cues: Drive through heels to activate glutes
Keep knees tracking over toes
safetyNotes: Avoid locking knees at the top to protect joints
Keep lower back pressed into the pad throughout

sumo-rdl-381.description: Sumo RDL is a wide-stance hip hinge that targets the glutes through controlled lowering and driving.
steps: Stand with feet wide, toes out, and grip the bar inside your legs with flat back.
Hinge at hips to push butt back while lowering the bar down your thighs.
Lower until hamstrings stretch then drive hips forward to stand tall.
Hinge at hips again to lower the bar back to the start position.
cues: Keep back flat and chest up
Push hips back then drive them forward
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding your lower back during the hinge.

romanian-deadlift-380.description: The Romanian deadlift is a hip-hinge exercise that targets the hamstrings and glutes by lowering a barbell from hip height.
steps: Stand with feet hip-width apart holding a barbell in front of your thighs with an overhand grip.
Hinge at the hips by pushing them back while keeping a slight bend in the knees and flat back.
Lower the barbell along your legs until you feel a stretch in the hamstrings.
Drive hips forward to stand tall and return to the starting position.
cues: Push hips back like closing a door behind you
Keep the bar close to your legs and back flat
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding the lower back to prevent strain; maintain a neutral spine throughout.

nordic-hamstring-curl-384.description: Bodyweight exercise that strengthens the hamstrings by lowering and raising your torso from a kneeling position.
steps: Kneel on a padded surface with ankles secured under a stable anchor or held by a partner.
Keep torso straight from knees to head and core braced.
Slowly lower your body forward toward the floor while resisting with your hamstrings.
Push off the floor with your hands to return to the upright starting position.
cues: Keep body straight like a plank from head to knees
Control the lowering phase with your hamstrings
safetyNotes: Use hand assistance on the way up if you cannot control the full range to avoid strain.

staticbarbell-lunges-386.description: Static barbell lunges target the quadriceps by holding a barbell on the upper back in a stationary split-stance lunge.
steps: Set the barbell on your upper back and grip it firmly with both hands.
Step one foot forward into a lunge stance with feet hip-width apart.
Lower until both knees reach about 90 degrees and hold the position.
Drive through the front heel to return to standing.
cues: Keep torso upright with chest lifted
Front knee tracks over ankle, not past toes
safetyNotes: Avoid letting the front knee cave inward to reduce knee stress.

dumbbell-single-leg-deadlift-382.description: A single-leg hinge exercise holding a dumbbell that targets the hamstrings while challenging balance and stability.
steps: Stand on one leg holding a dumbbell in the opposite hand, with your knee slightly bent.
Hinge at the hips and extend your free leg straight back for balance.
Lower the dumbbell toward the floor while keeping your back flat and hips square.
Drive through your standing heel to return to the upright starting position.
cues: Keep hips level and square throughout the hinge.
Maintain a neutral spine from head to tailbone.
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding your lower back; stop if balance is lost and reset.

ball-glute-bridge-385.description: A stability ball glute bridge performed lying on your back to strengthen the hamstrings through controlled hip extension.
steps: Lie on your back with knees bent and heels resting on the stability ball.
Engage your core and press through your heels to lift your hips off the floor.
Squeeze your hamstrings and glutes at the top for a brief pause.
Slowly lower your hips back to the starting position on the floor.
cues: Drive through your heels
Keep core braced to avoid lower back arch
safetyNotes: Do not let your hips sag or twist during the lift to protect your back.

smith-machine-squat-387.description: The Smith machine squat is a guided barbell squat that targets the quadriceps with added stability for beginners.
steps: Set the bar at shoulder height and stand with feet shoulder-width apart beneath it.
Position the bar across your upper back, grip it, and rotate the handles to unrack.
Bend knees and hips to lower until thighs are parallel to the floor.
Drive through heels to stand up fully, then rotate handles to rerack the bar.
cues: Keep chest up and core braced
Drive through heels with knees tracking over toes
safetyNotes: Do not let knees cave inward; keep them aligned with toes throughout.

machine-leg-press-390.description: The machine leg press targets the quadriceps by pushing a weighted platform away while seated in the gym.
steps: Sit with your back flat against the pad and feet shoulder-width on the platform.
Release the safety handles and hold them lightly for support.
Push the platform away by straightening your legs without locking the knees.
Slowly bend your knees to lower the platform back to the start position.
cues: Drive through your heels
Keep knees aligned with toes
safetyNotes: Avoid locking your knees at the top to protect the joints.

smith-machine-lunge-388.description: Smith machine lunge strengthens quadriceps using a guided barbell during a forward or reverse stepping motion.
steps: Position the Smith bar at shoulder height across your upper back and unrack it.
Step one foot forward into a split stance with feet hip-width apart.
Bend both knees to lower until your front thigh is parallel to the floor.
Drive through the front heel to return to the starting standing position.
cues: Keep torso upright and ribs stacked over hips
Track front knee over ankle without letting it cave inward
safetyNotes: Avoid letting back knee slam into floor to reduce joint impact risk.

hack-squat-machine-389.description: Machine-based squat variation that primarily targets the quadriceps with a guided range of motion.
steps: Position feet shoulder-width on the platform and rest shoulders under the padded supports.
Unlock the safety handles and lower your body by bending knees and hips.
Descend until thighs are parallel to the platform or to a comfortable depth.
Drive through heels to extend legs and return to start, then re-lock the handles.
cues: Keep lower back pressed firmly against the back pad
Drive through heels while keeping knees aligned with toes
safetyNotes: Never lock out knees at the top to protect your joints.

machine-leg-extension-393.description: The machine leg extension is a seated exercise that isolates and strengthens the quadriceps.
steps: Sit on the machine with your back against the pad and adjust the leg roller so it rests just above your ankles.
Grip the handles and keep your upper body stable.
Extend your knees to lift the weight until your legs are straight.
Slowly bend your knees to lower the weight back to the starting position.
cues: Keep your back flat against the pad throughout the movement.
Control the lowering phase without swinging your legs.
safetyNotes: Avoid locking your knees at the top to reduce joint stress.

barbell-reverse-deficit-lunge-392.description: Barbell reverse deficit lunge strengthens quads by stepping backward from an elevated platform into a deep lunge.
steps: Stand on a low platform with a barbell across your upper back and feet together.
Step one foot backward off the platform and lower until your back knee nearly touches the floor.
Drive through your front heel to push back up to the starting position on the platform.
Repeat on the opposite side for the desired reps.
cues: Keep chest up and core braced
Drive through the front heel
safetyNotes: Maintain balance and avoid letting the front knee collapse inward.

goblet-squats-heel-elevated-squats-396.description: Goblet squat with heels elevated on a plate to target quads while holding a weight at chest level.
steps: Hold a dumbbell or kettlebell at chest height with elbows tucked and stand with heels on a stable elevated surface.
Brace your core, then bend hips and knees to lower until thighs are at least parallel to the floor.
Drive through midfoot to stand back up, keeping chest tall and knees tracking over toes.
Return to the tall starting position with full hip and knee extension.
cues: Keep chest lifted and elbows in
Push knees outward in line with toes
safetyNotes: Use a stable surface under your heels to prevent slipping or rolling.

single-leg-press-391.description: A machine exercise where one leg presses a weighted platform to target the quadriceps.
steps: Sit in the leg press machine with your back flat against the pad and one foot centered on the platform.
