Position the other leg to the side for stability and adjust the seat so your knee starts at about 90 degrees.
Push the platform away by extending your working leg until it is nearly straight.
Slowly bend the knee to lower the platform back to the starting position in a controlled motion.
cues: Brace your core to keep your lower back pressed into the seat.
Drive through your heel while keeping the knee aligned with the toes.
safetyNotes: Avoid locking the knee at the top to reduce joint stress.

dumbbell-step-ups-399.description: Dumbbell step-ups build quad and glute strength by stepping onto a raised platform while holding weights at your sides.
steps: Stand facing a sturdy step or bench while holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
Place one full foot flat on the step and drive through the heel to lift your body up.
Bring the trailing foot onto the step so both feet are on the platform.
Step the trailing foot back down first, then follow with the lead foot to return to the start position.
cues: Drive through the heel of the lead foot
Keep your chest tall and core braced
safetyNotes: Use a stable platform that will not move or tip during the exercise.

dumbbell-rear-lunges-398.description: Dumbbell rear lunges target the quadriceps by stepping backward into a lunge while holding weights.
steps: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart, holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
Step one foot backward and lower until both knees reach about 90-degree angles.
Drive through the front heel to push back up to standing.
Bring the back foot forward to return to the starting position.
cues: Keep your torso upright and chest lifted
Drive through the front heel to stand
safetyNotes: Keep your front knee aligned over the ankle to avoid joint stress.

barbell-curtsy-lunge-397.description: The barbell curtsy lunge targets the quadriceps through a crossed-leg reverse lunge while holding a barbell on the upper back.
steps: Position the barbell on your upper back and stand with feet hip-width apart.
Step one leg back and across behind the front leg into a curtsy stance.
Lower until both knees bend and the front thigh is nearly parallel to the floor.
Drive through the front heel to return to the starting position.
cues: Keep torso upright and chest lifted
Front knee tracks over the ankle
safetyNotes: Avoid letting the back knee slam into the floor to protect the joints.

barbell-deficit-curtsy-lunge-395.description: Barbell curtsy lunge from an elevated stance that deepens range of motion to target the quads.
steps: Place barbell on upper back and stand on a low platform with feet together.
Step one foot back and across behind the other, lowering until back knee drops below platform.
Drive through front heel to return to standing position.
Repeat on opposite side or complete prescribed reps.
cues: Keep torso upright and core braced
Front knee tracks over ankle without caving inward
safetyNotes: Control the descent to protect knees; avoid letting front knee collapse inward.

weighted-squat-402.description: A weighted squat builds leg strength by holding weights while squatting down and standing up, primarily targeting the quadriceps.
steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, holding weights at your sides or chest.
Brace your core, then bend knees and hips to lower until thighs are parallel to the floor.
Drive through your heels to stand back up to the starting position.
Repeat for the target number of reps.
cues: Keep chest up and back straight
Knees track over toes without caving in
safetyNotes: Maintain a neutral spine to avoid rounding the lower back.

bulgarian-split-squat-quad-focused-400.description: A rear-foot elevated split squat that builds quad strength through unilateral leg movement.
steps: Stand with your back to a knee-height bench and rest the top of one foot on it behind you.
Step your front foot forward until your front knee is above your ankle.
Lower your body by bending the front knee until your thigh is roughly parallel to the floor.
Push through your front heel to straighten the leg and return to the start position.
cues: Keep torso upright and ribs stacked over hips
Drive front knee forward over toes
safetyNotes: Use a stable bench to avoid slipping or losing balance.

staticdumbbell-lunges-403.description: Static dumbbell lunges strengthen the quadriceps by holding a split stance with weights and performing controlled knee bends.
steps: Stand with feet together holding dumbbells at your sides.
Step one foot forward into a split stance with feet flat.
Bend both knees to lower until front thigh is roughly parallel to the floor.
Drive through the front heel to straighten both legs and return to the split stance.
cues: Keep torso upright and ribs stacked over hips
Front knee tracks over ankle without caving inward
safetyNotes: Stop if you feel knee pain and reset your stance for better alignment.

wall-sit-with-dumbbell-curl-and-press-pl.description: Wall sit holding dumbbells while doing curls to overhead press and thigh adductions, targeting quads with arm work.
steps: Stand with back flat against a wall, feet shoulder-width apart, and a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
Slide down into a wall sit with thighs parallel to the floor and knees bent at 90 degrees.
Curl the dumbbells to your shoulders then press them straight overhead.
Squeeze knees together for adduction, hold the position for the set duration, then return to standing.
cues: Keep lower back pressed into the wall
Exhale as you press overhead
safetyNotes: Stop if knees feel stressed and avoid dropping below parallel thigh position.

machine-shoulder-press-narrow-grip-404.description: Seated machine exercise using a narrow grip to press handles overhead and target the shoulders.
steps: Adjust the seat height so the handles align with your shoulders and sit with your back flat against the pad.
Grip the handles with a narrow overhand grip and keep your feet flat on the floor.
Press the handles upward in a controlled motion until your arms are fully extended overhead.
Slowly lower the handles back to the starting position at shoulder level.
cues: Keep your core braced and ribs stacked over hips.
Press straight up without letting elbows flare outward.
safetyNotes: Avoid locking elbows at the top to reduce joint stress.

squat-press-407.description: A squat press combines a bodyweight squat with an overhead arm press, targeting shoulders and legs.
steps: Stand tall with feet shoulder-width apart and hands near your shoulders.
Bend knees and hips to lower into a squat until thighs are parallel to the floor.
Drive through heels to stand while extending arms straight overhead.
Lower arms to shoulders and repeat the full movement.
cues: Keep chest up and core braced
Press arms fully overhead at the top
safetyNotes: Avoid knees caving inward on the squat
Do not arch lower back when pressing

bent-over-dumbbell-reverse-fly-406.description: Bent-over dumbbell reverse fly targets rear shoulders by lifting weights outward while hinged at the hips.
steps: Hold a dumbbell in each hand and stand with feet hip-width apart.
Hinge at the hips until torso is nearly parallel to the floor with a flat back.
With slight elbow bend, raise dumbbells out to sides until arms reach shoulder height.
Slowly lower the dumbbells back to the starting position.
cues: Keep back flat and core braced
Lead with elbows and squeeze shoulder blades at top
safetyNotes: Avoid rounding the lower back or using momentum to prevent strain.

barbell-upright-row-409.description: Barbell upright row targets shoulders and upper back by pulling a barbell from waist to chest height while standing.
steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, gripping the barbell overhand with hands slightly narrower than shoulders at thigh level.
Pull the bar straight up toward your chin by driving elbows high and out to the sides.
Pause briefly when the bar reaches upper chest height.
Lower the bar slowly back to the starting position at your thighs.
cues: Lead with elbows higher than wrists
Keep the bar close to your body
safetyNotes: Use a grip at least shoulder-width to reduce shoulder impingement risk
Avoid using momentum or swinging the weight

reverse-fly-machine-405.description: The reverse fly machine targets rear shoulders by pulling arms backward while seated against a pad.
steps: Sit with chest against the pad, feet flat on the floor, and adjust seat height if needed.
Grasp the handles with a neutral grip and slight elbow bend.
Pull handles back by squeezing shoulder blades together until arms are in line with torso.
Slowly return handles to the start position with control.
cues: Squeeze shoulder blades together at the end of each rep.
Keep chest pressed into the pad and torso stable.
safetyNotes: Avoid swinging or using momentum to prevent shoulder strain.

single-are-cable-reverse-fly-408.description: Single-arm cable reverse fly isolates the rear deltoids by pulling a cable outward and back from across the body.
steps: Attach a handle to the cable machine at chest height and stand sideways with feet shoulder-width apart.
Grab the handle with the hand farthest from the machine and extend your arm across your body.
Pull the cable outward and backward in an arc until your arm reaches shoulder height.
Pause briefly at the top then slowly return the handle to the starting position across your body.
cues: Lead with your elbow and squeeze the rear shoulder at the end of the pull.
Keep your torso still and avoid using momentum to swing the weight.
safetyNotes: Control the movement throughout to avoid shoulder strain from swinging or using too much weight.

cable-bar-upright-row-411.description: An upright row on a cable machine using a bar attachment that pulls upward to target the shoulders.
steps: Stand facing the cable machine with a straight bar on the low pulley, feet shoulder-width apart and overhand grip at shoulder width.
Pull the bar upward to chin height while driving elbows high and outward.
Pause at the top then lower the bar slowly back to the hips in a controlled motion.
cues: Lead with elbows high and out
Keep torso upright and core braced
safetyNotes: Avoid jerking or raising above chin height to limit shoulder impingement risk.

smith-machine-shrug-410.description: Smith machine shrug targets the upper trapezius by elevating the shoulders against the fixed bar path.
steps: Stand centered in the Smith machine with feet shoulder-width apart and grip the bar overhand slightly wider than shoulders at mid-thigh height.
Unrack the bar by standing tall with arms fully straight and extended.
Shrug shoulders straight up toward your ears, squeezing at the top.
Lower shoulders slowly back to start and rerack the bar on the safety stops.
cues: Keep arms straight with no elbow bend
Shrug straight up without rolling shoulders
safetyNotes: Avoid using momentum or bouncing the bar
Keep core braced to maintain neutral spine

dumbbell-upright-row-412.description: The dumbbell upright row targets the shoulders by lifting weights from hips to chest level while standing.
steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart holding dumbbells in front of thighs palms facing you.
Pull the dumbbells upward close to your body leading with your elbows.
Raise until elbows reach shoulder height and weights are near your chest.
Lower the dumbbells slowly back to the starting position.
cues: Lead with elbows higher than the weights
Keep core braced and avoid swinging
safetyNotes: Avoid lifting elbows above shoulder height to reduce shoulder impingement risk.

barbell-skullcrusher-415.description: Barbell skullcrusher is a lying triceps extension that isolates the triceps using a barbell.
steps: Lie on a flat bench holding a barbell with overhand grip, arms extended above chest.
Keep upper arms still and bend elbows to lower the bar toward your forehead.
Extend elbows to press the bar back to the start position.
Repeat for reps then rack the bar safely.
cues: Keep elbows tucked and pointed toward ceiling
Lower with control and avoid locking elbows hard
safetyNotes: Use a spotter with heavier loads to prevent the bar from dropping toward your face

overhead-tricep-barbell-extensions-414.description: Overhead barbell tricep extensions isolate and strengthen the triceps by extending the arms from a bent overhead position.
steps: Grip the barbell with both hands and press it overhead until arms are fully extended.
Keep upper arms stationary and bend elbows to lower the barbell behind your head.
Straighten elbows to raise the barbell back to the overhead starting position.
Control the descent and repeat the movement for the desired reps.
cues: Keep elbows tucked close to your head throughout
Brace your core to stay tall and avoid arching
safetyNotes: Lower the barbell slowly to reduce elbow joint stress

overhead-tricep-dumbbell-extensions-413.description: An overhead dumbbell extension that isolates the triceps through controlled elbow bending and straightening.
steps: Hold a dumbbell with both hands and raise your arms straight overhead.
Bend only at the elbows to lower the dumbbell behind your head.
Keep upper arms still and extend elbows to raise the dumbbell overhead.
Return to the starting position with arms fully extended.
cues: Keep elbows close to your head and pointing forward.
Move only at the elbows without shifting your shoulders.
safetyNotes: Avoid locking elbows at the top to protect the joints.
Keep core tight to prevent lower back arching.

cable-single-arm-tricep-kickback-416.description: Cable single-arm tricep kickback isolates the triceps by extending the arm backward from a hinged position.
steps: Attach a handle to the low pulley and grasp it with one hand.
Hinge forward at the hips keeping your upper arm pinned to your side.
Extend your elbow to kick the handle back until the arm is straight.
Slowly bend the elbow to return the handle to the start position.
cues: Keep upper arm stationary against your torso
Squeeze tricep at full extension
safetyNotes: Maintain a flat back to avoid lower back strain

tricep-dips-machine-418.description: The tricep dip machine strengthens the triceps through a seated, controlled pushing motion with adjustable resistance.
steps: Adjust the seat so your feet rest flat and handles align near shoulder height.
Sit tall with back supported against the pad and grip the handles firmly.
Push the handles downward by straightening your elbows until arms are fully extended.
Bend elbows slowly to return to the start position with control.
cues: Keep elbows tucked close to your sides
Control the lowering phase without swinging
safetyNotes: Avoid locking elbows at full extension to protect joints
Stop immediately if shoulder discomfort occurs

cable-bar-push-down-419.description: Cable bar pushdown is a triceps isolation move using a straight bar on a high cable pulley.
steps: Attach a straight bar to the high pulley and grip it palms-down at shoulder width.
Stand facing the machine with elbows tucked at your sides and upper arms vertical.
Push the bar down by straightening your elbows until arms are fully extended.
Slowly bend elbows to return the bar to the start position with control.
cues: Keep elbows pinned to your sides
Squeeze triceps at the bottom
safetyNotes: Avoid using momentum or swinging the body.

cable-bar-tricep-pull-down-grip-change-4.description: Cable machine tricep pushdown using a bar attachment with grip changes to target the triceps.
steps: Attach a straight bar to the high pulley and stand facing the machine with feet shoulder-width.
Grip the bar overhand at shoulder width with elbows tucked at your sides.
Push the bar down by extending your elbows fully, then switch to underhand grip and repeat.
Slowly bend elbows to return the bar to the starting position with control.
cues: Keep upper arms locked at sides throughout the movement.
Squeeze triceps hard at full extension before returning.
safetyNotes: Avoid swinging the body or using momentum to complete reps.

cable-rope-pull-downs-420.description: Cable rope pull downs target the triceps by extending the elbows to pull a rope down from a high cable pulley.
steps: Attach rope to high pulley and grip ends with palms facing each other, elbows tucked at sides.
Stand facing machine with feet shoulder-width and slight knee bend.
Extend arms down by straightening elbows until hands reach thigh level.
Slowly bend elbows to return rope to starting position.
cues: Keep elbows pinned to sides throughout.
Squeeze triceps at full arm extension.
safetyNotes: Avoid using momentum or leaning back to prevent shoulder strain.

hip-bridges-optional-423.description: Hip bridges warm up the lower body by activating the glutes and hamstrings.
steps: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor hip-width apart.
Press through your heels to lift your hips toward the ceiling.
Squeeze your glutes at the top and hold briefly.
Slowly lower your hips back down to the starting position.
cues: Squeeze glutes firmly at the top
Keep core engaged throughout
safetyNotes: Avoid overarching your lower back.

deadbug-extensions-optional-425.description: Deadbug extensions warm up the lower core by extending opposite arm and leg while lying on your back to build stability.
steps: Lie on your back with knees bent at 90 degrees and arms extended toward the ceiling.
Brace your core to press your lower back flat into the floor.
Slowly extend one arm overhead and the opposite leg straight out.
Return to the start position with control and repeat on the other side.
cues: Keep lower back pressed into the floor
Move slowly and control the extension
safetyNotes: Avoid arching your back off the floor

deadbug-extensions-optional-424.description: Deadbug extensions build core stability by extending opposite arm and leg while lying on your back.
steps: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat, arms extended toward the ceiling.
Press your lower back into the floor and engage your core.
Slowly extend one arm overhead and the opposite leg straight out.
Return to the start position and repeat on the other side.
cues: Keep lower back pressed into the floor
Move slowly with control
safetyNotes: Avoid arching your lower back off the floor.

hip-bridges-optional-426.description: A bodyweight glute bridge that activates the glutes and hamstrings from a supine position.
steps: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor hip-width apart.
Rest arms at your sides with palms facing down.
Press through your heels to lift hips toward the ceiling.
Squeeze glutes at the top then lower hips back to the floor.
cues: Drive through your heels
Keep ribs stacked over hips
safetyNotes: Avoid arching your lower back excessively at the top position.

dumbbell-skullcrusher-422.description: Dumbbell skullcrusher is a triceps isolation exercise done lying on a bench by lowering and extending dumbbells toward the forehead.
steps: Lie on your back on a bench holding a dumbbell in each hand with arms extended above your chest.
Keep upper arms still and bend elbows to lower the dumbbells toward your forehead.
Straighten elbows to press the dumbbells back to the starting position above your chest.
Control the full range and repeat for reps before returning weights safely to the floor.
cues: Keep upper arms stationary and elbows tucked.
Move only at the elbows with a controlled tempo.
safetyNotes: Start light to avoid losing control and hitting your face.

tricep-push-ups-421.description: Tricep push-ups target the triceps using a narrow hand position during a standard push-up movement.
steps: Start in a high plank with hands close together under your chest, fingers forward, and body straight.
Lower your chest toward the floor by bending elbows while keeping them tucked close to your sides.
Push through your palms to straighten your arms and return to the high plank position.
Repeat for the desired number of reps while maintaining control.
cues: Keep elbows tucked to your sides
Maintain a straight line from head to heels
safetyNotes: Avoid flaring elbows or sagging hips to prevent shoulder or lower back strain.

walking-dumbbell-lunges-394.description: Walking dumbbell lunges target the quadriceps through alternating forward steps and knee bends while holding weights.
steps: Stand tall holding a dumbbell in each hand at your sides.
Step forward with one leg and lower until both knees reach about 90 degrees.
Drive through the front heel to bring the back leg forward into the next lunge.
Continue alternating legs while walking forward for the desired reps.
cues: Keep your torso upright and core braced.
Track your front knee over your midfoot.
safetyNotes: Avoid letting your front knee cave inward to reduce joint stress.

bird-dog-optional-427.description: Bird dog is a bodyweight move on all fours that strengthens core, back, and glutes while building balance.
steps: Start on hands and knees with wrists under shoulders and knees under hips, back flat.
Extend one arm straight forward and the opposite leg straight backward.
Hold briefly with hips level and core braced, then return to start.
Repeat on the other side.
cues: Keep your back flat and avoid twisting at the hips.
Move slowly and control the reach.
safetyNotes: Do not let your lower back arch or sag during the hold.

muscle-groups-433.description: The crunch targets the abdominal muscles through a controlled upper-body lift from a supine position.
steps: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor, hip-width apart.
Place hands lightly behind your head or crossed over your chest.
Engage your core and lift your shoulders a few inches off the ground.
Slowly lower your upper body back to the starting position.
cues: Keep lower back pressed into the floor
Exhale on the lift and inhale on the way down
safetyNotes: Avoid pulling on your neck with your hands

quadriceps-435.description: Bodyweight squats strengthen the quadriceps through repeated knee and hip bends while engaging the core.
steps: Stand tall with feet shoulder-width apart and toes pointed slightly outward.
Bend at the hips and knees to lower your body toward the floor.
Descend until your thighs are roughly parallel to the ground.
Drive through your heels to push back up to the starting position.
cues: Keep chest lifted and core braced.
Track knees over toes without letting them cave inward.
safetyNotes: Stop if you feel sharp knee pain and reset your stance.

bird-dog-optional-432.description: The bird dog builds core stability and strengthens the back, abs and glutes from a hands-and-knees position.
steps: Start on all fours with hands under shoulders and knees under hips, back flat.
Extend one arm forward and the opposite leg backward while keeping hips level.
Hold briefly at full extension, then return to the starting position.
Repeat on the opposite side.
cues: Keep your spine neutral and hips square
Move slowly with control
safetyNotes: Avoid arching the lower back or rotating the hips.

inchworms-optional-431.description: Inchworms are a dynamic warm-up that stretches the hamstrings while engaging the core and upper body through a flowing plank motion.
steps: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart and arms at your sides.
Hinge at the hips and place hands flat on the floor in front of your feet.
Walk hands forward one at a time until you reach a straight high plank.
Walk feet forward one at a time to meet your hands, then stand up tall.
cues: Keep core braced to avoid sagging in the plank.
Take small controlled steps with hands and feet.
safetyNotes: Stop if you feel sharp pain in the lower back or wrists.

inchworms-optional-428.description: Inchworms are a dynamic warm-up that targets the core, shoulders and hamstrings by moving from standing into plank and back.
steps: Stand with feet hip-width apart and hinge at the hips to place hands on the floor in front of you.
Walk hands forward until you reach a straight high-plank position.
Walk hands back toward your feet while keeping legs as straight as possible.
Roll up through the spine to return to standing.
cues: Keep core braced so hips stay level in plank
Avoid rounding your lower back as you walk hands out
safetyNotes: Stop if you feel sharp pain in the lower back or hamstrings

chest-434.description: Push-ups build chest strength while engaging the core for full-body stability.
steps: Start in a high plank with hands slightly wider than shoulders and body straight.
Lower your chest toward the floor by bending elbows to about 45 degrees.
Press through your palms to return to the high plank position.
Repeat the movement for the target reps while keeping form tight.
cues: Keep core braced so hips stay level with shoulders
Lower with control and avoid flaring elbows outward
safetyNotes: Do not let your lower back sag or arch to prevent strain on the spine.

calves-438.description: Calf raises build lower leg strength by lifting heels to target the calves.
steps: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart and hold a support if needed for balance.
Rise onto the balls of your feet by lifting your heels as high as possible.
Pause briefly at the top of the movement.
Lower your heels slowly back to the floor to return to the start.
cues: Keep your core engaged for stability
Move through a full range without bouncing
safetyNotes: Avoid locking your knees or using momentum to reduce joint strain.

glutes-437.description: Glute bridge strengthens glutes and core abdominals by lifting hips from a lying position.
steps: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor hip-width apart.
Engage your core and press your lower back into the floor.
Lift your hips toward the ceiling by squeezing your glutes until your body forms a straight line.
Pause briefly then lower your hips slowly back to the start.
cues: Drive through your heels
Keep ribs stacked over hips
safetyNotes: Avoid overarching the lower back by keeping the core braced.

snow-angels-optional-430.description: Snow angels warm up the shoulders and upper back by sweeping arms overhead while lying on the floor.
steps: Lie on your back on the floor with arms at your sides and palms facing up.
Sweep arms out to the sides and overhead while keeping them in contact with the floor.
Reverse the motion to bring arms back down to your sides.
cues: Keep lower back pressed into the floor
Move arms slowly without lifting off the ground
safetyNotes: Prevent your lower back from arching as arms move overhead.

biceps-440.description: Bicep curls build upper arm strength by flexing the elbow to lift weight toward the shoulders.
steps: Stand with feet hip-width apart holding weights at your sides palms facing forward.
Keep elbows close to your torso and curl the weights up toward your shoulders.
Squeeze your biceps briefly at the top then lower the weights with control.
Return to the starting position with arms fully extended.
cues: Keep upper arms stationary against your sides
Move only at the elbows
safetyNotes: Avoid swinging or using momentum from your body

back-439.description: Back is a beginner core exercise performed lying down that targets the abdominals for stability and strength.
steps: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor hip-width apart.
Place hands lightly behind your head or cross arms over your chest.
Engage your core and lift shoulders a few inches off the floor.
Lower back down slowly with control to the starting position.
cues: Keep lower back pressed into the floor
Exhale as you lift and inhale as you lower
safetyNotes: Avoid pulling on your neck with your hands.

shoulder-taps-429.description: Shoulder taps are a plank variation that builds core stability and shoulder strength through alternating hand taps.
steps: Start in a high plank with hands under shoulders, feet hip-width, and body in a straight line.
Lift one hand and tap the opposite shoulder while keeping hips level.
Return the hand to the floor under your shoulder.
Alternate sides and repeat for the target reps.
cues: Brace your core to stop hips from rocking.
Keep your body straight from head to heels.
safetyNotes: Avoid letting hips twist or drop to prevent lower back strain.

shoulders-443.description: Shoulder taps from plank position build core stability while engaging shoulders and preventing hip sway.
steps: Start in high plank with hands under shoulders and body straight from head to heels.
Lift one hand to tap the opposite shoulder while bracing core to stay stable.
Return hand to floor and repeat tap on the other side.
Alternate sides for the target reps then lower to knees to finish.
cues: Brace core to keep hips level
Move slowly without rocking
safetyNotes: Stop if lower back sags or hips twist to avoid strain.

core-abdominals-442.description: The abdominal crunch targets the core muscles by curling the upper body toward the knees.
steps: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor, hands lightly behind your head.
Engage your core and lift your shoulders off the ground while exhaling.
Pause briefly at the top, then lower your upper body back down with control.
Return to the starting position with your shoulders on the floor.
cues: Keep your lower back pressed into the floor
Avoid pulling on your neck
Focus on using your abs to lift
safetyNotes: Avoid straining your neck by pulling with your hands.
Stop if you experience lower back pain.

hamstrings-436.description: Hamstring exercise strengthens the back of the thighs through controlled knee bends using bodyweight.
steps: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart and hands on hips for balance.
Shift weight onto one leg while keeping a slight bend in that knee.
Bend the opposite knee to lift your heel toward your glutes.
Slowly lower the foot back to the floor with control.
Switch legs and repeat for the desired reps.
cues: Keep hips square and core braced
Squeeze the hamstring at the top
safetyNotes: Avoid arching the lower back or leaning forward

triceps-441.description: This triceps exercise strengthens the back of the upper arms while engaging the core abdominals for stability.
steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart and arms extended overhead.
Bend elbows to lower hands behind the head while keeping upper arms still.
Straighten arms to press back to the starting position.
Lower arms and rest after completing the set.
cues: Keep elbows pointed forward and close to head
Brace core to maintain balance
safetyNotes: Avoid letting elbows flare outward to reduce shoulder strain.

circuit-cardio-447.description: A beginner circuit of dynamic cardio moves that engage and strengthen the core abdominals through controlled reps.
steps: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart and arms at sides.
Perform 10 jumping jacks by jumping feet wide and raising arms overhead.
Do 10 high knees by driving alternate knees toward chest at a brisk pace.
Return to standing position with feet together and arms relaxed.
cues: Brace core throughout to stabilize the torso.
Land softly on the balls of your feet.
safetyNotes: Avoid arching the lower back during jumps to protect the spine.

circuit-pull-449.description: The Circuit Pull is a core exercise that targets the abdominals through controlled pulling motions for stability.
steps: Stand with feet hip-width apart holding a cable or band at chest height.
Brace your core and pull the handle straight toward your torso.
Control the return to the starting position without twisting.
Repeat the movement for the target number of reps.
cues: Keep ribs stacked over hips
Pull with control using your core
safetyNotes: Avoid arching the lower back or using momentum to prevent strain.

warm-up-upper-445.description: Dynamic upper body warm-up that activates core abdominals through controlled arm and torso movements.
steps: Stand tall with feet shoulder-width apart and arms relaxed at sides.
Raise arms to shoulder height while bracing your core.
Gently rotate torso side to side, keeping hips stable.
Lower arms slowly and return to the starting stance.
cues: Brace core as if expecting a light punch
Move slowly without momentum or bouncing
safetyNotes: Avoid twisting beyond comfortable range to protect the spine

circuit-push-448.description: Circuit Push engages the core abdominals via controlled forward pushing motions in a circuit format.
steps: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, core braced, and hands at chest height.
Extend both arms forward in a smooth push while keeping torso stable.
Pause briefly at full arm extension without locking elbows.
Return hands to chest and reset posture before the next rep.
cues: Brace core to prevent any sway or twist
Move slowly and control the return
safetyNotes: Avoid arching the lower back or using momentum.

circuit-lower-450.description: Beginner circuit of lower body moves that engage core abdominals for stability and endurance.
steps: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart and core braced.
Perform squats by bending knees and sitting hips back.
Lie on back for glute bridges, lifting hips toward ceiling.
Return to standing and repeat the sequence for reps.
cues: Keep ribs stacked over hips
Breathe steadily and brace core
safetyNotes: Avoid overarching the lower back during bridges

warm-up-lower-446.description: Dynamic warm-up that activates lower body mobility while engaging core abdominals for stability.
steps: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart and arms relaxed at sides.
Lift one knee toward chest while bracing core and keeping torso upright.
Lower foot to floor and switch to opposite leg in a marching rhythm.
Continue alternating legs for reps then return to standing with both feet flat.
cues: Brace core on each lift
Keep spine neutral and tall
safetyNotes: Avoid leaning backward to prevent lower back strain.

trapezius-444.description: Shoulder shrugs build trapezius strength in the upper back and improve posture for beginners.
steps: Stand tall with feet hip-width apart and arms at your sides.
Raise shoulders straight toward ears while keeping arms relaxed.
Pause briefly at the top of the movement.
Lower shoulders slowly back to the starting position.
cues: Keep neck neutral and avoid rolling shoulders.
Lift using upper back muscles only.
safetyNotes: Avoid using momentum which can strain the neck.

side-plank-292.description: The side plank builds core strength by holding a straight side-lying position that targets the abdominals and obliques.
steps: Lie on your side with your forearm on the ground and elbow directly under your shoulder.
Stack your feet and lift your hips until your body forms a straight line from head to heels.
Hold the position while keeping your core tight for the desired duration.
Lower your hips slowly back to the floor to return to the start.
cues: Keep your body straight without letting hips sag
Press through your forearm to stay stable
safetyNotes: Avoid dropping your hips, which can reduce effectiveness and strain the lower back.

calf-press-leg-press-machine-341.description: The calf press on the leg press machine targets the calves by raising and lowering the heels against resistance.
steps: Sit on the leg press machine and place the balls of your feet on the lower edge of the platform with heels hanging off.
Unlock the safety handles and extend your legs until straight without locking the knees.
Lower the platform by dropping your heels down as far as comfortable.
Press through the balls of your feet to raise the platform by pointing your toes.
Return to the start position and repeat for reps before locking the safety handles.
cues: Keep legs straight throughout the movement
Control the lowering phase without bouncing
safetyNotes: Avoid locking your knees at the top to reduce joint stress.
